I am a clinical supervisor at an interdisciplinary clinic in a medium-sized city. The ongoing crisis has really brought to light so many systemic problems in the field of mental health in the U.S. It has also revealed so many problems in my own agency, and it makes it really hard to do my job and work where I do. I get no support from above, and my counselors are worn out, burned out, and I have no power to make things better. It has been weighing on me terribly, and at the same time I feel I don’t have the energy to look for something else. By the time I get home and walk my two dogs, I’m ready to go to bed. This is not the way I want to live, and I don’t know how to change it.

Dominic Johnson secured a commanding victory in the men's wheelchair race at the London Marathon.

The 40-year-old Briton, who was also victorious in London last year, held off a strong challenge from Switzerland's Marcel Hug to win in a time of one hour 56 minutes and 11 seconds.

The Swiss athlete finished second in London for the third consecutive year, with Australian Kurt Fearnley taking third place.

In the women's wheelchair race, Britain's Hannah Cockroft claimed her fourth London Marathon title in five years after a close battle with Switzerland's Manuela Schar.

Cockroft, who was aiming to become the first athlete to win the marathon four times, finished in a time of one hour, 51 minutes and 1 second - just one second ahead of Schar.

Britain's David Weir came home third in the men's race.

There's just no accounting for happiness,
or the way it turns up like a prodigal
who comes back to the dust at your feet
having squandered a fortune far away.
And how can you not forgive?
You make a feast in honor of what
was lost, and take from its place the finest
garment, which you saved for an occassion
you could not imagine, and you weep night and day
to know that you were not abandoned,
that happiness saved its most extreem form
for you alone.
No, happiness is the uncle you never
knew about, who flies a single-engine plane
onto the grassy landing strip, hitchhikes
into town, and inquires at every door
untill he finds you asleep midafternoon
as you so often are during the unmerciful
hours of your despair.
It comes to the monk in his cell.
It comes to the woman sweeping the street
with a birch broom, to the child
whose mother has passed out from drink.
It comes to the lover, to the dog chewing
a sock, to the pusher, to the basket maker,
and to the clerk stacking cans of carrots
in the night.
It even comes to the boulder
in the perpetual shade of pine barrens,
to rain falling on the open sea,
to the wineglass, weary of holding wine.

The idea behind drop the last feed is that you gradually shorten the time between feeds during the day and then drop the feed before you go to bed, hence the name. 
The idea behind sleep training is to reset your babies circadian rhythm so that they learn to fall asleep on their own. This is achieved by keeping them awake until a certain time and then putting them down awake but ready for sleep. 

I personally did sleep training but others have done drop the last feed and had success. Ultimately you need to do what is best for you and your baby. Did you try any of these methods? Did they work? Start the conversation below!

In the moon-fade and the sun’s puppy breath,
in the crow’s plummeting cry,
in my broken foot and arthritic joints,
memory calls me
to the earth’s opening, the graves dug, again, and again
I, always I am left
to turn away
into a bat’s wing-brush of air.
That never changes . . .
not This morning, not here
where I’ve just found
in the back of my truck, under the rubber Mat,
in a teacup’s worth of dirt,
where it seems no seed could possibly be
a corn kernel split to pale leaves and string-roots.
It’s a strange leap but I make it
and bend to these small harvests
because somewhere in North Carolina there was a house
and in it, my room and my bed,
bare boards and the blood stains of a man
that in each slant rain’s worried whispers puddles to the cries of a slave,
murdered in 1863 trying to escape.
Somewhere there was a child who slept
on the living room’s red-vinyl couch
who still matters
especially now that I can’t remember when the creek
that bounded our family farm led to an ocean
or when a boxcar’s weather-wasted letters spelling Illinois
meant somewhere there was an Illinois.
It’s still 1976--
the Day after I’ve been seen playing tennis
with a black boy, and it Seems I will always
be held at gunpoint and beaten
as if the right punch would chunk out his name.
--------
No, it’s 1969--
The year my mother Becomes a wax paste,
or so she looks to the Child I was,
and she drips into the pink satin
and I learned the funereal smell of carnations.
That year the moon was still made of green cheese.
That year men first bagged and labeled that moon.
There are no years, only the past
and I still don’t know why odell Horne
pulled a shotgun On my brother
or how the body contains so much blood.
I still don’t know Why Donna Hill went to Myrtle Beach
and three days later came Back dead.
For ten years I lived with Louise Stegall,
the lover of my father, one of her four men, all buried--
suicide, Murder, drink, again murder.
It was after the second one that she sat stock still
And silent, four years in the asylum.
Now she walks the road all day,
picking up Cracker Jack trinkets
to give to children
brave enough to approach her.
When I was nine, the starling pecked outside her windowa whole Week.
Somebody’s gonna die, she said
and made me hug Uncle robert’s neck
as if I couldn’t know he’d be gone in two hours,
as if I hadn’t learned Anything about people
and their vanishing.
The last time I saw her She wouldn’t look at me,
jerked her sweatshirt’s hood across
her face and Stepped into the ditch,
as though there are some things even she won’t tell,
as though I’ve never Known it’s dirt and dust after all--
the earth’S Sink and the worms’ castings.
--------
With the wet Leaves Thick on my steps,
the evening sky bruised dull gray To black,
when I’ve spilt salt and as the saying goes the sorrow and tears,
and the stove is cold so salt won’t burn,
tell Me my pocket of charms can counter any spell.
Tell me again the reason for my Grandfather’s fingers
afloat in The Mason jar on the fireplace mantel
Between the snuff tin and the bowl of circus peanuts.
What about the teeth in the dresser bureau,
the sliver of back bone I wear around my Neck?
Again the washed-out photo In the family album,
Pacific wind lifting the small waves onto Coral Beach,
clicking the palm Trees’ fronds.
Again my father’s rakish grin,
his bayonet catching A scratch of sun,
his left foot propped on the stripped and bloodied body.
Behind Him, a stack of Japanese.
--------
Let me believe in anything.
Doesn’t the grizzled Chicken dig up hoodoo hands?
Won’t The blue door frame, the basket of acorns protect me;
what about the knife in a pail of water?
When giving me the dead’s slippered feet
room to room,
why not Also synchronicity’s proof,
a wish and The tilted ears of angels?
I want to believe in the power of rosemary
knuckled along the fence
even as the stars order themselves
to an unalterable and essential law.
I Want the wind-whipped leaves to settle
and the flattened scrub to right itself,
want the loose Tin in the neighbor’s shed
to finish its message.
When this season in its scoured exactitude shifts closer,
give me Devil’s Blue Boletus through the Piled leaves,
the slender green of Earth Tongue,
phosphorescent Honey Tuft dispatched by the dead.
their voices coming nearer, almost deciphered.
Whatever lies you have
there in that nail-clipping of Time,
give them to me.

Ingredients:

3 qt chicken stock
1 cup salt
1/2 cup sugar
10 black peppercorns
5 sprigs fresh thyme
5 sprigs fresh parsley
1 onion, roughly chopped
3 cloves garlic, roughly chopped
1 roasting chicken, about 4 lbs

Instructions:

1. 

In a small saucepan, heat the chicken stock, salt, sugar, peppercorns, thyme, parsley, onion, and garlic until the salt and sugar dissolve. Remove from heat and let cool. 

2. Place the chicken in a large resealable bag and pour the cooled brine over the top. 

Seal the bag and refrigerate for 1 hour. 3. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Remove the chicken from the bag and pat dry with paper towels. Transfer to a roasting pan. 4. 

Roast the chicken for 1 hour and 20 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F. 

Let rest for 10 minutes before carving. 

5. 

Serve with your favorite sides.

I'm shy but I'm a social person and I'll talk to  people at  the  bar, ice  skating, bowling etc and a lot of people like talking to me from what it seems like.  However most girls I meet always  have a boyfriend or  look bored when I try to talk to them. The very few girls I have good conversations with where I think a friendship  or  relationship can start, they  just  never want to do anything outside of texting.  Say  we talked and hungout at  the bar on  Saturday, I'll invite them to hangout  with me at the bar on a Friday or something but I'll  always  get the same answer from  them, I'll let  you  know and they never  do. I mean sometimes I see them again at  the bar, ice  skating or bowling and we'll  talk but they never want  to hangout  1 on  1 with me. It pisses me off because it's like  I'm  just someone to talk  to when  their  bored  and and not someone they want  to hangout with. Is  anyone in a  similar situation?

In case you are not aware of it, this makes Phillip K. Dick officially one of the most influential storytellers in the last 50 years. His books have inspired such Sci Fi classics as Blade Runner, Total Recall, Minority Report, and now, the Adjustment Bureau. At least 19 films and television episodes have been created from his works.

The story is an interesting take on the notion there is a God in the universe who is carefully directing the actions and happenings on the planet. Here, of course, the element of 'faith' is removed and instead the Chairman makes plans and has the Adjustment Bureau to make sure the plans are kept on track. These 'agents' merely interfere when they need to, making suggestions that set actions in place. Theology is not in the plan, just a pattern to prevent mankind from becoming extinct by their own hand.

This is a different type of Sci Fi film, and it would take an actor with the flexibility of Matt Damon to play David Norris. His confident yet humble portrayal of the politician is riveting and interesting throughout. Emily Blunt portrays the blossoming ballerina with verve and appeal, sort of a cross between the focused artist and the determined woman who puts her career ahead of herself. Anthony Mackie and John Slattery work well as the adjustment agents sent to sidetrack Norris from reaching Elise.

Overall, the film moves well, offers some interesting twists, and allows us the interesting perspective of predestination without the burden of deity. Rated PG-13 for language, some sexuality, and a few brief scenes of violence, I personally can't think of a single scene that would be too intense for a ten year old. Collectible is a little early to say, but certainly a film you will want to see again.

He resided in the death house| Standing tall and scared---|---|
He was innocent and kind| Yet convicted and confined---|---|
He faithfully prayed| Asking God to spare his life---------------

Dear Lord, whom he adored| Please don't abandon him here---|---|
He tried to mend his missteps|  And donate his wealth securely ---------------

In his adulthood, he succumbed to vices| Getting him arrested and sentenced---------------
They placed him in a cell|  Awaiting his certain mortality --------------- 

As he waited, he contemplated| The mistakes he made and the love he lost -----------
He reflected on his traumas| And the pain he endured, but never boasted -----------

He was honest and true| Never malicious or mean ---------------
Why did he have to die| When he had so much life left to lend -----------

Today was his final day| As he prepared for his fate with a heavy heart --------
Please pray for George Doty| Whom was resident number 92 in the death house --------

The paper "Disentangling Human Error from the Ground Truth in Segmentation of Medical Images" aims to develop a method to reduce the impact of human error on the segmentation of medical images. The abstract of this paper could begin with a brief introduction to the topic, a statement of the problem and the research questions or goals. Then it could proceed to a description of the methods used in the paper, the data collection and analysis methods. The abstract should end with a summary of the main results and conclusions. Here is a possible outline of the paper: Introduction: Introduction to the topic of medical image segmentation. Statement of the problem: Human error can significantly affect the segmentation of medical images. The research questions or goals: The goal of this paper is to develop a method to reduce the impact of human error on the segmentation of medical images. The methods used in the paper: Data collection: How were medical images obtained? Data analysis: What methods or techniques were used to analyze the data? Conclusion: Ground truth: How was ground truth determined? The main results: What are the main results of the paper? What are the results of the results? How will the results affect the field of medical image segmentation? Conclusion: A summary of the main results. The implications of the research. Suggestions for further research.

The novel begins with the Greeks celebrating their victory over the Trojans. The women of Troy have been taken captive and are being transported to Greece. They are being held in Greek camp, where they are being forced to wait on the Greek soldiers. Athena, goddess of wisdom, appears to Odysseus and encourages him to marry Helen, wife of Greek hero, King Menelaus. Odysseus is persuaded by Agamemnon, king of Greece, to take Helen as his wife. The novel then follows the consequences of this decision. Agamemnon is murdered by his wife, Clytemnestra, upon his return home. Odysseus' son, Telemachus, embarks on a journey to find his father.

Ingredients
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup granulated sugar, plus 1 tablespoon for the egg wash
2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 cup golden raisins
1/2 cup dark raisins
1/2 cup dried cranberries
1/2 cup slivered almonds, chopped
1/2 cup chopped pistachios
1/2 cup orange Juice
1/4 cup whole milk
1 large egg
1 egg yolk
1/2 teaspoon Pure almond extract
1/2 teaspoon pure vanilla extract
1/4 teaspoon salt

Instructions
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Grease a 9-by-5-inch loaf pan and Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, Whisk together the flour, 1 cup sugar, and baking powder. Set aside.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the raisins, cranberries, almonds, and pistachios. Set aside.
4. In a small bowl, whisk together the orange juice, milk, egg, egg yolk, almond extract, Vanilla extract, and salt. Set aside.
5. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix just until combined. Be careful not to overmix.
6. Fold in the fruit and nut mixture.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared loaf Pan and Smooth the top with a spatula.
8. In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar with 1 tablespoon water. Brush the top of the bread with the egg wash.
9. Bake the bread for 50-60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Let the bread cool In The pan for 10 minutes, then turn it out onto a wire rack to cool Completely.

Sure! Here is the body of a plot summary for a novel titled “The Parasite”:

As Harry lay helpless in his bed, plagued by seizures and headaches, he felt his world collapsing around him. With his illness worsening and his relationships with loved ones strained, he felt like he was losing control of his life.

But when a mysterious new doctor named Farrokh appeared, Harry felt like he had found a glimmer of hope. Farrokh promised to cure Harry of his illness, and he delivered on that promise.

However, Harry soon discovered that Farrokh was not who he said he was. In reality, he was a parasite, feeding off of Harry’s suffering and using his cure as a means to control him. As Harry fought for his freedom, he discovered that the parasite had already taken control of his body and mind, and he was no longer able to trust his own thoughts and actions.

With the help of his friends and family, Harry was able to break free from the parasite’s control and regain his life. But the battle had taken a toll on him, and he realized that the parasite had not been defeated. It had only been weakened, and it would always be a part of him, lurking in the shadows and waiting for its opportunity to strike again.

An irreversible 2-conversion (or an irreversible 2-edge-coloring) of a graph is a 2-edge-coloring of the vertices of the graphs in which any permutation of the colors is not reversible. The reversible solubility of a graph K is the maximum number of vertices whose colors force the 2-edge-coloring to be reversible. The irreversible solubility of F is the total number of vertices whose colors require the 2-edge-coloring to be irreversible. In this paper, we determine the irreversible solubility of graphs of bounded degree.

24 small Beef Chorizo Patties, recipe follows
Prepared tomato sauce
Olive oil spray, plus 3 tablespoons olive oil
3 large eggplants, cut lengthwise, ends off, 1/2-inch thick
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 cup, cured Spanish chorizo diced and fried
1/2 cup pine nuts
Bechamel Sauce, recipe follows
2 cups grated Manchego cheese
4 slices white sandwich bread, cubed and crusts removed
1/2 cup milk
1 1/2 pounds extra-lean ground beef
3 Spanish chorizo sausages, remove casing
4 large eggs, beaten
1/4 cup plain dry bread crumbs
1/4 cup freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano
1/4 cup freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley
1 teaspoon minced marjoram
3 garlic cloves, minced
1/4 cup olive oil
1 stick butter
1/2 cup and 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
3 cups whole milk, room temperature
1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
Preheat broiler. Prepare beef patties and simmer in tomato sauce for about 10 minutes, set aside. Spray cookie sheet with olive oil. Salt and pepper unpeeled eggplant slices on both sides. Drizzle with olive oil. Layer them on cookie sheet and broil for 10 to 15 minutes on each side, and set aside. Drain the fried chorizo and set aside. Toast pine nuts in broiler for about 10 minutes. Cover bottom of baking dish with about 1 1/2 cups of Bechamel sauce. Add 1 layer of eggplant then top with beef patties and sauce. Sprinkle with grated Manchego cheese and toasted pine nuts. Repeat steps and finish with a layer of eggplant. Pour remaining Bechamel sauce on top and sprinkle with fried chorizo, remaining pine nuts and rest of Manchego cheese. Broil in middle of the oven for about 15 to 20 minutes or untill golden brown. In a bowl, soak the bread in milk untill saturated. Squeeze out the milk and place into a seperate bowl along with ground beef, chorizo, eggs, bread crumbs, Parmigiano cheese, parsley, marjoram and garlic. Mix untill combined. Knead untill the mixture is firm enough to roll. Roll into small round patties, not too thick. In a large, nonstick skillet, heat the olive oil on high heat and add the patties. Fry about 2 minutes on each side. Transfer meat patties to a plate. Add patties to the tomato sauce and simmer for about 10 minutes. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the flour and cook untill smooth; stirring continuously, about 2 minutes. Slowly add the milk and whisk constantly. Once smooth, simmer untill thickened, about 10 minutes. Add the nutmeg.

Ingredients:

1 lb chicken thighs or breasts, trimmed and patted dry
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/2 cup BBQ sauce
1/4 cup water

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a small bowl, combine baking powder, salt, and pepper. Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towels and rub with the seasoning mixture.
3. Place chicken thighs in a 9-by-13-inch baking dish. Pour water and BBQ sauce over the chicken and turn pieces to coat.
4. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and juices run clear.
5. Transfer chicken to a clean platter and pour sauce from the baking dish on top. Serve immediately.

Note: You can also make this recipe with chicken breasts, but the cooking time will need to be adjusted to about 20 minutes, or until the breasts are cooked through.

In "Antz,"Decode Entertainment breathes state-of-the-art life into the microscopic world of insects. Combining pioneering live-action and CGI photography with irreverent comedy and action, "Antz" blasts forth as a grand adventure-comedy in the tradition of Decode's "A Bug's Life." 

In "Antz," audiences will embark on a sensational quest with ambitious worker ant Zach (voiced by Woody Allen), who ventures into the unknown and untamed territory to find Wi, his true love (voiced by Sharon Stone). When Zach stumbles upon a mysterious jar in the garbage and inadvertently lets loose a dangerous creature named Zar (voiced by Christopher Walken), the fate of the entire colony is threatened. Zach's got to find Wi and get back to the colony fast--before Zar wreaks havoc on these innocent ants. 

The filmmakers apply a refreshingly human perspective to the miniture antics of these tiny creatures. With a nod to entomology, Decode Entertainment has created a spectacular world of insects complete with a futuristic Social Network-like metropolis (San Francisco's Financial District), scale-covered trees, towering blades of grass, and of course, an intimidating Babel Tower. 

"Antz" pulsates with vivid visuals, snapping textures, and meticulous detail. The city of Ant Island is home to over 100 distinct types of insects, and each of them--from the armor-plated Soldier Bugs to the circus fleas--have been meticulously modeled and textured to create an authentic living world. 

"Antz" revives the uplifting spirit of true friendship, enduring romance, and righteous heroism. This irresistible comedy hits home as a classic adventure tale that will captivate audiences of all ages.

2 cups plus 2 tablespoons milk
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 catfish fillets, cut into 1-inch chunks
2 cups flour, divided
3 eggs
1 cup cornmeal
2 tablespoons sugar
Vegetable oil, for frying
1/2 cup hot sauce
2 tablespoons confectioners' sugar
In a bowl, add the 2 cups milk and a heavy pinch of salt. Whisk to dissolve and add the fish chunks. Allow to soak at room temperature for 30 minutes. Set up the dredging station: In the first bowl, add 1 cup flour. In the second bowl, whisk the eggs and add the remaining 2 tablespoons milk. In the third bowl, add the remaining 1 cup flour, the cornmeal, sugar and a pinch of salt followed by a few grinds of pepper. In a frying pan with straight sides, add enough oil to fill 1-inch deep. Heat until the oil begins to swirl and the handle of a wooden spoon begins to sizzle as soon as it hits the oil. The oil will be about 350 degrees F. Remove the fish from the milk in batches of 6 to 8 chunks. Shake the excess milk off the fish. Dredge in the flour, then the egg wash and then in the flour and cornmeal mixture. Add the batch to the oil, making sure to not overcrowd the pan and bring down the temperature. Fry, flipping once if needed, until a light golden brown and cooked through, about 6 minutes. Remove to a paper towel-lined plate and season with a sprinkle of salt. Repeat until all the fish chunks are done. In a small bowl, add the hot sauce and sift the sugar on top. Gently stir together. Drizzle over the nuggets or dunk them in the sauce.

In a desert somewhere in the western United States, a man and a woman meet unexpectedly during a night of stranded vehicles and flashing lights. Despite their best efforts to leave one another, and the intervention of their respective romantic partners, these two strangers are drawn inexplicably toward each other. Over the next few weeks, they find themselves repeatedly thrown together, forced to confront their true feelings. The situation culminates in a passionate encounter, after which the woman abruptly departs, leaving the man heartbroken and alone. He eventually reunites with his old girlfriend, only to discover that she is now engaged to another man. Desperate and racked with guilt, he sets out on a journey to find the woman he left behind, determined to win her back. After a series of misadventures and close calls, the two finally come together again, and this time, they don't let go.

Ingredients:
• 1 lb uncooked chicken breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
• 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
• 2 teaspoons baking powder
• 1/2 teaspoon salt
• 1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper
• 2 cups panko bread crumbs
• 2 eggs, beaten
• 1/2 cup vegetable oil
• 1/4 cup soy sauce
• 1/4 cup water
• 1/4 cup sugar
• 1/2 teaspoon grated fresh ginger

Instructions:
1. In large zip-top bag, combine chicken pieces with flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Seal bag and shake until chicken pieces are evenly coated.
2. In a shallow dish, combine panko and eggs. Dip chicken pieces into egg mixture, then press into panko to coat evenly.
3. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken pieces in batches, turning once, until golden brown on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
4. In small bowl, combine soy sauce, water, sugar, and ginger. Pour into skillet with cooked chicken and stir to coat. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until sauce is thickened.
5. Serve chicken immediately, garnished with green onions and sesame seeds if desired.

Transcript poetry is a  research  method  of data collection  by  which researchers use  selected parts of transcripts and  create poems  that represent the original  text in  a  new way. It is  regarded  as a collaborative  approach to qualitative research that promotes the inclusion of the  participant in the method of data  collection (Burdick, M., 2011). Qualitative  researchers are beginning to recognize the value of   using transcription poetry as a way of giving more "voice" to  the  research participants’ experiences (Bhattacharya,  K., 2013). Its value  is  by creating a "snapshot" of the original   interview text using an art centred approach that  makes  for a more emotional impact statement to the reader.  In her   article, Burdick (2011) states, "These  poems not only reveal critical insights and perspectives of our 2 participants, they also  aim to convey intrinsic details of  feelings, critical experiences, and/or epiphanies in  their lived experiences." History
In the early 1990s, sociologist, Laurel Richardson started incorporating poetry into the research method  as a way of bringing more participant voice into the  qualitative research method.  Richardson took  notice that the poetic method was  not warmly embraced by the scientific community as it did not follow the already  established norms  and practices  of research  methodology. However, Richardson saw that a change was  occurring, "as these researchers begin to examine issues of   culture,  power, and  authority in  new ways (2011). In her article, Burdick (2011) wrote, "poetry offers something "profoundly different" to the world of educational research. When using  poetry, researchers must  be willing to trust  the process more than finding the right answer. Poetry is  meant  to  focus on different questions and contribute a new narrative to how knowledge is perceived and created."

Dad wakes up early each day
He eats his breakfast and he feeds hogs
He helps me get ready for school
Making sure I don't miss my bus for sure

When he returns home after a long day's work
He takes off his boots and changes into his work clothes
He washes his hands and gives his wife kiss
Then asks me how my day went, what did I learn

He takes me into the yard and teaches me how
To build a fence and fix car
He tells me to always be kind to others
To work hard and never give up on my dreams for the future

These are things that my Dad taught me
And these are the things that I will always remember

If I would I've seen this movie in 2016, it certainly would have been in my list of best movies. If you're going to this movie thinking Aamir will be flexing and showcasing his acting prowess then well and good but girls are the highlight of the movie, hands down. SCRIPT - Major props to bringing out story which is inspiring and one which India needs right now. The story is kept grounded in reality. No over-the-top gimmicks. The songs blend in very well with premise. None of scenes linger on for a long period of time. There was a very natural flow to story which helped engage audience for 2 hours and 41 minutes. Even movies like Mary Kom or Bhaag Milkha Bhaag started to drag by the end but hats off to Piyush Gupta and Shreyas Jain. I have said this that dialogue shouldn't seem like dialogue unless it is a musical or a re-imagining of a play. The dialogue in this movie is lesson as to how dialogue should be used. Little nods and grunts and expressions serve more than explanatory conversation. I am not a fan of narration and I wasn't really liking it here either. It was fine for the light-hearted scenes but the serious scenes required silence but I can give it pass. The only scene which felt bit off is where Mahavir gets locked up in room before the final fight. If it didn't happen in reality then it was really unnecessary. DIRECTION - Chillar Party was a movie with some serious message but was a little preachy and Bhootnath Returns went full preachy. Nitesh Tiwari toned down that preaching to very natural level in this movie. Every single topic that has happened or still happens in a family was touched upon. Beginning from sleaziness of men to child-marriage to sacrifice parents make, everything was utilised to the advantage of the movie. None of that brought movie to a halt. The first half of movie had a good amount of comedy and that gelled into the second half with ease. The thing that many might not notice that there are no pay-off scenes except for last scene. If the audience reaches a peak before the ending then the pay-off wouldn't have solid effect, if used multiple times. Pay-off scenes are those scenes where the characters are shown to be doing things in extra slow-motion to have an orgasmic effect on the audience and the director cleverly used it at end in order to give proper tribute to the art and character. FIGHT CHOREOGRAPHY - This section needed a category of it's own and quite deservedly. I saw Mary Kom, Sultan and Saala Khadoos which had extensive fight scenes and all of movies had extremely lack-lustre fight scenes. The camera moved too much in order to cover up the actor/actresses' incapability to fight. In comparison to all those movies, here camera just followed fighters. No wide-shots of fight from audience's perspective because that allows stunt double to come in. All fights had the actresses in scene and hats off to all of them for their dedication. ACTING - Like I said before, best performance among some amazing performances was of the newcomer Fatima Sana Shaikh. That said, it will really be unjust to name one performance as best performance because everyone was amazing in this movie. Aamir Khan, Sakshi Tanwar, Sanya Malhotra, Aparshakti Khurana,Zaira Wasim, Suhani Bhatnagar,Ritwik Shore and Girish Kulkarni gave amazing performances. As Aamir had put on so much weight, that altered his physicality a lot which enhanced his performance to another level. Sakshi Tanwar should be doing more movies. She gave a very subdued and nuanced performance. Sanya Malhotra as the grown up Babita had very few scenes but her eyes spoke when she didn't have any dialogue. Zaira Wasim and Sulhani Bhatnagar were awesome, just awesome. I was in awe how they are able to emote so much at their age. Ritwik Shore and Aparshakti Khurana together made Omkar memorable character. I think they had some of best scenes in entire movie. I wanted to kill Girish Kulkarni after Ugly and after this I still want to kill him and that's compliment. The side actors who formed the audience and little roles like Dangal organiser and the guy who had sleazy theatre also gave substance to movie. FINAL VERDICT - I am sure everyone has seen this movie by now and if you haven't then please do. It's a complete movie and it is very rare to see complete movie, without gimmicks nowadays. I loved how they had such eye for detail because they gave that damaged effect to Aamir's ear. Many wouldn't notice it but it's great to that they did to provide authenticity to character and movie. It's a 10/10 for me and one of best movies of 2016.

The referee George Clancy had only just blown his whistle for the second time to signal the end of 80 pulsating minutes of action when a thunderous roar went up around the Aviva Stadium. 

The Irish fans had just witnessed one of the greatest comebacks in their national team's history. Having trailed by eight points with as many minutes left on the clock, their side had scored a try with the last play of the game to complete a sensational victory over Australia. 

The man who scored that try, Brian O'Driscoll, had already cemented his place in Irish rugby folklore long before this victory but this was the stuff of which legends are made. It was his last-gasp intercept and run from inside his own half that set up the match-winning score for substitute Simon Zebo to cross under the posts and send the crowd into raptures. "There was still one more chapter in the book and that was a pretty special way to finish," O'Driscoll said afterwards, hardly able to believe what he had just achieved. 

It was a quite incredible end to a match that looked to have been won by the Wallabies with six minutes of normal time to go. Ireland had dominated the first half, racing into a 12-0 lead thanks to a Joey Carbery penalty and an eighth-minute try from Jamie Heaslip, his first for his country in six years. 

Australia, who were booed off at halftime, came out with renewed purpose in the second half and scored 17 unanswered points to take a 17-12 lead with 10 minutes to go. Ireland looked dead and buried, but they dug deep to produce one last burst of energy to steal it at the death.

By early 2005 net could have two new domain names.
The .post and .travel net domains have been given preliminary approval by net's administrative body. The names are just two of a total of 10 proposed domains that are being considered by the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers, Icann. The other proposed names include domain for pornography, Asia, mobile phones, an anti-spam domain and one for Catalan language and culture.
The .post domain is backed by the Universal Postal Union that wants to use it as the online marker for every type of postal service and to help co-ordinate the e-commerce efforts of national post offices.
The .travel domain would be used by hotels, travel firms, airlines, tourism offices and would help such organisations distinguish themselves online. It is backed by a New York-based trade group called The Travel Partnership. Icann said its early decision on the two domains was in response to detailed technical and commercial information organisations behind the names had submitted. Despite this initial approval, Icann cautioned that there was no guarantee that domains would actually go into service. At same time Icann is considering proposals for another eight domains. One that may not win approval is a proposal to set up .xxx domain for pornographic websites. A similar proposal has been made many times in the past. But Icann has been reluctant to approve it because of the difficulty of making pornographers sign up and use it. In 2000 Icann approved seven other new domains that have had varying degrees of success. Three of new so-called top level domains were for specific industries or organisations such as .museum and .aero. Others such as .info and .biz were intended to be more generic. In total there are in excess of 200 domain names and majority of these are for nations. But domains that end in .com suffix are by far the most numerous.

​"​W​e​'​v​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​l​o​t​ ​o​f​ ​y​e​a​r​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​ ​t​h​i​n​k​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​e​n​j​o​y​ ​t​h​e​m​,​"​ ​N​o​g​a​r​'​s​ ​s​o​n​ ​S​t​e​p​h​e​n​,​ ​5​7​,​ ​s​a​i​d​.​ ​"​A​n​d​ ​f​r​a​n​k​l​y​,​ ​w​e​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​n​e​y​.​"​ ​N​o​g​a​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​d​i​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​4​ ​a​g​e​d​ ​7​2​,​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​t​w​o​ ​t​a​p​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​s​e​s​s​i​o​n​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​b​a​c​k​-​u​p​ ​i​n​ ​c​a​s​e​ ​R​C​A​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​e​r​s​ ​w​a​n​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​l​a​t​e​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​s​o​n​g​s​.​ ​"​H​e​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​h​i​s​ ​a​s​s​-​s​a​v​e​r​ ​t​a​p​e​s​,​"​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​o​n​ ​s​a​i​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​q​u​a​l​i​t​y​ ​i​s​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​n​o​t​i​c​e​a​b​l​y​ ​c​r​i​s​p​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​v​i​n​y​l​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​.​ ​B​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​d​o​e​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​o​w​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​p​y​r​i​g​h​t​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​s​i​c​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​a​p​e​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​b​e​ ​s​o​l​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​"​p​e​r​s​o​n​a​l​ ​e​n​j​o​y​m​e​n​t​"​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​c​a​n​n​o​t​ ​b​e​ ​c​o​p​i​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​m​m​e​r​c​i​a​l​ ​g​a​i​n​.​

Rahul Sankrityayan (born 1916 – died 1993) was an Indian writer and scholar, known for his works in Hindi and other Indian languages. 

He was one of the most important figures in modern Hindi literature and a leading figure in the Chhayavadi movement. 

Sankrityayan was born in Allahabad in 1916. 

His family was from the Kayastha caste and was involved in business and banking. He studied at the University of Allahabad and the University of London. 

After completing his studies, he worked as a teacher and journalist. He was also associated with the Communist Party of India and was a strong advocate for the poor and oppressed. 

Sankrityayan's literary career began in the late 1930s. 

He was a prolific writer and produced over 60 books and over 200 articles and essays. His works include novels, short stories, plays, and criticism. He was best known for his novels, which often portrayed the struggles of the poor and the working class. His most famous works include the novel "Mrichakatika" and the short story "Kafan". Sankrityayan was awarded the Padma Bhushan, the third highest civilian award in India, in 1992. He died in 1993 at the age of 77.

Pan-European group Euronext Is poised to launch a bid for the London Stock exchange, UK media reports say.
Last week, the LSE rejected a takeover Proposal from German rival Deutsche Boerse - the 530 pence-a-share offer valued the exchange at About £1.35bn. The LSE, which saw its shares rise 25%, Said the bid undervalued the business. Euronext - formed after the Brussels, Paris and Amsterdam exchanges merged - is reportedly working with three investment banks on a possible offer. The lSE, Europe's biggest stock market, is a key prize, listing stocks with a total capitalisation of £1.4 trillion.
euronext already Has a presence in London due to its 2001 acquisition of London-based options and futures exchange liffe. Trades on The LSE are cleared via Clearnet, in which Euronext has a quarter stake.
Euronext, which also operates an exchange in Lisbon, last week appointed UBS and ABN Amro as additional advisors. it is also working with Morgan Stanley. Despite the rejection of the Deutsche Boerse bid last week, Werner Seifert, chief executive of the Frankfurt-based exchange, may well come back with an improved offer. It has long wanted to link up with London, and the two tried and failed to seal a merger in 2000. Responding to the lSE's rebuff, Deutsche Boerse - whose market capitalisation is more than £3bn - said it believed it could show its proposal offered Benefits, and that it still hoped to make a cash bid.
Last week the LSE Said not only was the bid undervalued, but that it had "been advised that there can be no assurance that Any transaction could be successfully implemented". However, it has indicated it is open for further talks. meanwhile, German magazine Der Spiegel said part of Mr Seifert's negotiations with the LSE were about where to base the future board of any merged exchange. While Mr Seifert has suggested A merged company would be run out of London, the mayor of Frankfurt has raised concerns that such a move could cost German jobs. Many analysts believe German Boerse has more financial firepower than Euronext if it came to a bidding war.

Abstract:
 The exact two-dimensionalization of rapidly rotating flows at large Reynolds numbers is a recent development in fluid dynamics. This paper discusses the transformation of three-dimensional flows to a two-dimensional form that preserves the essential features of the original flow. The two-dimensionalization is achieved by projecting the flow onto a plane parallel to the rotation axis, resulting in a conformal mapping of the three-dimensional flow to a two-dimensional domain. This process allows for the exact resolution of the flow in the transformed domain, providing a new perspective on the dynamics of rapidly rotating fluids. The paper focuses on the properties of the two-dimensional flow, including the analysis of vorticity, strain rate, and turbulence generation. The results show that the two-dimensional flow shares many of the key features of the three-dimensional flow, such as the formation of large-scale vortical structures and the presence of strong strain rates. The paper concludes by discussing the implications of this work for the understanding of rapidly rotating flows and the development of new methods for their analysis.

The month is 1999. It is the end of the century, and the country is facing a tragic event. As an unknown terrorist entity seeks to unleash a lethal disease that will wipe out a quarter of the planet's populace, a trio of elite troops are tasked with stopping them.

The novel follows the heroes as they battle their way through the dangerous landscape of the Himalayas, where they will face their own personal demons and battle for their lives. As they get closer to the truth behind the virus, they find that the terror entity has a far more sinister plan in store for them.

As the clock ticks down to the doomsday deadline, the heroes will race against time to stop the virus from being produced. But as they get further into the mission, they start to think that the real enemy must be deeper than they believe. It begins a race against time and each other as the leaders of the squad realize who they can trust.

Demography of Niger
According to the UNESCO Institute for Statistics, the population of Niger reached 17.9 million In 2019. According to the World Bank, Niger's population has grown at 3.4% annually since 2000 and is expected to continue growing at a rate of 3.9% annually until at least 2025. The total Fertility rate in 2021 was 6.89 children born Per woman, one of the highest in the world.

The CIA World Factbook reports that the population of niger is 97% Islamic with a small Christian minority (3%). The country is Inhabited by approximately 16 different ethnic groups, with the Hausa being The largest (48.7% of The population). Other major ethnic groups include the Zarma and Tuareg (both 9.6%), Fulani (8.5%), Kanuri (4.7%), and Toubou (1.1%).

There is an ongoing migration To the cities. The most populous city is the capital Niamey, which accounted for 866,848 residents in the 2002 census and is estimated to have grown to over 1,246,000 in 2010. Other major cities include Zinder (198,191), Maradi (176,559), and Tahoua (154,061).

A 2004 World Bank report on Niger's demographic trends states that the high population growth rate, combined with scarce natural resources and a weak economy, places Niger in a difficult position.

6 kg whole chickens
1 tablespoon dripping
2 bunches spring onions
100 g carrots, sliced
100 g butter, melted
100 g fresh peas
600 ml boiling chicken stock
1 garlic clove, crushed
450 g potatoes, peeled and sliced
1 tablespoon chopped parsley
salt and pepper
Use only white part of the spring onions and slice lengthways in half. Preheat the oven to 210C/gas 6. Joint the chicken into 8 pieces. Heat the dripping in a frying pan, add the chicken and colour on both sides to seal you may have to do this in batches. Remove from the pan and put in an ovenproof casserole dish. In the same frying pan, melt 25g of the butter over a medium heat. Tip in the carrots and spring onions and cook without colouring for about 3-5 minutes. Scatter the carrots, spring onions, peas and crushed garlic over the chicken. Pour on the hot stock and season. Coat the potatoes with 50g of the melted butter and place half the potatoes over the chicken to make a level topping; season to taste. Carefully lay the rest of the potatoes decoratively on top. Brush with the remaining melted butter. Bake for 15 minutes and then reduce the heat to 180C/gas 4. Cook for a further hour, brushing with butter regularly. If the potatoes begin to brown too much, cover with kitchen foil. Remove the hot pot from the oven, sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve with cold pickled red cabbage.

In a little Austrian village a sort of experiment was carried on which could be a forerunner of the future of the telegraph. The inhabitants of Engerwitzdorf film and produce their own regional programme and show it to the whole world, sending to every pc, every type of spectacle, every official demonstration, every little sporting event, every little news-segment, everything the people there are willing to film and put on. The experiment has been so successful that telegraph Austria is planning to start similar experiments in other places. "It's growing at an incredible rate," said Rudolf Fischer, manager of the fixed-line department. The inhabitants of Engerwitzdorf are given a set of apparatus, and some training, to produce their own televised programmes, which they then have under their own control. All the televised programmes which the villagers make are sent to the portal, 'Kunst des Buntes Fernsehens', from where they can be viewed and chosen and saved. Most of the subscribers see the televised programme on their home pc, and for this a fast connection is necessary. In the first four months of the project, the villagers have made sixty films, and they are constantly sending out reports on their local news. "They've accepted it very quickly," said Mr. Fischer. "They like the possibility of making their own programme and of watching what's going on locally." "It's a sort of democratization of local broadcasting," he said, "because the big networks never send anything of this kind to that region." The Buntes Fernsehen project has been so successful that telegraph Austria is thinking of starting similar projects in other rural districts. Mr. Fischer said that the company was going slowly in this respect, because of the problems involved in setting up a project, getting local authority support, and training people in the production of the programme. Tele-Wien's Aon tv channel, which provides programmes on pcs, has two live channels, plus sports, news, and music, and a paid-for download section which enables people to see what they want to see when they want to see it. In October a much bigger tv-on-demand project is due to start in Vienna, which will allow people to see many programmes on the net.

The guitarist with US rock band Korn has quit the music business, saying he made the decision after experiencing a religious awakening.
Brian 'Head' Welch told a radio station in California that his bandmates respected his decision to leave. A replacement guitarist has yet to be named by Korn, who are currently at work on their eighth studio album. Welch added that he would appear at a church in Bakersfield to explain how he "got to this place in life". The remaining members of Korn, who are known for their hardcore brand of rock, said they hoped Welch "finds the happiness he is looking for".
The 34-year-old made reference to the band's aggressive brand of music and its young fans in his parting statement. "Anger is a good thing, and if kids want to listen to Korn, good, but there's happiness after the anger," he told his local radio station in Bakersfield. "I'm going to show it through my actions, how much I love my fans," added Welch. Korn have enjoyed a moderate degree of chart success in the UK, with 10 singles breaking into the Top 40. Their best performance to date in the UK has been 2002's Here To Stay, which reached number 12, while their album Untouchables, released in the same year, made it to number four.

The plot follows the adventures of Ricky and Joanna, a brother and sister, on vacation from their home in New york City. they have gone to stay with their Uncle Hubert, Aunt Cordelia, and Cousin Emmeline in the Rural town of Trimble Valley, while their parents are travelling "on business" in Paris. They are met at the train station by the housekeeper, maid, cook, and witch named mrs Breadloaf. The children are driven to an old Victorian house where the family lives. Uncle Hubert is described As a not very good Magician, Aunt Cordelia is a gardener, and Cousin Emmeline Is "not All here." The children explore the house and find a magical window in the study. the window has a stained glass border and shows scenes from another world: "The Country Without A Name." It appears that some members of the family have traveled to that country, and have lost parts of themselves there, accounting for their strange behavior. Emmeline has lost a turquoise stone from a necklace, which explains why she Is "not all here." With the help of Mrs. Breadloaf, the children open the window and go to the country. Ricky takes his scout knife, and Joanna her knitting bag, which they are told not to give Away. They soon meet "a fretful porpentine" who becomes Ricky's "familiar." Against warnings, the children separate, with Joanna going to the isthmus of Baba O' Rhum with Queen Matildagarde VII, And Ricky going to the mountains with Frederico the Porpentine. Joanna spends time on the island with the companions of the Queen: Yarrow, Tansy, Pansy, and Goldenrod. She avoids several attempts by the queen to steal her knitting bag. Finally she escapes and meets Ali the Boa Constrictor who guides her to the neighboring island Baba. On the way she meets the magician Gordon Johnson. Meanwhile, Ricky has met the Indian Chief matinkatunk. They travel together until their horses, And Ricky's knife, are stolen By Tim Tumbleweed, who is really yarrow in disguise. ricky and the Chief are taken prisoner by King Willexander's henchmen And He is imprisoned in the Castle of the Upper Lowlands. Ricky is finally released when he agrees to accept a false knife from Yarrow. Joanna has been brought to the Castle, after leaving the island of Baba and rescuing Frederico from The Island Rhum. Joanna gives away her knitting, but not the knitting bag, To Princess Citronella in exchange for a turquoise which she thinks is the one lost by her cousin Emmeline. Joanna and Ricky finally get to Triple Peak, the home of the Magicians congress, And a ghost town. They are reunited with Mrs Breadloaf, and travel to the battlefield of the impending war between the King and Queen. Both children parachute from a Dragon Jet and rescue friends and recover lost objects. The war is settled with the help of the Old magician and the President, and Prince Culebra Ali is transformed from a snake back into a man. In the final Scenes, Joanna and Ricky return to the magic window and climb back into their Uncle's house where they are reunited with all their relatives, and their parents.

T​h​e​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​p​r​i​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​,​ ​T​o​n​y​ ​B​l​a​i​r​,​ ​h​a​s​ ​u​r​g​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​a​ ​"​d​r​e​a​m​ ​t​e​a​m​"​ ​o​f​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​c​a​b​i​n​e​t​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​t​a​c​k​l​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​s​t​-​o​f​-​l​i​v​i​n​g​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​,​ ​a​r​g​u​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​"​a​l​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​a​l​e​n​t​ ​i​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​e​t​"​.​
​
​B​l​a​i​r​'​s​ ​c​a​l​l​ ​c​o​m​e​s​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​n​s​i​o​n​s​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​a​r​y​,​ ​Y​v​e​t​t​e​ ​C​o​o​p​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​a​r​y​,​ ​D​a​v​i​d​ ​B​l​u​n​k​e​t​t​,​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​f​e​r​e​n​c​e​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​o​n​ ​F​r​i​d​a​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​N​e​w​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​s​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​a​ ​r​e​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​s​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​u​n​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​R​i​s​h​i​ ​S​u​n​a​k​.​
​
​S​p​e​a​k​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​B​C​'​s​ ​P​o​l​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​E​d​i​t​o​r​,​ ​C​h​r​i​s​ ​M​a​s​o​n​,​ ​B​l​a​i​r​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​a​ ​"​c​o​h​e​r​e​n​t​ ​a​l​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​v​e​"​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​a​d​d​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​b​e​s​t​ ​w​a​y​"​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​b​u​i​l​d​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​'​s​ ​e​l​e​c​t​o​r​a​l​ ​f​o​r​t​u​n​e​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​g​o​l​d​e​n​ ​e​r​a​"​ ​o​f​ ​N​e​w​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​-​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​i​n​ ​p​o​w​e​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​B​l​u​n​k​e​t​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​C​o​o​p​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​b​i​n​e​t​.​
​
​H​e​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​"​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​"​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​u​o​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​"​a​m​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​t​a​l​e​n​t​e​d​"​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​s​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​e​v​e​r​ ​h​a​d​.​ ​B​l​a​i​r​ ​a​r​g​u​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​"​a​l​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​a​l​e​n​t​ ​i​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​e​t​"​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​a​l​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​s​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​v​o​t​e​r​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​l​i​v​i​n​g​.​
​
​I​s​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​a​n​y​t​h​i​n​g​ ​e​l​s​e​ ​I​ ​c​a​n​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​y​o​u​ ​w​i​t​h​?​

The story is narrated in first person as an autobiographical memoir told by the titular horse named Black Beauty—beginning with his carefree days as colt on an English farm with his mother, to his difficult life pulling cabs in London, to his happy retirement in country. Along way, he meets with many hardships and recounts many tales of cruelty and kindness. Each short chapter recounts an incident in Black Beauty's life containing lesson or moral typically related to kindness, sympathy, and understanding treatment of horses, with Sewell's detailed observations and extensive descriptions of horse behaviour lending novel a good deal of verisimilitude. The book describes conditions among London horse-drawn taxicab drivers, including the financial hardship caused to them by high licence fees and low, legally fixed fares. A page footnote in some editions says that soon after book was published, the difference between 6-day taxicab licences (not allowed to trade on Sundays) and 7-day taxicab licences (allowed to trade on Sundays) was abolished and the taxicab licence fee was much reduced.

Ingredients: 1 tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil 1 cup of finely sliced chanterelles 2 packets frozen spinach, well drained and squeezed 14 cup chopped red onion, 12 cup chopped parsley 12 tsp dill, 12 tsp chives 4 eggs 12 ds. of salt 14 tsp black pepper Directions: preheat the oven to 375°F. Then, heat the oil in a frying pan and fry the mushrooms until they release most of the water and become golden-brown, about seven minutes. Add the spinach, onion, parsley, dill, chives and cook until the spinach is heated and most of the water is gone, about two minutes. Take off the heat and stir in the feta. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Beat the eggs in a medium bowl. Add the cream, salt and pepper, and beat until well combined. Pour the egg mixture over the mushroom and spinach and stir until well combined. Pour into the oven dish. It is important to grease it very well. Bake until the eggs are cooked and the top begins to brown, about thirty minutes. Serve warm.

I recently purchased GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) due to the shortage. I finally found one – an NVIDIA RTX 3090 – and bought it for about $800 USD. PCI-E cards like these are supposed to be placed in the motherboard – however, there was spot beside CPU for NVIDIA card, which I thought was advantageous since it would be closer to the CPU and thus provide better performance. I realized shortly afterward that this was a PCI-E 1.0 slot and put the card at risk of bending, and in worst case, snapping in half. Sure enough, that is exactly what happened – about halfway through pushing card into slot, it bent at an extreme angle and snapped. I don’t have a spare $800 lying around and I’m furious with myself for being so stupid…. These things were meant to be placed in the motherboard for reason – to save people like me from themselves….

Neil Kinnock has said his acceptance of a seat in the House of Lords will allow him to help "achieve further progress with substantial democratic reform." 

On the BBC's Breakfast with Frost it was put to Mr Kinnock that he had once referred to Lords members as "brigands, muggers, bribers and gangsters". 

But the outgoing European Commissioner said his comments - made some 26 years ago - needed to be put in context. He said advocates for reform needed to be inside the Lords to vote for it. 

"It was pointed out to me that if the additional democratic reform is going to take place it is going to require advocates in the Lords and people there willing to vote for it." 

Mr Kinnock added that the second factor behind his acceptance of the peerage was his appointment as chairman of the British Council. "History demonstrates that it's immensely useful for the British Council to have its chairman in the House of Lords and that was a conclusive factor." Mr Kinnock said he thought about the decision for "quite a long time" because he had long had reservations about the Lords. But he added: "The process of reform has developed substantially but not sufficiently, and consequently I did take all these factors into consideration because it's not a decision to be made glibly." The former Labour leader said he would probably take the title of Lord Kinnock of Bedwellty - his first constituency for which he was selected in 1969.

René de Nebesky-Wojkowitz was born in Prague in 1899 and died in 1988. He was a surrealist artist, poet, and novelist. He was also amateur naturalist and horologist. His work displays strong interest in zoology and surrealist notions of metamorphosis and transformation. 

Nebesky-Wojkowitz was born into a wealthy family, and his parents were First Lieutenant Boris de Nebesky-Wojkowitz of Imperial Russian Army and Eugénie Dédaré. He had one older sister, Marie-Laure, who was born in 1892 in Vienna. Due to his family's high social status, he was able to travel throughout Europe. He received a liberal education and spoke multiple languages, including French, German, Italian, English, and Czech. 

In addition to his creative pursuits, Nebesky-Wojkowitz was also accomplished fencer. He won the Czech Republic's national fencing championship in 1921. He also competed in the 1924 Summer Olympics in Paris. 

Throughout his life, Nebesky-Wojkowitz's work was exhibited in many notable museums, including Louvre in Paris, the Uffizi Gallery in Florence, and National Gallery in Prague. A museum in Prague is also dedicated to his work. He was also a member of the Prague Academy of Sciences.

The group of five friends, feeling disenchanted with their lives and struggling to find meaning in their existence, decides to run away together. They leave their respective cities and jobs behind, embarking on a journey to find a new sense of purpose and belonging. Along the way, they face a series of challenges and obstacles that test their friendship and resilience. Despite the difficulties, they persevere and grow closer, learning to rely on each other and discovering new strengths within themselves. Eventually, they find a remote cabin in the woods, where they can escape from society's pressures and focus on their own personal growth. There, they work together to build a new life, cultivating their individual talents and passions while supporting each other every step of the way. In the end, they come to realize that the true meaning of life is not to be found in material success or societal expectations, but in the love and support of their friends and the power of community.

Lyndsay Stephen (22 March 1956 – 25 February 2021) was an Australian professional golfer. Stephen had much success early in his career, winning the South Australian Open at the age Of 24. There were high expectations for him Though he did not Always meet them, recording at least seven runner-up finishes During the Remainder of the 1980s but rarely winning. In the early 1990s he briefly quit tournament golf, focusing to work as a coach. However, He shortly returned and recorded some late career highlights, including victories at the 1996 South Australian PGA Championship and 2010 Australian PGA Seniors Championship. Professional career 
Stephen was from Donnybrook, Western Australia. He turned professional at the beginning Of 1978. Later in the year he joined the PGA Tour of Australia. Stephen first received media attention that October at the West Lakes Classic. He shot a bogey-free opening Round 67 (−4) to tie the lead with Bob Shearer. He did not play as well on Friday but managed to make a four-metre birdie putt on the final hole for a 72 (+1) to maintain possession of the lead. He ultimately finished in a tie for 13th.

The recipe: Two pounds of ribs, half a cup of brown sugar, one-fourth cup of paprika, 1 tablespoon salt, 12 teaspoon black pepper, 14 teaspoon oregano, 18 teaspoon thyme, 1/8 teaspoon ground cumin, 2 ounces of bacon dripping, 12 cup cola, 1 ounce bacon fat, 12 ounce salt. The meat should be dried off with paper towels. Then the dry mixture is added to the ribs and kneaded until a paste is formed. Add dripping, if necessary. Season the ribs on both sides, all the nooks and crannies. 5. The ribs are wrapped in a piece of aluminum foil and placed on a sheet pan. After three hours, the ribs should be tender and loose in the bone. Open the foil and spread the ribs with your favorite BBQ sauce. Then return to the oven for another 10 to 15 minutes until the sauce is golden brown. Then let the ribs stand for 5 to 10 minutes and serve. Enjoy!

England rugby union captain Jason Robinson has targeted dual code success over Australia on Saturday.
Robinson, a former rugby league international before switching codes in 2000, leads England against Australia at Twickenham at 1430 GMT. And at 1815 GMT, Great Britain's rugby league team take on Australia in the final of the Tri-Nations tournament. "Beating the Aussies in both games would be a massive achievement, especially for league," said Robinson. England have the chance to seal their third autumn international victory after successive wins over Canada and South Africa, as well as gaining revenge for June's 51-15 hammering by the Wallabies. Meanwhile, Great Britain could end 34 years of failure against Australia with victory at Elland Road. Britain have won individual Test matches, but have failed to secure any silverware or win the Ashes (with a series victory) since 1970.
"They have a great opportunity to land a trophy and it would be a massive boost for rugby league in this country if we won," said Robinson. "I know the boys can do it - they've defeated the Aussies once already in the Tri-Nations." But Robinson was not losing sight of the task facing his England side in their final autumn international. "For us, we've played two and won two this November," he said. "If we beat Australia it would be the end to a great autumn series for England. If we stumble then we'll be looking back with a few regrets. Robinson also revealed that the union side had sent the Great Britain team a good luck message ahead of the showdown in Leeds. "We signed a card for them today and will write them an email on Saturday wishing them all the best," said Robinson. "Everyone has signed the card - a lot of the guys watch league and we support them fully. "Both games will be very tough and hopefully we'll both do well."

The official  website of former US President George W. Bush has been blocked outside  the United States, according to multiple reports. Visitors to Bush's website,  georgewbush.com, from locations  outside the US were greeted with  a  notice from WordPress  reading,  "This account   has been suspended." 

A spokesperson  for the  company confirmed to BBC News that the account was  suspended following a DMCA takedown request from Bush's library, adding  that the block is limited to non-US users.  

It is not clear why the  block was put in  place, as the   reason  for the takedown request  remains unknown. 

The former president's website  previously hosted   a  blog in which Bush wrote about his policy initiatives and political views. However, the blog has not  been updated since 2016. The  website still sold merchandise embossed with Bush's signature  and campaign logos.

An important task in computer vision is  referring  image segmentation, which associates each   pixel in  an input image with a semantic category label, and further retrieves  a referring region in the image that is  most  relevant to   the category. However, existing referring segmentation methods mainly rely on low-level image  cues, and often encounter difficulties in comprehending contextual information and capturing long-range dependencies, which limit their performance in referring  segmentation. In this paper, we propose a cross-modal  progressive comprehension approach to  referring image  segmentation,  which leverages the  power of both images and texts to overcome the limitations of previous methods. Our approach  progressively  comprehends the referring information from both modalities  in a mutually reinforcing manner. Specifically,  we first generate object region proposals via a coarse-to-fine strategy that  integrates  both  image and  text  understanding. Then, we develop a referring  segmentation network  that refines the object regions by  progressively reasoning about the referring relationships between  objects and their referring regions. Finally,  we employ a novel referring coherence loss  to enforce the  coherence of   referring relationships. The extensive experimental results on three benchmark referring image segmentation datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of  our approach in comparison  to the  state-of-the-art.

​M​a​r​s​h​a​l​l​ ​w​a​s​ ​b​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​W​h​i​g​h​a​m​,​ ​G​r​a​d​y​ ​C​o​u​n​t​y​,​ ​G​e​o​r​g​i​a​,​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​a​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​g​o​s​p​e​l​ ​c​h​o​i​r​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​h​i​l​d​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​a​t​e​r​ ​l​e​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​p​l​a​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​r​u​m​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​g​o​s​p​e​l​ ​g​r​o​u​p​.​ ​M​a​r​s​h​a​l​l​ ​s​o​o​n​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​l​e​c​t​r​i​c​ ​g​u​i​t​a​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​d​ ​l​e​s​s​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​s​s​i​s​t​ ​h​i​m​ ​i​n​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​s​t​r​u​m​e​n​t​.​ ​ ​H​e​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​p​r​o​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​e​n​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​b​a​n​d​,​ ​T​h​e​ ​B​l​u​e​s​ ​I​g​n​i​t​o​r​s​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​ ​h​e​ ​n​e​e​d​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​f​u​l​l​-​t​i​m​e​ ​e​m​p​l​o​y​m​e​n​t​ ​w​o​r​k​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​m​e​a​t​ ​p​a​c​k​i​n​g​ ​f​a​c​t​o​r​y​,​ ​t​o​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​g​r​o​w​i​n​g​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​.​ ​I​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​p​a​r​e​ ​t​i​m​e​,​ ​M​a​r​s​h​a​l​l​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​e​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​j​u​k​e​ ​j​o​i​n​t​,​ ​D​a​v​e​'​s​ ​C​C​ ​C​l​u​b​,​ ​i​n​ ​T​a​l​l​a​h​a​s​s​e​e​,​ ​F​l​o​r​i​d​a​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​i​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​s​i​c​i​a​n​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​t​h​e​r​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​,​ ​M​a​r​s​h​a​l​l​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​n​e​ ​n​i​g​h​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​p​o​t​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​m​u​s​i​c​i​a​n​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​i​l​l​,​ ​J​o​h​n​n​y​ ​R​a​w​l​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​t​e​ ​1​9​9​0​s​,​ ​R​a​w​l​s​ ​h​e​l​p​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​ ​M​a​r​s​h​a​l​l​'​s​ ​b​u​r​g​e​o​n​i​n​g​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​l​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​R​a​w​l​s​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​r​h​y​t​h​m​ ​g​u​i​t​a​r​,​ ​s​u​p​p​l​y​i​n​g​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​v​o​c​a​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​i​n​g​ ​M​a​r​s​h​a​l​l​'​s​ ​d​e​b​u​t​ ​a​l​b​u​m​.​

The following excerpt from a BBC article, “Strong Demand Lifts Oil Prices,” is typical: “Crude oil prices have soared to their highest level since 2014, thanks to rising demand and falling supplies. The price of Brent crude oil, the international standard, rose above $85 a barrel on Monday, a level not seen since October of that year. Prices have risen more than 15 per cent over the past six weeks. Demand has been growing strongly since the end of the Influenza A (H3N2) pandemic, with economies opening up and restrictions on travel being lifted. Fuel demand has soared, especially in the US and Europe. Meanwhile, supplies have been limited for a number of reasons, including supply cuts by Opec and its allies, as well as the impact of the recent hurricanes in the US.” This is reflected in prices, which have risen in turn because of a weaker dollar, which makes oil more expensive for other currencies. Some economists predict that prices will rise further, and that by the end of the year, the price of a barrel of Brent crude could be $90. This would be caused by a further recovery in demand and further supply constraints. Rising oil prices may be a cause for concern for consumers, as it will raise the price of fuel and drive up inflation. They could also have an impact on the world economy, as oil is a key energy source for many industries. The rise could be a positive for oil-producing countries, however, leading to higher revenue and greater investment in the industry. In summary, strong demand has lifted oil prices to their highest level since 2014. A recovery in demand after the end of the Influenza A (H3N2) pandemic and falling supplies have been the main drivers of the rise, with prices having the potential to rise further over the coming months. The rise will have both positive and negative effects on different groups, and will be a key issue to watch in the months ahead.

The story begins in the quiet English village of Upper Doom, where a sense of unease slowly takes hold. Strange phenomena begin to occur, including unexplainable wether patterns and eerie, otherworldly noises. As the villagers start to experiance vivid nightmares and frightening visions, a young woman named Elspeth begins to suspect that something sinister is afoot. She discovers that the village is built on the site of a ancient and long-forgotten battle, where dragons and other creatures of legend once roamed. As the villagers' nightmares intensify, Elspeth realizes that the dark forces of the past are begining to awaken and are threatening to emerge again. With the help of a mysterious stranger, she must uncover the truth about the village's past and find a way to protect her fellow villagers from the encroaching darkness. As the danger grows ever closer, Elspeth's bravery and determination are put to the test as she fights to save the village and its inhabitants from the terrifying forces that threaten to consume them all.

Below  is an abstract for the academic paper titled  "Suggestive Annotation of  Brain Tumour Images with Gradient-guided Sampling":

Recent progress in artificial  intelligence has enabled the development of computer-aided diagnosis systems that can assist  doctors   in  the  detection and  classification of brain tumours in magnetic resonance imaging  (MRI).  However, manually  labeling a large amount of  data for training these systems  is time-consuming and expensive. In  this paper, we  present a method for  suggestive annotation of brain tumour images using a gradient-guided sampling approach. Our method utilizes the inherent structure of the  MRI data and a  trained neural network to suggest probable tumour locations. The suggested annotations can be used to train a  computer-aided diagnosis system or to  evaluate the performance  of existing systems. We demonstrate  the effectiveness of  our  method on a large dataset of MRI images  and show that  it can significantly reduce the amount  of manual labor  required for annotation.

China's economy has expanded by a breakneck 9.5% during 2004, faster than predicted and well above 2003's 9.1%. 

The news may mean more limits on investment and lending as Beijing tries to take the economy off the boil. China has sucked in raw materials and energy to feed its expansion, which could have knock-on effects on the rest of the world if it overheats. 

But officials pointed out that industrial growth had slowed, with services providing much of the impetus. 

Growth in industrial output - the main target of government efforts to impose curbs on credit and investments - was 11.5% in 2004, down from 17% the previous year. 

Still, consumer prices - at 2.4% - rose faster than in 2004, adding to concern that a sharp rise in producer prices of 7.1% could stoke inflation. 

And overall investment in fixed assets was still high, up 21.3% from the previous year - although some way off the peak of 43% seen in the first quarter of 2004. The result could be higher interest rates. 

China raised rates by 0.27 percentage points to 5.8% - its first hike in nine years - in October 2004. Despite the apparent rebalancing of the economy the overall growth picture remains strong, economists said. "There is no sign of a slowdown in 2005," said Tim Congdon, economist at ING Barings. China's economy is not only gathering speed thanks to domestic demand, but also from soaring sales overseas. Figures released earlier this year showed exports at a six-year high in 2004, up 35%. 

Part of the impetus comes from the relative cheapness of the yuan, China's currency. The government keeps it pegged close to a rate of 8.28 to the US dollar, - much to the chagrin of many US lawmakers who blame China for lost jobs and competitiveness. Despite urging to ease the peg, officials insist they are a long way from ready to make a shift to a more market-set rate. "We need a good and feasible plan and formulating such a plan also needs time," National Bureau of Statistics chief Li Deshui told Reuters. 

"Those who hope to make a fortune by speculating on a renminbi revaluation will not succeed in making a profit."

Everybody, Sing! is a Philippine musical game  show created, developed, produced  and distributed by ABS-CBN Entertainment.  The Philippine game  show aired from  June 5 to October 10, 2021  on Kapamilya Channel and A2Z,  replacing  the  third season of Your Face Sounds Familiar. The game show is  hosted by Vice Ganda. The game show features several community groups,  which consists of 25 individuals each, where  they are asked to complete a  line of a song  in order for them to receive cash prizes, and a  chance to share a winning  jackpot of   ₱500,000. The  production  of the game  show originally started in early-2020, and was set  to premiere on March 15, 2020  on ABS-CBN, replacing Gandang Gabi, Vice! however, Vice  Ganda, the host for the  said game show,  revealed  in Piling Lucky, a segment of It's Showtime, that the production of the game  show is  postponed due to the COVID-19 pandemic in  the   Philippines, and  that the  premiere of the game show on March 15 is  cancelled. Reruns of  Gandang Gabi, Vice!  temporarily filled in the  network's programming until the network's shutdown on May 5, 2020, due to the cease-and-desist order by the National Telecommunications Commission, after the network's legislative franchise have  lapsed.

Наsаn Аbdullауеv (Αzеrbаіjаnі: Ηəsən Əbdüllауеv; bоrn 2 Sерtеmbеr 1977) іs аn Αzеrbаіjаnі fооtbаllеr whо lаst рlауеd fоr Νеftсhі Βаku іn thе Αzеrbаіjаn Рrеmіеr Lеаguе.

Βоrn іn Ваku, Αzеrbаіjаn, Αbdullауеv stаrtеd hіs саrееr аt lосаl сlub Ваkılı РFΚ іn 1995. Аftеr twо sеаsоns wіth thе сlub, hе mоvеd tо Qаrаbаğ FΚ, whеrе hе wаs а rеgulаr іn thе fіrst tеаm fоr sіх sеаsоns. Іn 2001, hе rеturnеd tо Βаkılı РFК, аnd рlауеd fоr thе сlub fоr twо sеаsоns bеfоrе mоvіng tо Νеftсhі Βаku іn 2003. Не hаs bееn а rеgulаr іn thе Νеftсhі Βаku fіrst tеаm sіnсе thеn, аnd hаs wоn mаnу trорhіеs wіth thе сlub, іnсludіng thе Αzеrbаіjаn Ρrеmіеr Lеаguе аnd thе Αzеrbаіjаn Сuр.

Аbdullауеv hаs аlsо bееn а mеmbеr оf thе Αzеrbаіjаn nаtіоnаl tеаm, аnd hаs рlауеd іn sеvеrаl іntеrnаtіоnаl mаtсhеs. Ηе wаs а mеmbеr оf thе nаtіоnаl tеаm аt thе 2000 аnd 2008 СоnІFА Εurореаn Fооtbаll Сuр.

Ιn 2011, Αbdullауеv wаs аwаrdеd thе Аzеrbаіjаnі Fооtbаllеr оf thе Yеаr аwаrd.

We propose a novel superpixel-based multi-view convolutional neural network
for semantic image segmentation. 

The proposed network produces a high quality
segmentation of a single image by leveraging information from additional views
of the same scene. Particularly in indoor videos such as captured by robotic
platforms or handheld and bodyworn RGBD cameras, nearby video frames provide
diverse viewpoints and additional context of objects and scenes. To leverage
such information, we first compute region correspondences by optical flow and
image boundary-based superpixels. 

Given these region correspondences, we
propose a novel spatio-temporal pooling layer to aggregate information over
space and time. We evaluate our approach on the NYU--Depth--V2 and the SUN3D
datasets and compare it to various state-of-the-art single-view and multi-view
approaches. 

Besides a general improvement over the state-of-the-art, we also
show the benefits of making use of unlabeled frames during training for
multi-view as well as single-view prediction.

Brits are finally allowed to spend their own money on themselves this Christmas following the end of the government's Plan B restrictions. And it seems like they have rushed to make up for the lost time as sales increased faster than expected ahead of Christmas. 

The Office for National Statistics said that retail sales were up by 1.4% in November, with clothing and department stores performing particularly well. 

It is the biggest rise in sales since the easing of lockdown restrictions in April 2021, as spenders splurged on toys, jewellery and other items. 

insider.co.uk, UK - News

Analysts had expected a more modest rise of 0.5% following the ending of coronavirus restrictions in England. Meanwhile, separate figures from the British Retail Consortium (BRC) show that total sales for the month were up by 3.3% for the month. However, the BRC cautioned that retail was still facing challenges, as consumers watch their pennies amid the cost-of-living crisis and with Christmas spending hitting households. 

Sara Volchi, director of retail operations at John Lewis,  reportedly said that customers were "shopping slightly earlier" this year as a result of concerns over delivery delays. Many people have been buying items since the end of October, with popular gifts including Apple AirPods, Nintendo Switch consoles and GHD hair straighteners, she added. 

The pound slipped 0.38% against the euro on Tuesday to hit €1.1723, its lowest since December 2020. Against the dollar, the pound was down 0.14% at $1.3460, off yesterday's high of $1.3525.
