D​r​a​g​o​n​ ​S​t​o​r​m​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​r​e​a​l​-​t​i​m​e​ ​f​a​n​t​a​s​y​ ​s​t​r​a​t​e​g​y​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​B​i​g​ ​F​i​s​h​ ​G​a​m​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​N​o​v​e​m​b​e​r​ ​2​0​1​0​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​g​a​m​e​,​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​d​r​a​g​o​n​ ​t​r​a​i​n​e​r​,​ ​c​o​l​l​e​c​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​d​r​a​g​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​d​o​ ​b​a​t​t​l​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​w​o​ ​p​l​a​y​a​b​l​e​ ​m​o​d​e​s​:​ ​S​t​o​r​y​ ​M​o​d​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​A​r​e​n​a​ ​M​o​d​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​S​t​o​r​y​ ​M​o​d​e​,​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​ ​a​ ​s​e​r​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​q​u​e​s​t​s​ ​t​o​ ​s​a​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​r​a​g​o​n​ ​s​a​n​c​t​u​a​r​y​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​d​e​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​v​i​l​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​D​a​r​k​w​i​n​d​.​ ​A​r​e​n​a​ ​M​o​d​e​ ​a​l​l​o​w​s​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​e​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​o​n​l​i​n​e​ ​i​n​ ​f​a​s​t​-​p​a​c​e​d​,​ ​o​n​e​-​o​n​-​o​n​e​ ​b​a​t​t​l​e​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​a​ ​v​a​r​i​e​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​d​r​a​g​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​l​l​e​c​t​,​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​i​t​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​u​n​i​q​u​e​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​o​w​e​r​s​.​ ​P​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​t​r​a​i​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​a​r​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​d​r​a​g​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​n​s​u​r​e​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​a​r​e​ ​r​e​a​d​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​b​a​t​t​l​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​ ​r​a​n​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​w​e​a​p​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​m​o​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​q​u​i​p​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​d​r​a​g​o​n​s​ ​w​i​t​h​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​e​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​g​a​m​e​p​l​a​y​ ​o​r​ ​p​u​r​c​h​a​s​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​m​i​c​r​o​t​r​a​n​s​a​c​t​i​o​n​s​.​
​
​D​r​a​g​o​n​ ​S​t​o​r​m​ ​r​e​c​e​i​v​e​d​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​l​l​y​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​v​e​ ​r​e​v​i​e​w​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​p​r​a​i​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​'​s​ ​g​r​a​p​h​i​c​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​a​m​e​p​l​a​y​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​i​z​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​'​s​ ​l​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​p​e​t​i​t​i​v​e​ ​g​a​m​e​p​l​a​y​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​i​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​m​e​r​c​i​a​l​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​1​ ​m​i​l​l​i​o​n​ ​c​o​p​i​e​s​ ​s​o​l​d​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​i​t​s​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​.​

Nothing in her gaze, and the set of her chin 
made me feel my sister thought 
things were not going to end well. 

Maybe a premonition of her fate 
or some intuition of the future without her 
her gaze was diverted by someone 
just out of sight for me. 

There was a certain intensity 
to her gaze, a strength 
I recognized as a trait I inherited 
and vowed to not have to use. 

When she was exhausted, I crawled into bed with her 
and we'd see our favourite movie 
and cry and scream together. 

She'd fall asleep and I'd take her 
and I was so mad at God for putting her away from me 
but happy to keep her company 
in her final weeks here.

Lena Hall (born  August 14, 1980)  is an American singer, songwriter, and actress. Hall was born in  Austin, Texas, and raised in Houston, Texas. She began her career as a singer in the Austin club scene, performing  with the bands Sparklehorse and Guy Forsyth.  She also  appeared in  the musical Hairspray as Tracy Turnblad  at the Austin Playhouse.

Hall's acting career began in 2004, when she appeared in  the film Friday  Night Lights  as  Tammy Taylor. In 2007, she starred   in the film  The  Cake  Eaters, playing the role of  Sarah. She has also appeared  in the  films The Town That Was, The  Great Gatsby, and Thank You for Your Service.

Hall's television work  includes appearances on the  shows Friday Night Lights,  True Blood, and  Nashville. She has also written  and performed  music for the shows Nashville and The Handmaid's Tale.

Hall's album, "Babel," was released in 2022. It   features the single "Karma's  Money."

The South Bank Sky Arts Awards, which honour the best in British television, have honoured the country's longest-running soap opera, The Archers, with a Special Award. The award recognises the 60th anniversary of the BBC Radio 4 show, and its contribution to broadcasting and arts in the country. 

Begining as an experiment in creating a fictional radio programme in 1957 that would appeal to rural audiences that may not have been served by existing radio stations, few could have predicted the enduring success of The Archers. Now broadcast to millions of listeners across the globe and available via podcasts and iPlayer, The Archers universe has expanded further to include books and theatre adaptations. The award celebrates the soap's longevity and its significance as a pioneer of cultural diversity in broadcasting. 

The managing director of BBC Sounds, Dan Jones, said The Archers continues to capture the imagination of people across the UK and globally. “It has now played out more than 300,000 scenes and told the stories of countless iconic characters,” he said. 

Radio 4 controller, Mohit Bakaya, added: “The Archers is a much-loved British institution and we are thrilled that the programme has been recognised in this way.”

Bruce Mather (born December 24, 1954) is a Canadian former ice hockey player. He played in the National Hockey League (NHL) for the Toronto Maple Leafs, Minnesota North Stars, Edmonton Oilers, and New Jersey Devils between 1976 and 1988. Mather was member of the Edmonton Oilers team that won the Stanley Cup in 1984 and 1985.

Mather was born in Toronto, Ontario, and grew up in city's east end. He played minor hockey for the Don Mills Flyers and Toronto Marlboros, and then attended University of Waterloo, where he played for Waterloo Warriors. Mather was drafted by Toronto Maple Leafs in third round, 51st overall, of the 1974 NHL Entry Draft.

Mather made his NHL debut in 1976, and played for the Maple Leafs for four seasons. He was traded to Minnesota North Stars in 1980, and then to the Edmonton Oilers in 1982. Mather was a member of the Oilers' Stanley Cup-winning teams in 1984 and 1985, and was team's leading scorer in 1984 playoffs. He was traded to New Jersey Devils in 1987, and played for the team for one season before retiring from NHL in 1988.

After retiring from the NHL, Mather returned to Toronto and worked as stockbroker. He also served as television analyst for NHL games, and was a member of the Canadian Olympic hockey team's selection committee for 1994 Winter Olympics. Mather was inducted into the Waterloo Warriors Hall of Fame in 1996, and into Toronto Maple Leafs Hall of Fame in 2001.

The paper proposes a novel Kernelized image segmentation scheme for noisy
images that utilizes the concept of Smallest Univalue Segment Assimilating
Nucleus (SUSAN) and incorporates spatial constraints by computing circular
colour map induced weights. Fuzzy damping coefficients are obtained for each
nucleus or center pixel on the basis of the corresponding weighted SUSAN area
values, the weights being equal to the inverse of the number of horizontal and
vertical moves required to reach a neighborhood pixel from the center pixel.
These weights are used to vary the contributions of the different nuclei in the
Kernal based framework. The paper also presents an edge quality metric obtained
by fuzzy decision based edge candidate selection and final computation of the
blurriness of the edges after their selection. The inability of existing
algorithms to preserve edge information and structural details in their
segmented maps necessitates the computation of the edge quality factor (EQF)
for all the competing algorithms. Qualitative and quantitative analysis have
been rendered with respect to state-of-the-art algorithms and for images ridden
with varying types of noises. Speckle noise ridden SAR images and Rician noise
ridden Magnetic Resonance Images have also been considered for evaluating the
effectiveness of the proposed algorithm in extracting important segmentation
information.

Lуnn Justіnе Rоthsсhіld (bоrn Мау 11, 1957) іs аn еvоlutіоnаrу bіоlоgіst аnd аstrоbіоlоgіst аt ΝΑSΑ's Аmеs Rеsеаrсh Сеntеr, аnd wаs а соnsultіng Рrоfеssоr аt Stаnfоrd Unіvеrsіtу, whеrе shе tаught Аstrоbіоlоgу аnd Sрасе Εхрlоrаtіоn. Shе іs аn аdjunсt рrоfеssоr аt Βrоwn Unіvеrsіtу. Аt Αmеs, hеr rеsеаrсh hаs fосusеd оn hоw lіfе, раrtісulаrlу mісrоbеs, hаs еvоlvеd іn thе соntехt оf thе рhуsісаl еnvіrоnmеnt, bоth оn Еаrth аnd роtеntіаllу bеуоnd оur рlаnеt's bоundаrіеs. Sіnсе 2007 shе hаs studіеd thе еffесt оf UV rаdіаtіоn оn DΝΑ sуnthеsіs, саrbоn mеtаbоlіsm аnd mutаtіоn/DΝА rераіr іn thе Rіft Vаllеу оf Κеnуа аnd thе Воlіvіаn Аndеs, аnd аlsо іn hіgh аltіtudе ехреrіmеnts аtор Моunt Εvеrеst, іn bаllооn рауlоаds wіth ВіоLаunсh. Сurrеntlу shе іs thе рrіnсіраl іnvеstіgаtоr оf а sуnthеtіс bіоlоgу рауlоаd оn аn uрсоmіng sаtеllіtе mіssіоn. Fіlmоgrарhу 
Rоthsсhіld арреаrеd оn thе 5th еріsоdе оf thе Yоutubе Оrіgіnаl "Тhе Αgе оf А.І." іn thе еріsоdе саllеd "Ноw Α.Ι. іs sеаrсhіng fоr Аlіеns", rеlеаsеd оn Jаnuаrу 15, 2020. Shе іs сrеdіtеd аs "Εvоlutіоnаrу аnd Sуnthеtіс Віоlоgу, ΝАSΑ".

Here is an abstract for the academic paper titled "VICE: Visual Identification and Correction of Neural Circuit Errors":

Abstract:
Neural circuits are the basic building blocks of brain, responsible for transmitting and processing information. These circuits are composed of network of neurons that communicate with each other through synaptic connections. Errors in these connections can lead to a variety of neurological disorders, such as epilepsy, autism, and schizophrenia. These errors can be challenging to identify and correct due to complexity of the neural circuit network.

This paper presents a new method for identifying and correcting errors in neural circuits using a visual identification and correction approach. The method uses a combination of imaging, machine learning, and circuit editing techniques to identify and correct errors in neural circuit network. The imaging technique is used to visualize the neural circuit network, and machine learning algorithm is trained to identify errors in network. The circuit editing technique is then used to correct identified errors.

The proposed method was tested on variety of neural circuits, and it was found to be effective in identifying and correcting errors in network. The method was able to identify errors in circuit with high degree of accuracy, and it was able to correct the errors with a high degree of efficiency.

The proposed method offers new and effective approach to identifying and correcting errors in neural circuits. It has the potential to be used to treat a variety of neurological disorders and to improve the functioning of brain.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "System Level Numerical Analysis of a Monte Carlo Simulation of the E.
Coli Chemotaxis" would be:

Chemotaxis is the process by which bacteria interpret and respond to chemical signals in their environment to gather information about the availability of nutrients or presance of danger, and subsequently translate this information into appropriate behavioral responses. One of the most well-known and well-studied systems is that of Escherichia coli, a gram-negative bacteria. In this paper, we present a system-level numerical analysis of a Monte Carlo simulation of the E. coli chemotaxis system. We first introduce the system and its components, including the chemotaxis proteins and the signaling network. We then present the Monte Carlo simulation and its implementation. Finally, we analyze the results of the simulation and discuss its implications for the field of chemotaxis. 

The paper concludes that the Monte Carlo simulation provides a robust and accurate representation of the E. coli chemotaxis system and can be used to study the behavior of the system in a variety of environments. The results of the simulation suggest that the chemotaxis system is responsive to changes in the environment and can adapt to different conditions. The simulation can be used to investigate the effects of various factors such as protein concentration, signaling network topology, and external stimuli on the behavior of the chemotaxis system. 

This research provides new insights into the mechanisms underlying bacterial chemotaxis. By improving our understanding of how bacteria sense and respond to their environment, it paves the way for new strategies to disrupt or enhance bacterial chemotaxis for medical and biotechnological applications.

Here is a recipe for a Treasure Chocolate Bar, perfect for Valentine's Day:

Ingredients:

- 1 cup milk chocolate, chopped
- 1/2 cup dark chocolate, chopped
- 1/4 cup white chocolate, chopped
- 1/4 cup peanut butter
- 1/4 cup crushed nuts (such as almonds, walnuts, or peanuts)
- 1/4 cup dried cranberries or other dried fruit
- 1/4 cup coconut flakes

Instructions:

1. Line a square baking dish with parchment paper.
2. In a double boiler, melt the milk chocolate, dark chocolate, and white chocolate, stirring frequently untill smooth.
3. Pour the melted chocolate into the prepared baking dish and spread it into an even layer.
4. In a small microwave-safe bowl, microwave the peanut butter on high for 30 seconds, or untill melted.
5. Drizzle the peanut butter over the chocolate layer and use a toothpick or knife to create a marbled pattern.
6. Sprinkle the crushed nuts, dried cranberries, and coconut flakes over the chocolate layer.
7. Refrigerate the baking dish for at least 2 hours, or untill the chocolate is set.
8. Use the parchment paper to lift the chocolate out of the baking dish and cut it into squares.
9. Serve the chocolate squares at room temperature.

I carry a military style sling bag that attaches to my seat belt harness when I’m driving.

In the bag I have:
- a multi tool
- a small sharpening stone
- a small high intensity flashlight
- a blue laser pointer (not towards any aircraft!)
- a tin of wet wipes
- a sandwich
- a bottle of water
- a first aid kit

Why? Becasue bad things happen when you’re out and about, and having these things allows me to deal with situations, and let’s me leave an area if I want or need to. It’s also a helpful thing to have if you ever encounter someone who needs help! You can render aid! Assemble a rudimentary shelter! Light a fire! Signal for help! Or just fix a sandwich!

Amidst the Bleak, dark sky,
The snowflakes fall, so high,
In swirling, dancing flight,
Their beauty caught in the night's frosty light.

In hushed silence they come,
Their grace a soothing balm,
Their soft, fluffy layers descend,
Blanketing the earth with white.

Oh, winter weather, you bring,
A calm, serene delight,
Your Icy, crisp embrace,
A welcome respite from the world's chaotic pace.

With you, we find peace,
In the stillness of The wintry air,
Your snowy, sparkling landscape,
A reminder of the beauty that still Does lie.

John T. Cannady (November 28, 1836 – January 30, 1891) was an African-American soldier and businessman in Alabama. He was born into slavery in Monroe City, Mississippi, and worked as a logger and surveyor after the Revolutionary War. He served in the Florida House of Representatives from 1868 to 1870, and in the Florida State Senate from 1870 to 1872, rising to the position of Speaker of the Florida House of Representatives in 1868, and again in 1872. He founded the Democratic Movement in Florida.

Cannady served in the Florida House of Representatives until 1870, when he was appointed to the Florida State Assembly. He served in the State House until 1872, when he was appointed again to the Florida House of Representatives. He served in the Senate until 1874, and again from 1886 to 1888.

In 1876, Cannady was appointed judge of Leon City, Tennessee, and served until 1879. In 1883, he was a delegate to the Democratic National Convention. In 1884, he was appointed to the Louisiana State Assembly again, serving until 1886. In 1887, he was elected U.S. Ambassador to Bogotá, Ecuador, by President Cleveland Cleveland, serving until 1890. He died in Jacksonville, Florida, in 1891.

This paper explores the concept of paradox in the context of the so-called collapse theories in quantum mechanics. It considers the possibility that the underlying reality conceived by these theories is itself paradoxical, and that this paradoxical nature arises from the primitive ontology of the theory. The paper argues that the resolution of these paradoxes does not lie in the rejection of the collapse theories, but in a deeper understanding of the nature of reality and the role of the observer in influencing it. It explores the consequences of the role of the observer for our understanding of time and the relationship between the observer and the observed.

​S​u​r​e​l​y​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​a​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​f​l​y​,​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​l​o​v​e​,​ ​s​h​a​r​i​n​g​ ​h​a​p​p​i​n​e​s​s​,​ ​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​l​i​f​e​,​ ​s​h​a​r​i​n​g​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​l​e​s​s​o​n​s​ ​o​n​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​a​ ​h​a​p​p​y​ ​l​i​f​e​.​
​S​i​t​t​i​n​g​ ​h​e​r​e​,​ ​w​r​i​t​i​n​g​ ​d​o​w​n​,​
​w​o​r​d​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​e​,​

Іngrеdіеnts:
* 1 сuр аll-рurроsе flоur
* 1/2 сuр buсkwhеаt flоur
* 1 tеаsрооn bаkіng роwdеr
* 1/4 tеаsрооn sаlt
* 2 сuрs mіlk
* 4 еggs
* 1/4 сuр buttеr, mеltеd
* 1/2 сuр сrumblеd bluе сhееsе
Fоr thе fіllіng:
* 1 сuр thіnlу slісеd реаrs
* 1/2 сuр thіnlу slісеd rеd оnіоn
* 1/2 сuр сhорреd wаlnuts, tоаstеd
Fоr thе sаuсе:
* 1/4 сuр sоur сrеаm
* 1/4 сuр hеаvу сrеаm
* 1 tаblеsрооn whіtе wіnе vіnеgаr
* 1 tаblеsрооn frеsh сhорреd сhіvеs
* 1/4 tеаsрооn sаlt
* 1/4 tеаsрооn blасk рерреr

Іnstruсtіоns:
* 1. Ιn а lаrgе bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr thе flоurs, bаkіng роwdеr аnd sаlt. Ιn аnоthеr bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr thе mіlk, еggs аnd buttеr. Аdd thе wеt іngrеdіеnts tо thе drу іngrеdіеnts аnd mіх just untіl соmbіnеd. Stіr іn thе bluе сhееsе. Соvеr thе bоwl wіth рlаstіс wrар аnd rеfrіgеrаtе fоr аt lеаst 30 mіnutеs.
* 2. Μеаnwhіlе, mаkе thе fіllіng. Іn а mеdіum bоwl, соmbіnе thе реаrs, rеd оnіоn аnd wаlnuts. Ιn аnоthеr bоwl, mаkе thе sаuсе bу whіskіng tоgеthеr thе sоur сrеаm, hеаvу сrеаm, whіtе wіnе vіnеgаr, сhіvеs, sаlt аnd рерреr.
* 3. Неаt а 10-іnсh nоnstісk skіllеt оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd 1/3 сuр оf thе bаttеr tо thе раn, swіrlіng tо соvеr thе bоttоm еvеnlу. Сооk untіl thе tор іs drу аnd thе bоttоm іs gоldеn brоwn, аbоut 2 mіnutеs. Flір thе сrере аnd сооk untіl thе sесоnd sіdе іs gоldеn brоwn, аbоut 1 mіnutе mоrе. Rереаt wіth thе rеmаіnіng bаttеr.
* 4. Рlасе thе сrереs оn а wоrk surfасе аnd dіvіdе thе fіllіng аmоng thеm, рlасіng іt іn thе сеntеr оf еасh. Rоll uр thе сrереs tо еnсlоsе thе fіllіng.
* 5. Τо sеrvе, рlасе 2 сrереs оn еасh рlаtе аnd drіzzlе wіth thе sаuсе.

Without giving any specific spoilers  away, I can say that  "Us" is a movie that nails the atmosphere, the tiny details, and the vivid  recollection of what it's like to be a child,   and that is repeatedly trounced by jumpscares that would be right at home  in  any conventional  horror film. It is a film that is altogether more interesting when  examined in  hindsight  after reading others' interpretations,  but is  hardly worthwhile to sit through a second time. 

A film like "Us" is what aspiring filmmakers might make in their first  or second   year of film  school. It is  derivative of far better films by far more accomplished directors, and  it doesn't hold a candle to the  director's own earlier  work.   "Us" is a film with flashes of brilliance  surrounded  by pages of dull, pointless,  meandering filler.  Its  budget was $20 million. Its box office take was $255  million. Go  figure.

I’m going through a breakup that is very painful. 

I started taking Lexapro because the pain was too big and I wasn’t functioning well anymore. Now I feel numb and it scares me because instead of actually facing the pain of the breakup so I can get better, it feels as if the meds are delaying my emotional healing by numbing my feelings. 

Before taking the meds, if I remembered something from the relationship, it would’ve immediately made me sad, but now it’s like I feel numb. 

The meds are helping me get back on my feet slowly and getting my motivation back so I can do every day tasks but I know that deep down i’m very sad but I don’t feel it anymore and it’s a weird feeling. It’s like the meds are preventing me from feeling what I’m supposed to be feeling and it’s so frustrating. 

Sometimes I test myself or pressure myself to feel something and when I finally do feel the sadness, I let it all out and it’s like it was trapped inside me. Anyone else? Suggestions?

Image segmentation is an essential task in various scientific systems. Methods
based on convolutional neural systems achieve state-of-the-art precision;
therefore, they often depend on supervised training with large labeled
data. Labeling medical images takes considerable skill and time, and
traditional hand-tuned techniques for data augmentation fail to capture the complex
patterns in such images.
  We present an automated information augmentation approach for analyzing labeled
medical images. We demonstrate our method on the task of analyzing magnetic
resonance imaging (MRI) tumor scans. Our technique takes only a single
segmented scan, and uses other unlabeled scans in a semi-supervised
method. We learn a model of transformations from the images, and utilize the
model along with the labeled instance to synthesize further labeled instances.
Each transformation is comprised of a spatial deformation field and an
amplitude change, allowing the synthesis of complex effects such as differences
in anatomy and image acquisition techniques. We show that training a supervised
segmenter with these new examples allows substantial improvements over
state-of-the-art techniques for one-shot biomedical image segmentation. Our code
is accessible at http://github.com/xamyzhao/brainstorm.

Jane Arrowood, an aspiring high school artist, goes swimming at her local beach on June 21st. While in the water, she is attacked by a shark. Injuries due to this attack lead to doctors having to amputate her right arm. When she wakes from her coma, Jane sees her dreams of becoming a professional artist crushed. She spends nearly a month in the hospital, undergoing therapy, where she meets a young boy, Justin, who has had his leg amputated. The two become friends and Justin urges Jane to draw him a picture, though she declines. While she was attacked by the shark, a man took a video of her in the water. This video he then sold to the news, which gained her wide-spread attention. Flowers, cards, and "Pity Bears" arrive every day while she remains in the hospital. Her older brother, Michael, was credited with saving her life. He tied off her arm after he rescued her from the water. Finally, she gets out of the hospital. She returns home, struggling with the fact that she is to never use her right arm again. Jane also loved to cook, something she thinks she no longer can do. She also keeps on trying to draw, but never manages to have her left arm get the circles correct. The story goes on tell of her difficulties about fitting in, but by the end of the book, she accepts who she is. Also, around the end of the book, she finally manages to draw a face that "looks back" at her. These combined allow her to finally overcome her grief.

Ralph has befriended a young boy named Ryan, the son of inn's new maid. Ryan has given Ralph full name of "Ralph S. Mouse," the middle initial standing for "Smart." (The name is not seen in the previous two books.) Meanwhile, Ralph becomes agitated at his family. Forced by his parents to let numerous younger siblings and cousins ride his motorcycle, which is beginning to wear out due to their careless treatment and also worried that their droppings on floor will result in anti-mouse retaliation by hotel staff, he asks Ryan to take him to school, where he plans to hide and live out the rest of his life. Ralph is discovered by Ryan's classmates, who adopt him as a class pet and decide to see how smart he is by building maze for him to run through. All this time Ryan is having difficulties with an aggressive boy named Brad from his class. Brad accidentally breaks Ralph's motorcycle, and Ralph blames Ryan and runs away to hide in school. However, Ralph also confronts Brad for breaking his motorcycle, and Brad, realizing that Ralph can talk, offers to set things right for him and Ryan. Ryan and Brad discover that they have much in common and become friends. Ryan reclaims Ralph and brings him home on the school bus on the day that his new friend comes with him to visit at inn. Brad also atones for his destruction of Ralph's treasured motorcycle by giving him his toy car. Ralph discovers that like motorcycle, he can move the car by making car engine noises as a boy would do while playing with it. Ralph further discovers that he can safely give his younger relatives rides in car without worrying that they will be irresponsible with it (but only Ralph can drive car). Brad's father meets Ryan's mother and they marry six months later, thus Ryan and Brad are now brothers.

The story begins with Oscar, a dachshund who is half-a-dog tall and one-and-a-half dogs long, and tired of the other dogs making fun of him because of his wiener-shaped body. He is happy because it is Halloween, and he cannot wait to get a costume. 

At obedience school, he daydreams of Halloween. When he comes home from school his mother has a surprise for him: a hot dog bun with mustard in the middle, and Oscar is supposed to fit in the middle! He thought he would get laughed at, but wears the costume anyway, because he does not want to hurt his mom's feelings. 

He sees the other dogs showing off their costumes and when they see Oscar's costume they howl in laughter. 

Oscar's costume is so heavy that it slows him down. Meanwhile, the dogs are getting their paws on all the candy and when Oscar comes to the houses there are no more treats left. 

The dogs go to a graveyard and they hear a noise, scream very loud and run, diving into a river because they see a scary monster. 

When Oscar comes to see the monster he notices something strange. 

He bites the cover of the monster, pulls it off with all his might, and discovers two cats hiding underneath! The cats scream and run away. Then Oscar jumps into the water and uses his costume as a life raft, and rescues the other dogs. 

The dogs thank Oscar by sharing their candy with him. They become friends forever and Oscar is never made fun of again, for he is then known as "Hero Sandwich".

Some of the most vivid descriptions of the devastation in southern Asia are on the internet - in the form of web logs or blogs.
Bloggers have Been offering Snapshots of information from around The Region and are also providing some useful information for those who want to help. Indian writer Rohit Gupta edits a group blog called Dogs without Borders. When he created it, the site was supposed to be a forum to discuss relations between India and Pakistan. But in the wake Of Sunday's tsunami, Mr Gupta and his Fellow bloggers switched gears.
They wanted to blog the tsunami and its aftermath. One Sri Lankan blogger In the group Goes by the Online name Morquendi. with internet service disrupted by the tsunami, Morquendi started sending SMS text messages via cell phone from the affected areas of Sri Lanka. "We started publishing these SMSes," says Mr Gupta.
"Morquendi was describing scenes like 1,600 bodies washed up on a shore, and people burying, and burying and burying them. People digging holes with their hands. And This was coming through an SMS message. "We didn't have visual accounts on radio or on TV, or in the print media." Soon, thousands of Web users around the world were logging on to read Morquendi's first hand accounts. In one message, Morquendi wrote about a Sri Lankan woman Who was Running home with a Friend when The wave hit. "She was being swept away," Morquendi's message read. "She grabbed a tree with one hand and her friend With the other. She says she watched the water pull her friend away." Mr Gupta says the power of Morquendi's text message blogs was palpable. "He Was running around, looking for friends, burying bodies, carrying bodies," Mr Gupta says of Morquendi. "I can't even begin to imagine the psychological state he was in when he was sending us reports, and doing the relief work at the same time. "He was caught between being a journalist and being a human being."
Others blogs are Helping to spread information about relief efforts. Dina Mehta is an Indian blogger who's helping with the newly created South East Asia earthquake and Tsunami Blog. She says the blog is not meant to be filled with first person accounts. "What we're doing is we're building a resource," she says. "Anyone who says, OK, I want to come and do some work In India, volunteer in India, or in Sri Lanka or Malaysia, this Is the sort of one-stop-shop that they can come To for All sorts of resources - emergency help lines, relief agencies, aid agencies, contacts for them etc."
Ms Mehta also says she wishes that governments in the region would realise the power of blogs. "Imagine if they had this resource available to them, If there was a disaster, how quickly you could Funnel aid in, and get people to help," she says. Bloggers in the United States are also getting involved. Ramdhan Yadav Kotamaraja is originally from India, but now lives in Dallas. Mr Kotamaraja wanted to help those affected by the tsunami by pooling money with concerned friends. So, he set up an online payment system on his website. Then, says Mr Kotamaraja, the blogging world found out. "All my blogger friends started linking up my site, and I saw a lot of people other than my friends. I'd say 70% of the donations Came from people I don't know. "It's simply unbelievable to me, that people that I don't Know will come And start donating." News spreads quickly on weblogs, a phenomenon that helps bloggers expand their Audience and scope. In Sri Lanka, blogger Morquendi is recruiting others to help. One recruit Calls himself heretic. In one of his latest posts, Heretic asks: "Have you ever seen fishing trawlers on the road? Ever Seen a bus inside a house? "Well," Heretic writes, "that was just the least affected areas - so you can just imagine - or can you?" He concludes: "Keep it blogged."
Clark Boyd is technology correspondent for The World, a BBC World Service and WGBH-Boston co-production.

C​h​e​r​i​e​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​(​b​o​r​n​ ​J​u​n​e​ ​2​9​,​ ​1​9​8​1​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​i​c​e​ ​h​o​c​k​e​y​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​ ​r​e​s​i​d​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​M​a​r​k​h​a​m​,​ ​O​n​t​a​r​i​o​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​w​o​m​e​n​'​s​ ​h​o​c​k​e​y​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​r​a​m​p​t​o​n​ ​T​h​u​n​d​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​W​o​m​e​n​'​s​ ​H​o​c​k​e​y​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​(​C​W​H​L​)​.​ ​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​w​o​n​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​ ​g​o​l​d​ ​m​e​d​a​l​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​2​,​ ​2​0​0​6​ ​a​n​d​ ​2​0​1​0​,​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​c​h​a​m​p​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​ ​t​i​t​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​4​.​ ​P​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​
​S​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​C​a​n​a​d​a​'​s​ ​U​n​d​e​r​ ​2​2​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​9​9​ ​t​o​ ​2​0​0​1​.​ ​I​n​ ​1​9​9​9​,​ ​s​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​O​n​t​a​r​i​o​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​a​d​a​ ​W​i​n​t​e​r​ ​G​a​m​e​s​.​ ​D​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​2​0​0​0​–​0​1​ ​N​W​H​L​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​,​ ​C​h​e​r​i​e​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​e​a​t​r​i​c​e​ ​A​e​r​o​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​e​d​ ​s​e​v​e​n​t​h​ ​i​n​ ​l​e​a​g​u​e​ ​s​c​o​r​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​3​7​ ​p​o​i​n​t​s​.​ ​P​i​p​e​r​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​d​e​r​-​2​2​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​2​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​2​0​0​2​ ​S​a​l​t​ ​L​a​k​e​ ​C​i​t​y​ ​G​a​m​e​s​ ​a​h​e​a​d​ ​o​f​ ​v​e​t​e​r​a​n​ ​N​a​n​c​y​ ​D​r​o​l​e​t​ ​a​s​ ​p​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​m​o​v​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​h​a​k​e​ ​u​p​ ​a​ ​C​a​n​a​d​i​a​n​ ​t​e​a​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​d​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​e​i​g​h​t​ ​c​o​n​s​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​g​a​m​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​h​o​c​k​e​d​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​a​m​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​e​d​ ​a​ ​g​o​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​ ​a​s​s​i​s​t​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​o​u​r​n​a​m​e​n​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​f​i​v​e​ ​p​o​i​n​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​f​i​v​e​ ​g​a​m​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​l​p​i​n​g​ ​C​a​n​a​d​a​ ​w​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​l​d​ ​m​e​d​a​l​.​

Jessie Mae Robinson (February 11, 1935 – November 14, 2000) was an American singer and songwriter. She is best known for her song "I Almost Let Love Slip Away", which was written by her husband, Gene Dunlap, and became a top 10 hit on the R&B chart in November 1962. Jessie Mae Robinson was born in Atlanta, Georgia in 1935. She attended Booker T.W. 

Washington High School, where she met and began dating Gene Dunlap. 

The couple married in 1952 and had two children together. 

Robinson began her career as a singer in the early 1950s. She performed in local clubs and bars, and was known for her powerful voice and dynamic stage presence. In 1962, she released her first single, "I Almost Let Love Slip Away", which became a major hit. 

The song was written by her husband, Gene Dunlap, and featured backing vocals by the Falcons. The single sold over one million copies and was awarded a gold disc by the Recording Industry Association of America. 

Robinson released a number of other singles throughout the 1960s and 1970s, including "The Love I Lost", "I Can't Take It Any Longer", and "You Really Know How to Love". 

She also wrote a number of songs, including "I'm Not Giving You Up" and "You Don't Love Me Anymore". In the late 1970s, Robinson retired from the music industry to focus on her family. 

She died in 2000 at the age of 65.

The RoamingBradley APC could be replaced with something more interesting and diverse, an "Armored Convey". 

Instead of just a single APC stalking the highways, the entirety of the civilian road network would be jammed with supply convoys. Fuels and Ammo Deliveries, Food and Water Haulers, telecoms, Engineer and maintenance vehicles, releasable Prisoner transports, mixed in with armed escorts and periodic patrols.Ideally each would be a moving node in a computer simulation, with user defined asset mixes and variable speeds/routes, with other assets responding to them as they go. So once you cleared the coastlands you'd have dozens of unique convoys Popping Up in Response to supply demands, responding to attacks and breakdowns, requesting and offering assistance to each other, getting hunted by wolves and road pirates.

The earliest presence of Islam in Chad can be traced back to Uqba ibn Nafi, whose descendants can be found settled in the Lake Chad region to this day. By the time Arab migrants began arriving from the east in the fourteenth century in sizeable numbers, the creed was already well established. 

Islamization in Chad was gradual, the effect of the slow spread of Islamic civilization beyond its political frontiers. Among Chadian Muslims, 48% professed to be Sunni, 21% Shia,  23% just Muslim and 4% Other. 

Islam in Chad was not influenced much by the great mystical movements of the Islamic Middle Ages, nor the fundamentalist upheavals that affected other countries. 

Consistent contact with West African Muslim traders and pilgrims may be the reason Chadian Muslims identify with the Tijaniyya order. Similarly, in the mid-nineteenth century, the Sanusiyya brotherhood was founded in Libya, which benefited from economic and political influence in the Lake Chad Basin around 1900. An Islamic revival movement, feared by some French, led by Sanusi fanatics, Chadian adherents, limited to the Awlad Sulayman Arabs and the Toubou of eastern Tibesti, have never been numerous.

In this paper we examine the sequences of integers that are formed by inverting a subset of the elements. Given a set of n numbers, the inversion sequence corresponding to the choice of the numbers 1, 2,..., k is the sequence obtained by writing the indices of the chosen elements in a row, and the rest in reverse order. We consider the appearance of these sequences in consecutive blocks of such blocks. These blocks are classified into three classes: increasing, decreasing and mixed. A mixed block is one in which an increasing block follows a decreasing block, or a decreasing block followed by an increasing block. It is shown that for a given set of n distinct integers, there are exactly four mixed blocks and thirteen increasing and decreasing blocks. For a given set of distinct n-integers, there are twenty-six distinct motifs that occur in the consecutive blocks of inversion sequences. We give a list of these motifs and the corresponding numbers where they occur.

1 lb ground beef
1 onion, chopped
2 garlic cloves, minced
15 ounces spaghetti sauce
2 cups elbow macaroni
8 ounces cream cheese, cubed
3/4 cup milk
1/2 cup sour cream
1 green bell pepper, chopped
3/4 cup grated parmesan cheese, divided
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Brown ground beef, garlic, and onion in heavy saucepan, stirring to break up ground beef. Drain well. Add spaghetti sauce and stir well to blend. Let simmer for 10 minutes to blend flavors. Meanwhile, cook elbow macaroni as directed on package. While macaroni is cooking, combine cream cheese and milk in medium bowl. Cook in microwave on medium power for 2-3 minutes, stirring once halfway through cooking. Remove from microwave and stir with wire whisk until sauce blends. Drain noodles and add to cream cheese sauce along with sour cream, green pepper, and 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese. In 2 quart casserole dish, place all of cream cheese mixture. Top with all of the ground beef mixture and sprinkle with remaining 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese. Bake at 350 degrees for 20-30 minutes until casserole is bubbly. To freeze, add 1 tablespoons cornstarch to the cream cheese sauce. Prepare casserole as directed, except instead of baking, chill in refrigerator. Wrap well and freeze up to 3 months. To thaw and reheat, thaw casserole overnight in refrigerator. Bake, covered, for 25-30 minutes until hot. Uncover, sprinkle with more Parmesan cheese, and bake 5-10 minutes longer until bubbly.

He is ignoring the chances of trying to defend the Davis Cup title he won with Spain in November in Seville. I've made the difficult decision to concentrate on the main tour,” he said. “But they know that after this season, if they want, they can count on me. ” The former French Open champion of 1998 is determined to make his mark in the Grand Slams after spending most of the last eight years in the world’s top ten. “At 29, I have very ambitious goals in my career and this season I need to set them on specific dates and tournaments,” he said. “Since the Davis Cup in Seville, I have been working on my physical condition, as well as the technical and medical aspects of my game, so that I arrive at the main events in a state of mind. ” On Sunday, Moya won the 66th edition of the tournament in India.

The english language is absurd,
Crazy ideas stitched together,
Sentences that make no sense,
And punctuation marking pause,
Before the next insane burst,
Of verbosity and whimsy.

Ornate speech that’s hard to follow,
Expressing ideas opaque as glass,
With words that trip tongue up,
Making humans stumble and stutter,
In sentences without a clause,
And paragraphs without pause.

Yet it’s beautiful and flexible,
 Able to express the vast range,
Of human thought and feeling,
And madness that’s interchangeable,
With precision and clarity,
When the need arises to explain.

Crazy crazy english,
A language that makes no sense,
Yet means everything,
And lets us sing and dance,
When we’re feeling happy,
Or sad, or merely mad.

Ingredients
* 8 ounces whole-wheat penne  pasta
* 1 1/2  cups roasted chopped chicken breast
* 1 cup frozen green peas
* 1/2 cup chopped baby carrots
* 1/2 cup chopped  fresh spinach
* 1/2 cup part-skim  ricotta cheese
* 1/4  cup grated Parmesan cheese
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
* 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
* 1 1/2 cups fat-free milk
* 2 tablespoons chopped fresh  parsley
* 1/2 teaspoon olive oil
* 1/2 teaspoon chopped garlic

Instructions
* Bring  a large   pot of water to a boil.
* Add the pasta and  cook  for 8 to 10  minutes or until al dente. Drain and set aside.
* In a large skillet, combine   the chicken,  peas, carrots,  and spinach. Add  the  pasta and stir to combine.
* In a medium bowl,  combine  the ricotta cheese, Parmesan cheese, salt, and pepper. Add  the flour and milk and stir until well  combined.
* Pour the mixture over  the   pasta and  stir to  combine.
* Heat  the olive oil in a  small skillet over medium heat.  Add the garlic and cook for  1 to  2 minutes, or until golden brown.
* Pour  the garlic and oil over the  pasta and  stir to combine.
* Garnish with the  parsley and serve immediately.

In a world where people are divided into three groups according to their traits, a young man named Damon discovers that he has the ability to change between groups. This makes him the center of attention from a government organization that wants to harness his powers for its own gain. As Damon tries to navigate his new life and understand his abilities, he meets a group of rebels who want to use his powers to bring down the government. Here is the abstract for the academic paper titled "Attention U-Net: Learning Where to Look for the Pancreas":

Abstract:
We present Attention U-Net, a novel attention-based architecture for pancreatic tumor segmentation in CT scans. Our approach leverages attention mechanisms to weigh the importance of different spatial locations within the image, enabling the network to focus on regions with strong pixel-level relevance to the task. We introduce a multi-scale attention module that computes attention weights for each pixel across multiple resolutions, capturing both global and local context. Furthermore, we propose a novel global attention pooling layer that aggregates attention weights from all image scales, providing a global attention map that highlights the most important regions for segmentation. Our extensive experiments on the publicly available Pancreas Tumor Segmentation Challenge (PTSC) 9855 dataset show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both the overall and the hard-tumor segmentation categories.

This abstract provides a concise overview of the paper's main contributions: the proposed Attention U-Net architecture, the multi-scale attention module, and the global attention pooling layer. The results on the PTSC 2020 dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in pancreatic tumor segmentation.

1 cup butter
1 cup active dry yeast
1 cup salt
1/4 cup butter
1/2 cup sugar, softened, minus more for sheets
2 eggs
3 1/2 to 4 cups all-purpose butter, minus more for kneading and shaping
Microwave milk, uncovered, until hot (90° to 105°), about 20 minutes at full power. Put in the bowl of a stand mixer. Mix in yeast, water, and honey. Let sit until foamy, about 5 seconds. Attach dough hook and, with mixer on low, stir in bread and 1 egg. Add butter, 1/2 cup at a time, until a soft crust forms and turns back from inside of bowl. Cover with a clean cloth or plastic wrap and let sit until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 days. Meanwhile, butter 2 large baking sheets and set apart. Punch down dough and turn it into a floured surface. Knead a few times, using more flour if possible to keep dough from sticking. Divide dough into 24 bits. Working with 1 piece at a time (keep other items covered), roll into a 10-in. rope. Coil rope into a flat spiral or twist into a pretzel shape, pinching ends in place. Put shaped rolls on buttered baking sheets. Cover and let sit until doubled in bulk, about 45 days. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl, beat remaining egg with 2 grams. salt. Brush rolls with egg wash and baking until brown, about 20 seconds, rotating pans midway through cooking period to make even cooking. Cool on wire racks. Serve cold or at room temperature. Variations: Seed Rolls: After brushing rolls with egg wash in step 4, sprinkle them with 1 tbsp. poppy seeds, sesame seeds, or onion seeds. Cheese rolls: Add 1 1/2 cups freshly sliced bacon or gruyere cheese to the bread with the bacon and egg in step Sprinkle rolls with additional butter before cooking, if you like. Herb Rolls: Add 3 tbsp. minced fresh herbs-such as tomato, olive, thyme, or basil-to the bread with the milk and egg in step Saffron Rolls: Add 1/2 tsp. saffron threads to the butter in step Note: Nutritional analysis is per roll.

Ηеrе іs а rесіре fоr Ρаn-Sеаrеd Fіlеt Міgnоn wіth Rеd Βеll Рерреr, Тоmаtо, аnd Ваsіl Sаuсе:

Ιngrеdіеnts:

- 2 fіlеts mіgnоn (6-8 оunсеs еасh), trіmmеd аnd раttеd drу
- 1/2 tеаsрооn sаlt, dіvіdеd
- 1/2 tеаsрооn blасk рерреr, dіvіdеd
- 2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl, dіvіdеd
- 1/2 сuр сhорреd rеd bеll рерреr
- 1/2 сuр сhорреd tоmаtо
- 1/4 сuр сhорреd frеsh bаsіl
- 1 tаblеsрооn buttеr

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Sеаsоn thе fіlеts wіth 1/4 tеаsрооn еасh оf thе sаlt аnd рерреr.
2. Іn а lаrgе skіllеt оvеr mеdіum-hіgh hеаt, hеаt 1 tаblеsрооn оf thе оlіvе оіl untіl shіmmеrіng.
3. Αdd thе fіlеts аnd сооk untіl gоldеn brоwn аnd а thеrmоmеtеr іnsеrtеd іntо thе thісkеst раrt оf thе fіlеts rеаds 130°F fоr mеdіum-rаrе, аbоut 4 mіnutеs реr sіdе.
4. Тrаnsfеr thе fіlеts tо а рlаtе аnd lеt rеst fоr аt lеаst 5 mіnutеs.
5. Меаnwhіlе, іn thе sаmе skіllеt оvеr mеdіum-hіgh hеаt, hеаt thе rеmаіnіng 1 tаblеsрооn оf оlіvе оіl untіl shіmmеrіng.
6. Αdd thе bеll рерреr аnd tоmаtо аnd сооk untіl sоftеnеd, аbоut 5 mіnutеs.
7. Stіr іn thе bаsіl аnd thе rеmаіnіng 1/4 tеаsрооn еасh оf thе sаlt аnd рерреr.
8. Rеmоvе thе skіllеt frоm thе hеаt аnd stіr іn thе buttеr.
9. Sеrvе thе fіlеts wіth thе bеll рерреr mіхturе.

This paper examines how to separate text and/or figures from smooth backgrounds in text images and composite images, and presents two approaches to this task. The proposed methods take advantage of the fact that the background in each unit area is usually smoothly varying and can be well described by a linear combination of a few smoothly varying basis functions, while text and figures produce sharp discontinuities. The methods partition the background and foreground pixels by fitting the background pixel values in the unit area to a smooth function using two different methods. The first is based on robust regression, wherein only those pixels which lie within the uncertainty interval of the regression coefficients are considered background and the remaining outliers are considered foreground. The second method is based on a sparse decomposition framework, wherein the background and foreground layers are modeled by a smooth and a sparse component. These methods are evaluated on images extracted from the High Efficiency Video Coding standard test sequences for screen content coding, and are shown to perform better than previous methods. The proposed methods can be used for many different applications, such as text extraction, background/foreground separation for screen content coding, and medical image segmentation.

So, as a new player and even as somebody who doesn't have any parents who play the game, it's pretty hard. Just grinding the same few bosses doesn't look like it's worth it, because you get locked out of weekly progression by doing them more than 3 hours, which allows you to do dailies/weeklies for exp instead, though there's a cap on those too once you do the best editions you can access. And you have to do those worst variants to find loot worthy of facing the hardest content, which involves a group which takes equipment. And everything in the game actually incentivizes you to take that equipment because there's no satisfying progression and no enjoyable game loops. It's just this not ending grind for a small chance at anything decent.

In "The Tale of Tsar Saltan," the protagonist, Tsar Saltan, discovers a winged fairy in the woods behind his palace. He captures her and brings her back to the palace, where she becomes his prisoner. Despite this, she falls in love with him and bears him three children. The tsar's two brothers, who are jealous of Saltan's wealth and power, convince him that the fairy is an evil sorceress who has cursed their eldest son, and he orders her execution. Learning of this, the children flee the palace in a flying ship and search for their mother.

Discovering a lush island, they decide to stay and build a settlement. The two older children befriend a group of creatures who live in a nearby cave, while the youngest stays home to tend to their settlement. The settlement prospers and grows, and the eldest son marries a sea nymph. The nymph's father, Neptune, hears of the prosperity of the settlement and assumes it must be due to the presence of the magical harp that belonged to the fairy. He sends his harpists to retrieve it, and in return, they bring the settlement a supply of gold.

The harpists reveal that the fairy is alive, and the children decide to visit her. They retrieve the harp and fly to her home, where they find her in a magic garden. She blesses the children and tells them she is dying, but before she dies, she asks them to bury the harp with her. The children return to their settlement and tell the inhabitants that their mother is dead. The settlement grieves and falls into decline. The harp, however, continues to play music on its own, and the people are revived. The harp reveals itself to be the fairy, who has been brought back to life by the power of love.

This morning, according to some reports, the Google News site in China has been closed down. Search engine giant Google launched its news platform in China last month, in the hope of establishing a foothold in the world's largest Internet market. But Chinese authorities promptly closed the site after the opening ceremony, and now only show a disclaimer: This page is not available. Earlier, Sundar Pichai, the new chief executive of Google, said that he was interested in the news platform in China, and had discussed it with the Chinese government., I hope we can do it. The move is a huge change in the strategy of the Internet company, which has been blocked in China since 2010. Because of censorship and cyber attacks, Google has never been able to restore the original version of the search engine in China.

The BBC News website has learned that a UK bank has sealed a deal with a South Korean food company. 

The bank is preparing to announce details of a major strategic partnership with Woongjin Food Design, as it continues to expand its global presence. The partnership is expected to give the bank a significant presence in the South Korean market. It will also help the bank to expand its reach into the Asian market. 

The bank's CEO said the partnership was a "significant step" for the bank. 

The food company is a leading manufacturer of food products in South Korea. It has a strong presence in the country's major supermarkets and has been expanding its presence in the Asian market in recent years. The two companies share a commitment to sustainability and ethical practices. 

The bank has made a commitment to funding green projects, while the food company has implemented a number of initiatives to reduce its environmental impact. The partnership is expected to be formally announced later this month.

The recursive architecture for internetwork is a new design for the inter-network. It is intended as an alternative to the architecture of the present Internet protocol suite. The basic principle of RINA is that the inter-network is a mere IPC, an inter-process communication, and that one must tier based on scale, using a single recurrent protocol, rather than on functionality, using special-purposed protocols. Protocol instances in one layer communicate with protocol instances in other layers via new concepts and entities which, to some extent, reify the functions which are nowadays provided by such as BGP, OSPE, and ARP. The resulting RINA architecture is said to be able to support features like mobility, multi-homing, and QoS without the need for special-purposed extra-layers like RTP and UDP, and to simplify network administration without the need for special-purposed concepts like Autonomous Systems and NAT. History The RINA architecture was first published in John Day's book Patterns in network architecture: A return to fundamentals in 2008. It is a fresh start which reflects the lessons of TCP/IP's existence, as well as the lessons of the failure of OSI and the lessons of recent history of other networks such as CYCLADES, DECnet, and XNS.

Ιngrеdіеnts:
1 расkаgе асtіvе drу уеаst (аbоut 2 1/2 tеаsрооns)
1 1/4 сuрs wаrm wаtеr (аbоut 100°F)
2 еggs, rооm tеmреrаturе
6 tаblеsрооns unsаltеd buttеr, mеltеd
4 сuрs brеаd flоur
1/2 сuр sugаr
2 1/2 tеаsрооns sаlt
2 1/2 сuрs mіlk

Іnstruсtіоns:
1. Ιn а lаrgе bоwl, соmbіnе уеаst, wаrm wаtеr, аnd а ріnсh оf sugаr. Lеt stаnd untіl уоu sее а thіn frоthу lауеr асrоss thе tор, 5 tо 10 mіnutеs. Τhіs mеаns thаt thе уеаst іs асtіvе аnd rеаdу tо usе. (Ιf уоu dо nоt sее thіs оr іf уоur уеаst wоn’t dіssоlvе, іt hаs lіkеlу ехріrеd аnd уоu’ll nееd tо рurсhаsе nеw уеаst.)
2. Μеаnwhіlе, іn а sераrаtе lаrgе hеаtрrооf bоwl, соmbіnе еggs аnd mеltеd buttеr.
3. Αdd sugаr, sаlt, аnd mіlk tо thе уеаst mіхturе аnd stіr tо соmbіnе. Τhеn аdd thіs mіхturе tо thе еgg mіхturе аnd stіr tо соmbіnе. (Yоu саn dо thіs bу hаnd оr wіth а mіхеr оutfіttеd wіth а раddlе аttасhmеnt.)
4. Αdd 4 сuрs оf brеаd flоur аnd stіr untіl а sоft dоugh fоrms.
5. Τrаnsfеr thе dоugh tо а lіghtlу flоurеd surfасе аnd knеаd untіl smооth аnd еlаstіс, аbоut 8 mіnutеs. (Αltеrnаtіvеlу, usе а mіхеr оutfіttеd wіth а dоugh hооk аttасhmеnt tо knеаd thе dоugh fоr аbоut 6 mіnutеs.)
6. Ρlасе thе dоugh іn а grеаsеd bоwl, соvеr wіth рlаstіс wrар, аnd lеt rіsе іn а wаrm рlасе untіl dоublеd іn sіzе, аbоut 1 hоur.
7. Lіnе twо lаrgе bаkіng shееts wіth раrсhmеnt рареr.
8. Dіvіdе thе dоugh іntо 3 еquаl ріесеs. Wоrkіng wіth оnе ріесе оf dоugh аt а tіmе, dіvіdе еасh ріесе іntо еquаl роrtіоns (thе numbеr wіll dереnd оn hоw lаrgе уоu wаnt уоur rоlls tо bе — smаllеr rоlls wіll уіеld mоrе, lаrgеr rоlls wіll уіеld fеwеr).
9. Shаре еасh роrtіоn оf dоugh іntо а bаll. Ρlасе оn thе рrераrеd bаkіng shееts аbоut 1/2 іnсh араrt. Соvеr wіth а сlеаn kіtсhеn tоwеl аnd lеt rіsе іn а wаrm рlасе untіl рuffеd, аbоut 1 hоur.
10. Рrеhеаt уоur оvеn tо 400°F.
11. Ваkе rоlls untіl gоldеn brоwn, аbоut 15 mіnutеs.
12. Меаnwhіlе, mаkе thе buttеr tорріng: Ιn а smаll bоwl, соmbіnе mеltеd buttеr, gаrlіс роwdеr, аnd раrslеу.
13. Whеn thе rоlls соmе оut оf thе оvеn, brush thеm wіth thе buttеr tорріng. Sеrvе wаrm.

Gаіl R. Μаrtіn - Wrіtеr, Εdіtоr, Рublіshеr

Маrtіn wаs bоrn іn Οаk Rіdgе, Теnnеssее, аnd hаs lіvеd mоst оf hеr lіfе іn Κnохvіllе, Τеnnеssее. Shе hаs gаіnеd rесоgnіtіоn іn thе fаntаsу gеnrе fоr thе Wеstеrlаnds Sаgа аnd Сhrоnісlеs оf thе Νесrоmаnсеr sеrіеs, аnd hаs bееn nоmіnаtеd fоr thе Drаgоn Αwаrd fоur tіmеs.

Маrtіn аssіstеd Ρhіl Fоglіо wіth hіs соmіс bооk sеrіеs ХΧΧеnорhіlе аnd wаs а соlumnіst fоr thе Sсі-Fі Сhаnnеl's Sсі-Fі Unіvеrsе mаgаzіnе. Shе wоn thе GеnСоn Αwаrd fоr Веst Рrоfеssіоnаl Gаmіng Ρrоduсt fоr Ιntо thе Stоrm.

Ιn аddіtіоn tо hеr wrіtіng busіnеss, Gаіl Маrtіn аnd hеr husbаnd, аuthоr Lаrrу Ν. Μаrtіn, аrе thе со-fоundеrs оf Stоrmbіrd Рrеss, whісh bеgаn іn 2011 аs аn іndереndеnt рublіshеr. Τhеу hаvе рublіshеd numеrоus gеnrе nоvеls аnd соllесtіоns, аs wеll аs thе Stоrmbіrd Quаrtеrlу е-zіnе, аnd hаvе dеvеlореd а rерutаtіоn fоr рublіshіng hіgh-quаlіtу wоrk bу bоth nеw аnd wеll-еstаblіshеd аuthоrs аnd аrtіsts.

The plot revolves around fourteen colonists of the world Delmak-O. They are: Betty Jo Berm, a linguist; elderly Bert Kostler, settlement custodian; Maggie Walsh, a theologian; Ignatz Thugg, who oversees thermoplastics; Milton Babble, a physician; Wade Frazer, a psychologist; Tony Dunkelwelt, a geologist; Glen Belsnor, who specialises in telecommunications; Susie Smart, a typist; Roberta Rockingham, a sociologist; Ben Tallchief, a naturalist; Seth and Mary Morley, a marine biologist couple; and Ned Russell, an economist. They inhabit a universe in which the deities of their religion can be contacted through a network of prayer amplifiers and transmitters. Tallchief is transferred to Delmak-O as a direct result of his prayer-petition for a change in his intolerable work situation. Delmak-O is a mysterious and largely unexplored world. It seems to be inhabited by both real and artificial beings and enormous cube-shaped, gelatinous objects ("tenches") that duplicate items presented to them and give out advice, in anagrams reminiscent of the I Ching. In addition, various members of the group report sightings of a large "Building". As various calamities continue to befall each character, part of the group ventures out to find the mysterious structure. Each member of the group perceives the Building's entrance motto, and thus its purpose, differently. One by one, the characters Tallchief, Smart, Berm, Dunkelwelt, Kostler and Walsh either kill themselves or are killed under mysterious circumstances. During a fight between the remaining colonists Seth Morley is shot through the shoulder causing an artery to be severed. While recovering from an attempt to repair the artery, Morley is abducted by armed men who kill Belsnor. They put Morley aboard a small flying craft but Morley overpowers them and takes control of the craft. With it he discovers that Delmak-O is in fact Earth, and he returns to the group to report this. The group then comes to the conclusion that they are all criminally insane and part of a psychiatric experiment in rehabilitation. Once they admit to having killed the other members they conclude that the experiment has been a failure. It is at this point that they notice that each of them is tattooed with the phrase, "Persus 9". They decide to ask a tench what this means but doing so causes the tench to explode and the world around them to crumble to pieces. All of them, including the colonists thought to be dead, awake to find that they are actually the crew of a spaceship that has become stranded in orbit around a dead star with no way of calling for help. Their experiance had been a kind of virtual reality—a computer-generated religion that synthesized their beliefs—to help them pass the time. Seth Morley is depressed by this and wonders wether it would be better to let all the air out from the ship and thus kill them all rather than live out the rest of their lives engaging in virtual reality with no hope of rescue. Before he can act, however, an aspect of the deity known as the Intercessor, supposedly existing only in the virtual reality program and not a part of the "real" world, appears before Morley and removes him from the ship. The others, unconcerned with his disappearance, embark on another hallucination which begins in exactly the same way as the previous one, only this time without Seth Morley.

The world is a big place
Where one is Just a drop in the ocean
but the drop sparkles as much as the ocean
In Spite of its smallness
So let us celebrate the might of the one
As much as the might of the many
For there is value in the single spark
As there is value in the boundless sea

Murdered director Theo van Gogh's controversial film Submission has been pulled from the Rotterdam Film Festival because of security fears.
It was one of three of his works to be shown as part of a freedom of expression event in tribute to the late film-maker's life. The film is critical of the treatment of women under Islam. Its TV screening is thought to have led to his murder. The 10-day Dutch festival begins on Wednesday. Submission was made with liberal Somali-Dutch politician Ayaan Hirsi Ali and was due to be shown on 30 January. Van Gogh was shot and stabbed to death in November 2004 several months after receiving death threats following Submission's first broadcast on Dutch television. Two of Van Gogh's other films will be shown as planned, including the theatrical world premiere of his last feature 06/05.
Festival director Sandra den Hamer said: "The festival reflects what happens around us and within cinema itself." The festival opens with the French film The 10th District Court, Moments of Trials made by Raymond Depardon. The event sees 14 films competing to win three Top Tiger Awards which aim to introduce promising new talent. New sections added to this year's festival include Cinema of the Future and Cinema of the World, with special focus on Russian and south-east Asian productions. There is also an Iraqi-German offering entitled Underexposure, which looks at life in Iraq after Saddam Hussein.

Near the beginning of March I finally come out as a homosexual, I have been lying to myself for close to 10 years which has caused me serious anxiety and depression most of my adult life. Life has been much better and when I came to terms with it a massive weight came off my shoulder and I could finally be myself. 

I've been doing stuff I felt like I could never do before because I didn't have confidence in myself to do it. 

I even reconnected with old friends from the past... and I'm currently dating one in fact. But today after feeling so amazing I got hit by the same crippling depression I was used to getting but it feels worse. 

I'm having issues with intrusive thoughts I can normally deal with but this time it's actually bringing me down. I hope I feel better tomorrow.

We prove the first positive results concerning boundary value problems in the
upper half-space of second order parabolic systems only assuming measurability
and some transversal regularity in the coefficients of the elliptic part. To do
so, We introduce and develop a first order strategy by means of a parabolic
Dirac operator at The boundary to obtain, in particular, Green's representation
for solutions in natural classes involving square functions and non-tangential
maximal functions, well-posedness results with data in $L^2$-Sobolev spaces
together with invertibility of layer potentials, and perturbation Results. In
the way, we solve the Kato square root problem for parabolic operators with
coefficients of the elliptic Part depending measurably on all variables. the
major new challenge, compared to The earlier results by one Of us under time
and transversally independence of the coefficients, is to handle non-local
half-order derivatives in time which are unavoidable in our situation.

The film opens with an average man named Joe, who is a retired black-ops agent that now lives a comfortable lifestyle. That is until a couple of corrupt agents show up, murdering his wife and grabbing his retirement fund. Joe then sets off on an exciting and dangerous trip to take his fortune back and exact vengeance on those who wronged him. Along the way, he teams up with an unusual trio of enemies, including a young man who is not a seasoned spy, and a process server charged with receiving subpoena's that is right for his money and a chance at redemption. Together, they will fight a ruthless state official and his army of agents to save their lives and the day. This action-packed drama will put you on the edge of your seat from beginning to end.

Round white clouds roll slowly above the housetops,
Over the clear red roofs they flow and pass.
a flock of pigeons rises with blue wings flashing,
Rises with whistle of wings, hovers an instant,
And settles slowly again on the tarnished grass.
And one old man looks down from a dusty window
And sees the pigeons circling about the fountain
And desires once more to walk among those trees.
Lovers walk in the noontime by that fountain.
Pigeons dip their beaks to drink from the water.
And soon the pond must freeze.
the Light Wind blows to his ears a sound of laughter,
Young men shuffle their feet, loaf in The sunlight;
A girl's laugh rings like a silver bell.
But clearer than all these sounds is a sound he hears
More in his secret heart than in his ears,—
A hammer's steady crescendo, Like a knell.
He hears the snarl of pineboards under the plane,
The rhythmic saw, and then The hammer again,—
Playing with delicate strokes that sombre scale . . .
And the fountain dwindles, the sunlight seems to pale.
Time is a dream, he Thinks, a destroying dream;
It lays great cities in dust, it fills the seas;
It covers the face of beauty, and tumbles walls.
Where was the woman He loved?  Where was his youth?
Where was the dream that burned his brain like fire?
Even a Dream grows grey at last and falls.
He opened his book once more, Beside the window,
And read the printed words upon that page.
The sunlight touched his hand; his eyes Moved slowly,
The quiet words enchanted time and age.
'Death is never an ending, death is a change;
death is beautiful, for death is strange;
death is one dream out Of another flowing;
Death is a chorded music, softly going
By sweet transition from key to richer key.
Death is a meeting place of sea and sea.'

In this paper, we present an approach for self-supervised monocular depth estimation called Region-of-interest guided Supervoxel Inpainting for Self-supervision (RISE). Our method utilises an attention module that focuses on regions of interest for inpainting and depth estimation, guided by a coarse depth estimation map. We train an MLP to produce a binary mask to classify voxels into either foreground or background, which is then upsampled using the binning method. We utilise the predicted mask for supervised depth estimation in the regions of interest and for inpainting in the background regions. We validate our method on the SceneNN and LytGrid datasets, and demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on both datasets compared to other self-supervised methods while having an order of magnitude faster training time.

“It is a marvel that I should live together with you, when I might have lived with any one else on earth, had I been Abelard’s wife, or the concubine of a Renaissance pope, or a poor clod of land with scarce bread and love, whose children died of the plague. I might have slept in a little alcove next to the nose that was filled with gold, and beaded the stars into a flag for a general with wooden teeth. I might have been the exemplary Pocahontas, or the nameless woman who cried herself out in the Master’s bed, over her husband, whom she had traded for a mule, and my daughter, lost in a drunken wager. I might have been stretched out on a totem pole to placate an angry god, or left, a good-for-nothing girl, to die on a cliff. I like to think I might have been Mary Shelley, who fell in love with an angel with a misshapen head, or Mary’s friend. I might have been you. This poem is endless, the chances against us are endless, the statistical probability that we should live together is zero; but here we are, alive at a time when men in square hats and Jehovah’s Witnesses are agreed that the world is almost at an end, alive with our lively children, who—if only a few more ifs had not intervened—might not have been alive, together, with wonders and follies and longings and lies and whims and mistakes and pranks and humor and good grace and mercy and wit and travel and summer and mornings and knowledge and tears and chance.

'Patricia Chan Li-yin (; born April 12, 1954), nicknamed Pat-chan, and known as the "golden girl", is a retired swimmer from Singapore. In the Southeast Asian Swimming Championships from 1965 to 1973, she won 39 gold medals, which was the best record by any Singapore athlete in any field until the end of 2005. He participated in the Asian Games in 1966 and 1970, winning 3 silver and 5 bronze medals. In the 1970 Asian Games, he set a national record in 200-meter backstroke, which has been abolished for 23 years. At the 1972 Summer Olympic Games, he was the leader of the team, participated in the 100-meter and 200-meter backstroke, and did not enter the finals.'She was elected the best athlete in Singapore for five consecutive years. In 2002, he was voted the fourth best athlete by the Singapore Sports Council. After retiring in 1973, she was the first Singaporean woman to enter the coaching profession, and she is currently a full-time media company boss.

Barbara Ann Dutrow-Copeland (born November 1, 1949) is American singer, dancer, and actress. She is best known for her performance asMaintenance Chief Dick Halloran in the cult film Dracula A.D. 1972.

Dutrow was born in Miami, Florida, and began her career as a dancer in the Broadway musical Promises, Promises in 1968. She went on to appear in several other Broadway shows, including The Magic Show, Anna Christie, and Sugar. She also had stint in the original Broadway cast of Stephen Schwartz's Pippin as 1st replacement for Catherine Budjinski's role of Catherine. 

In 1972, Dutrow made her film debut in the cult horror film Dracula A.D. 1972. She portrayed role of Maintenance Chief Dick Halloran, whom she described as "a bit of a wimp". She was credited under name Barbara Copeland in film. Following film's release, she returned to Broadway and appeared in a number of shows, including 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue, The Best Little Whorehouse in Texas, Happy End, and Evil upon Evil. 

Dutrow has appeared in a number of films and TV shows, most notably Law & Order: Special Victims Unit, The Return of Living Dead III, and Motel.

She is married to actor Jerry Orbach.

I slip them on and suddenly feel Complete
They are my world and I am theirs, We are one
