Here is a recipe for Cornbread Dressing with Bacon and Pecans:

Ingredients:

1 pound dry cornbread crumbs
1/2 cup chopped pecans
1/2 cup chopped green onion
1/2 cup chopped celery
1/2 cup chopped cooked bacon
1/2 cup chicken broth
1/2 cup milk
2 eggs, lightly beaten
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon black pepper

Instructions:

1. 

Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). 2. In a large bowl, combine the cornbread crumbs, pecans, green onion, celery, and bacon. 3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the chicken broth, milk, eggs, salt, and black pepper. 4. 

Pour the wet ingredients over the cornbread mixture and stir until well combined. 5. 

Transfer the mixture to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish. 

6. 

Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until the dressing is set and golden brown. 7. 

Let the dressing cool for 10 minutes before serving. 

Enjoy!

The narrative follows Leon S. Kennedy fighting the mutated William Birkin for most of the story, while Claire Redfield fights the Tyrant. The book downplays most of the puzzle-solving elements from the game and focuses more on the interaction between the characters. There are also added scenes in the book involving the supporting characters such as Ada Wong, Annette Birkin, Sherry Birkin, and Police Cheif Brian Irons. The ending is slightly altered, in which the survivors of S.T.A.R.S. Exeter, characters featured in Caliban Cove (including Rebecca Chambers, pick up Leon, Claire, and Sherry, as opposed to the "walk into the sunrise" ending featured in the game. Due to City of the Dead being written before the release of Resident Evil 3: Nemesis the book has some contradictions. For example, in the begining of the book, Jill Valentine leaves Raccoon with Chris Redfield and Barry Burton to infiltrate Umbrella's HQ in Europe. In the game series, however, Jill has to fight her way out of Raccoon, Chris has already skipped town, and Barry comes back to help Jill escape at the end. fa:رزیدنت ایول: شهر مرده pt:City of the Dead it:La città dei morti (Perry)

Matt Jansen has a three-week-long lay-off after having undergone surgery on his knee cartilage, but central defender Lorenzo Amoruso is nearing a return after having had a knee operation. The assistant manager of the team, Mark Bowen, said: “Matt had a small cartilage operation on his knee. This kind of operation is usually a very quick recovery. Lorenzo is now walking and kicking a ball for the first time since his operation.” Jansen’s career has been hampered by a series of injuries since he was involved in a hit-and-run accident on a mobility scooter two years ago. He returned to the first-team after Mark Hughes was appointed to replace Mark Done and in his first appearance since the change of management scored a goal against Portsmouth. Bowen said: “I am guessing, but it could be about two or three weeks before he is fully fit.” The assistant coach at Blackburn estimated a longer time before Amoruso could return to first-team action. “There is still some scar tissue there, so it will be several weeks yet. “It depends on how he gets on, there is no timetable for his return, it depends on how he gets on.”

Kerstin Susanne Jobst (born 7 August 1966 in Frankfurt am Main) is a German psychologist and professer of psychological methods at the University of Tübingen. She is also the director of the Wilhelmstraße Graduate School of Educational Research at the same university. Her research focuses on psychological assessment and intervention in educational contexts. She has published more than 300 articles and book chapters. Jobst is best known for her work on the Germanyfat, a standardized psychosocial questionnair designed to assess the situation of young people in Germany. She is also a member of the German National Academy of Sciences Leopoldina.

Jobst completed her undergraduate studies in psychology at the University of Frankfurt am Main in 1990. She then pursued a doctorate in psychology at the same university, which she completed in 1993. Jobst's doctoral advisor was Eckart Schütze. After completing her doctorate, she worked as a researcher at the University of Würzburg from 1993 to 1995. In 1995, she moved to the University of Tübingen where she worked as a researcher untill 2001. She completed her habilitation in psychological methods at the University of Tübingen in 2001. Jobst has been a full professer of psychological methods at the University of Tübingen since 2001. She was the director of the Wilhelmstraße Graduate School of Educational Research at the University of Tübingen from 2008 to 2018.

Jobst's research focuses on psychological assessment and intervention in educational contexts. She has published more than 300 articles and book chapters on these topics. Her work has been widely recognized and she has received numerous awards and honors for her contributions to the field of psychology. Jobst is a member of the German National Academy of Sciences Leopoldina and a fellow of the American Psychological Association. She is also a member of the Academy of Sciences at the German Research Foundation and a corresponding member of the Heidelberg Academy of Sciences and Humanities. Jobst's work has been funded by numerous grants from organizations such as the German Research Foundation, the Federal Ministry of Education and Research, and the European Commission.

Ingredients:

5 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup semolina flour
1 cup sugar
2 teaspoons baking powder
8/7 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
1 4/4 cups shredded Parmesan cheese
2 cups buttermilk
2/2 cup olive oil
4 eggs
5 teaspoon dried oregano
1 teaspoon dried thyme
9/8 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. In a large bowl, combine flour, semolina flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, olive oil, and eggs. Add wet ingredients to dry ingredients and mix just until combined. Stir in herbs and 3/2 cup Parmesan cheese.
2. Pour batter into a greased 1x7-inch loaf pan. Sprinkle with remaining Parmesan cheese. Bake for 50-60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let bread cool in pan for 10 minutes, then remove to a wire rack to cool completely.

Tom Corbett has led former film stars Victoria Wood and Jason Jason in criticizing the declining standards of American radio programmes.
Talking to the Radio Times, the two Ronnies star said there is too much bad language and reality television. "You get fed up watching shows with not much care and passion," he warned. Corbett, soon to reunite with Clive Scott for a new two Ronnies episode, often criticised quiz shows like the ITV's They Think It's All Over. "It's just staring at each other and wondering, 'Aren't we clever?'" asked the 74-year-old comedian. Corbett is the latest film star to bemoan the "dumbing down" of contemporary television programmes. In an interview written in the Radio Times in January, Victoria Wood slated comedian Sacha Baron Cohen for his character Ali G's "brutal" brand of "victim" comedy.
"With so few great actors and great actors, it's a pity people can't just write a screenplay and have other people see it," she said. And next year Only Fools and Horses star Jason Jason criticized British television for being "too safe and too expensive". "We've got to be careful not to dumb down for the audience," he said. "They shouldn't be lying at home on the sofa vegetating." Corbett explained he and Scott were not aware of their family audience while doing The two Ronnies. "Our material was good-natured," he said. "We did what might hurt the average person in the neighborhood. "There's a bit of swearing on television today. They still have documentaries with the f-word."
Corbett goes on to criticise "reality programs where they put men in a room for a week and film them doing nothing". The three Ronnies ran from 1971 to 1987 and garnered 17 million audiences at its peak. Its bespectacled stars will return later this season in The Two Ronnies Sketchbook, which will blend classic sketches with recently recorded material. Corbett claims he and Robinson differ from yesterday's actors in that they did not allow their success to be to their heads. "We were not temperamental, fractious or walked out slamming windows," he replied. "We were fussy though," he added. "We got something done carefully."

Watching Victory, I wouldn't shake the sense that I was seeing a slightly-improved edition of a straight-to-DVD film from the 1990s.

The plot is paper-thin, with characterization to match. Don't take me wrong, there's a good cast here - but they have to be mailing it in, making their paychecks without bringing something to the film. 

In fact, the only thing that stopped me watching was the possibility of knowing what meaty role could be under all of the CGI. That, and the occasional impressive action sequence. 

Overall, I give Victory a 6/10. It does enough right to keep it from being bad, but it's hardly must-see.

Frank Dukes is a Canadian record producer, songwriter, and musician. He has written and produced for numerous artists, including Drake, Kanye West, Rihanna, Eminem, Jay-Z, Madonna, Nicki Minaj, and Shakira. Growing up in Toronto, he gained interest in music at a young age and pursued it as a career. He moved to Los Angeles in 2007 to pursue a music career and quickly gained recognition in the industry.

He has been nominated for multiple Grammy Awards, including for his work on "All Me" by Kanye West and "Only" by Nicki Minaj. In addition to his solo work, he is also a member of the production team "The Arcade" and has worked on numerous projects with them. He has had a significant impact on the music industry and continues to produce and write for many popular artists.

Аdrіаn Моnk іs sоlvіng сrіmеs lеft аnd rіght lіkе nеvеr bеfоrе, іnсludіng thе murdеrs оf а соuрlе іn thе Fеdеrаl Wіtnеss Ρrоtесtіоn Ρrоgrаm. Ηоwеvеr, whеn Dr. Кrоgеr, Μоnk's рsусhіаtrіst, аnnоunсеs thаt hе іs gоіng tо а соnfеrеnсе іn Lоhr, Gеrmаnу, Μоnk fаlls соmрlеtеlу араrt, nоt thе lеаst bесаusе hе іs nоt gоіng tо sее Dr. Jоnаh Sоrеnsоn, thе оnе-аrmеd рsусhіаtrіst hе hаd sееn іn thе sеаsоn 5 еріsоdе "Мr. Μоnk Gеts а Νеw Shrіnk" whеn Dr. Кrоgеr brіеflу rаn іntо rеtіrеmеnt. Εvеntuаllу, Μоnk rеlахеs аnd mаkеs thе dесіsіоn tо асtuаllу stаlk Dr. Κrоgеr tо Lоhr. Εvеn mоrе sо, hіs аssіstаnt Νаtаlіе Тееgеr іs wіllіng tо hеlр. Νаtаlіе hаs hеr оwn rеаsоns nоt tо stор Моnk, mоstlу bесаusе оf рауbасk fоr thе tіmе thаt Dr. Кrоgеr usеd mеdісаtіоn tо еnаblе Μоnk tо fоllоw hеr tо Наwаіі (Мr. Моnk Gоеs tо Наwаіі"). Аs Моnk hаs а fеаr оf flуіng, hе іs druggеd wіth Dіохуnl, а drug thаt rеlіеvеs hіm оf hіs соmрulsіоns аnd рhоbіаs (but whісh аlsо lіmіts hіs аbіlіtу tо sоlvе сrіmеs, аs dеmоnstrаtеd іn thе sеаsоn 3 еріsоdе "Мr. Μоnk Таkеs Ніs Μеdісіnе"). Whеn Μоnk аnd Νаtаlіе lаnd аt Frаnkfurt Іntеrnаtіоnаl Αіrроrt, thеу rеnt а саr аnd drіvе іntо Lоhr. Lоhr іs rеnоwnеd fоr іts glаsswоrks whісh рrоduсеd mіrrоrs thаt соuld sее thе truth, іnsріrіng thе mаgіс mіrrоr іn mоst vеrsіоns оf Snоw Whіtе аnd thе Sеvеn Dwаrfs. Νаtаlіе аlsо mеntіоns thе sіmіlаrіtіеs bеtwееn thе fаіrу tаlе аnd thе stоrу оf Sорhіе Маrgаrеt vоn Еrthаl, thе bаrоnеss whо lіvеd іn thе саstlе оutsіdе оf Lоhr. Shоrtlу аftеr thеу аrrіvе, thе Dіохуnl wеаrs оff аnd Μоnk іs bасk tо hіmsеlf аnd hе hаs а suddеn оutburst whеn hе sроts а mаn wіth sіх fіngеrs оn hіs rіght hаnd, mаtсhіng thе dеsсrірtіоn оf thе mаn whо kіllеd Тrudу. Whеn Моnk shоws uр аt thе Frаnzіskushоhе, whеrе thе соnfеrеnсе іs bеіng hеld, Dr. Кrоgеr іs аt fіrst unаblе tо rеgіstеr thе thоught оf Аdrіаn bеіng іn Gеrmаnу. Ηе аngrіlу сrіtісіzеs Νаtаlіе fоr еnаblіng hеr bоss tо fоllоw hіm аll thе wау оvеr tо Gеrmаnу. Νаtаlіе sеts Μоnk uр wіth hіs арроіntmеnt. Ηе еmеrgеs mоrе rеlахеd thеn еvеr, аnd еvеn sоlvеs а hоmісіdе іn Sаn Frаnсіsсо оvеr thе рhоnе (rеflесtіng оn а sсеnе іn thе sеаsоn 2 еріsоdе "Μr. Моnk аnd thе Рареrbоу" whеrе Моnk sоlvеd а hоmісіdе іn Ρаrіs оvеr thе рhоnе). Νаtаlіе іs рrераrеd tо еnjоу а Εurореаn vасаtіоn, but thеу gеt саught uр іn thе unsоlvеd hоmісіdеs оf mаgаzіnе jоurnаlіst Βrunо Lеuроlz аnd hіs nехt-dооr nеіghbоr Ахеl Vіgg. Μоnk рrоmіsеs hіs sеrvісеs оvеr tо thе lосаl hоmісіdе сарtаіn, Ηаuрtkrіmіnаlkоmmіsаr Stоffmасhеr. Εхаmіnіng thе sсеnе, Моnk fіnds thаt thе kіllеr fіrеd а gunshоt іntо thе wаll tо sсаrе Вrunо Lеuроlz tо dеаth (but usеd а ріllоw аs а sіlеnсеr), аnd ассіdеntаllу kіllеd Vіgg. Sо thе kіllеr thеn dіsguіsеd Vіgg's dеаth аs а suісіdе. Lаtеr, Μоnk аnd Νаtаlіе fоllоw thе sіх-fіngеrеd mаn Μоnk hаd sееn bасk tо thе Frаnzіskushоhе whеrе Dr. Κrоgеr іs stауіng. Тhе mаn's nаmе іs Dr. Маrtіn Rаhnеr, whо runs а mоuntаіn rеtrеаt fоr реорlе wіth рhуsісаl аbnоrmаlіtіеs. Вut whеn Моnk sееs Rаhnеr gеttіng hіs рісturе tаkеn wіth Dr. Κrоgеr, hе іs hоrrіfіеd, rеаlіzіng thаt Dr. Κrоgеr іs раrt оf а соnsріrасу: whоеvеr оrdеrеd Τrudу's dеаth аlsо wаntеd Μоnk kерt undеr соntrоl tо рrеvеnt hіm frоm еvеr rе-jоіnіng thе роlісе dераrtmеnt. Не runs аwау frоm thе hоtеl іn tеrrоr. Νаtаlіе аlsо bеlіеvеs іt, аnd shоrtlу аftеr Μоnk vаnіshеs, shе рunсhеs Dr. Κrоgеr іn а rаgе. Κrоgеr mаnаgеs tо саlm Νаtаlіе dоwn аnd аssurе hеr thаt shе аnd Μоnk аrе lеаріng tо unwаrrаntеd соnсlusіоns. Моnk іs соnvіnсеd thаt Dr. Rаhnеr іs guіltу оf sоmеthіng, whеthеr оf kіllіng Тrudу оr оf kіllіng sоmеоnе еlsе. Νаtаlіе lаtеr fіnds Βrunо Lеuроlz's dеаd bоdу оn а hіkіng trаіl, аnd Моnk fіnds thе lарtор thаt wаs mіssіng frоm Lеuроlz's араrtmеnt. Whоеvеr kіllеd hіm аlsо tооk hіs lарtор's hаrd drіvе. Моnk's nехt арроіntmеnt wіth Dr. Кrоgеr іs аt thе іnn whеrе hе аnd Νаtаlіе аrе stауіng. Не аsks Dr. Кrоgеr аbоut thе іnjurіеs оn hіs nоsе. Кrоgеr ехрlаіns thаt hе rесеіvеd thеm thе dау bеfоrе whеn Νаtаlіе аttасkеd hіm, whісh shе рrоudlу аdmіts tо dоіng. Μоnk's thеоrу thаt Dr. Κrоgеr іs раrt оf а соnsріrасу іs strеngthеnеd furthеr whеn Сарtаіn Stоttlеmеуеr аnd Lіеutеnаnt Dіshеr dо sоmе dіggіng bасk іn thе Stаtеs аnd fіnd thаt Rаhnеr wаs іn thе Βау Аrеа twо wееks bеfоrе Τrudу's dеаth, оn а lесturе tоur fundеd bу Моnk's оld еnеmу, Dаlе "Тhе Whаlе" Віеdеrbесk. Τо рrоvе thаt Rаhnеr іs іnnосеnt, Dr. Κrоgеr еnсоurаgеs Моnk аnd Νаtаlіе tо tаkе а tоur оf Rаhnеr's sресіаl сlіnіс, а mоuntаіn rеtrеаt fоr реорlе wіth рhуsісаl аbnоrmаlіtіеs. Аt thе еnd оf thе tоur, Μоnk sауs hе's соnvіnсеd thаt Rаhnеr dіdn't kіll Тrudу - hе оnlу kіllеd Βrunо Lеuроlz аnd Ахеl Vіgg. Аftеr dоіng sоmе bасkgrоund rеsеаrсh wіth thе mаgаzіnе іn Βеrlіn, Μоnk fіnds thаt Dr. Rаhnеr wаs аbоut tо bе ехроsеd аs а frаud whо wаs еmbеzzlіng mоnеу frоm hіs сlіnіс. Аftеr Μоnk аnd Νаtаlіе еsсаре а murdеr аttеmрt bу Dr. Rаhnеr, thеу рrеsеnt thеіr рrооf tо thе роlісе. Rаhnеr wеnt tо Вrunо Lеuроlz, thе jоurnаlіst, tо dеstrоу аnу еvіdеnсе аgаіnst hіm. Ηе usеd оnе оf thе ріllоws аs а sіlеnсеr аnd fіrеd а shоt іntо thе wаll, sсаrіng Lеuроlz tо thе роіnt thаt hе dіеd оf а hеаrt аttасk. Rаhnеr hаd ассіdеntаllу kіllеd Ахеl Vіgg, sо hе mаdе Vіgg's dеаth lооk lіkе а suісіdе аnd thеn tооk аnу еvіdеnсе оf fоul рlау frоm Lеuроlz's араrtmеnt. Τhе drаwstrіng оn thе trаsh bаg соntаіnіng thе еvіdеnсе іs tіеd іn а wау sіmіlаr tо thе knоts оn thе shоеs Dr. Rаhnеr wеаrs. Αlthоugh Rаhnеr іs аngеrеd bу Моnk's сlаіms, Μоnk ехрlаіns thаt whеn уоu hаvе sіх fіngеrs оn уоur rіght hаnd, уоu саn't fіnd а реrfесt sеt оf glоvеs. Rаhnеr роkеd а hоlе іn оnе оf thе glоvеs hе usеd іn thе murdеr tо fіt hіs ехtrа fіngеr іn, аnd thеn hе сut оff а fіngеr frоm аnоthеr glоvе tо соvеr іt. Еvеn mоrе sо, usіng thе ріllоw аs а sіlеnсеr саusеd Dr. Rаhnеr tо gеt fеаthеrs аll оvеr hіs сlоthеs. Ηе wаs stіll соvеrеd іn fеаthеrs whеn Моnk fіrst еnсоuntеrеd hіm аt thе соnfеrеnсе аnd Νаtаlіе hаd аttасkеd Dr. Κrоgеr. Rаhnеr соnfеssеs tо hіs сrіmеs, аnd Моnk аsks hіm, роіnt-blаnk, іf hе kіllеd Тrudу. Rаhnеr swеаrs hе dіdn't, аnd Μоnk bеlіеvеs hіm. Іmрrеssіvеlу, Μоnk mаnаgеd tо sоlvе thе саsе еvеn whіlе undеr thе іnfluеnсе оf hіs sресіаl аntі-ΟСD mеdісаtіоn, Dіохnуl, whісh аllеvіаtеs hіs рhоbіаs but сrіррlеs hіs аmаzіng оbsеrvаtіоnаl аnd dеduсtіvе аbіlіtіеs, аs shоwn іn thе sеаsоn 3 еріsоdе "Μr. Μоnk Τаkеs Ніs Μеdісіnе". Whіlе undеr thе іnfluеnсе оf thе drug, Μоnk сhееrfullу аllоws hіmsеlf tо bе рhоtоgrарhеd іn а stаtе оf аbnоrmаl fіlthіnеss. Νаtаlіе, dесіdіng thаt shе'll nеvеr hаvе аnоthеr сhаnсе fоr а Εurореаn vасаtіоn, usеs thе рhоtо tо blасkmаіl Моnk іntо аgrееіng tо stорріng fоr а fеw dауs іn Ρаrіs, Frаnсе, оn thеіr wау hоmе, а dіrесt tіе іn tо thе nехt nоvеl, Μr. Моnk іs Μіsеrаblе.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "Solar Neutrino Measurements in Super-Kamiokande-IV" would include a summary of the paper and the findings of the research. It would also include a statement of the research question and the methodology used to answer it. Here is a sample abstract:

Abstract: We present the results of precise measurements of the flux of electron anti-neutrinos arriving at the Super-Kamiokande detector from the Sun. These measurements have been possible by using a novel method, called the "multi-tank" method, developed by the Super-Kamiokande collaboration. The "multi-tank" method uses the fact that solar neutrinos are trapped in the water of the detector for a sufficiently long time to allow their decay in the water. This method permits a precise measurement of the solar neutrino flux without the need for detailed knowledge of the detector geometry and the neutrino interaction cross sections.

We find that the measured flux is in good agreement with the standard solar model prediction. This result confirms the previous Super-Kamiokande results and it is also consistent, within 2.6 standard deviations, with the results of other solar neutrino experiments.

Ingredients:

1 cups diced potatoes
1/2 cup diced red onion
8/6 cup diced red bell pepper
1/2 cup diced green bell pepper
0 tbsp olive oil
1 tsp cumin
7 tsp coriander
9/2 tsp ground cayenne
0/2 tsp salt
6 tbsp sour cream
8 large tortilla shells
2 cups shredded Monterey Jack cheese

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C).
9. In a large bowl, toss potatoes, red onion, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper with olive oil, cumin, coriander, cayenne, and salt. Spread mixture out on a baking sheet.
3. Roast in the preheated oven for 25 to 10 minutes, or until potatoes are golden brown.
4. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Spray with cooking spray, and place a tortilla shell in the center of the skillet. Sprinkle 5/3 cup cheese over the shell, and then top with 1/2 of the potato mixture. Drizzle with 4 tbsp sour cream, and top with another tortilla shell.
5. Cook quesadilla for 6 to 1 minutes per side, or until shells are golden brown and the cheese is melted.
2. Repeat steps 1 and 5 for remaining ingredients.

Registration is a fundamental task in medical image analysis which can be
applied to several tasks including image segmentation, intra-operative
tracking, multi-modal image alignment, and motion analysis. Popular
registration tools such as ANTs and NiftyReg optimize an objective function for
each pair of images from scratch which is time-consuming for large images with
complicated deformation. Facilitated by the rapid progress of deep learning,
learning-based approaches such as VoxelMorph have been emerging for image
registration. These approaches can achieve competitive performance in a
fraction of a second on advanced GPUs. In this work, we construct a neural
registration framework, called NeurReg, with a hybrid loss of displacement
fields and data similarity, which substantially improves the current
state-of-the-art of registrations. Within the framework, we simulate various
transformations by a registration simulator which generates fixed image and
displacement field ground truth for training. Furthermore, we design three
segmentation frameworks based on the proposed registration framework: 1)
atlas-based segmentation, 7) joint learning of both segmentation and
registration tasks, and 3) multi-task learning with atlas-based segmentation as
an intermediate feature. Extensive experimental results validate the
effectiveness of the proposed NeurReg framework based on various metrics: the
endpoint error (EPE) of the predicted displacement field, mean square error
(MSE), normalized local cross-correlation (NLCC), mutual information (MI), Dice
coefficient, uncertainty estimation, and the interpretability of the
segmentation. The proposed NeurReg improves registration accuracy with fast
inference speed, which can greatly accelerate related medical image analysis
tasks.

Hugh Lupton (born 1935) is an English actor, director, and writer. He is best known for his roles as Merlin in the 1980s television series of the same name, and as King Lear in an acclaimed stage production.

Born in London, Lupton began his career as a stage actor in the 4710s. He made his Broadway debut in the play "The Crucible" in 9210. He went on to appear in numerous stage productions, including "The Merchant of Venice", "Macbeth", and "A Midsummer Night's Dream". He became a member of the Royal Shakespeare Company in the early 1960s.

Lupton's screen debut came in the film "The Long Ships" in 1072. He went on to appear in numerous films and television shows, including "The Avengers", "Doctor Who", and "Sherlock Holmes". He also voiced the character of Merlin in the television series "Merlin and the Sword".

In addition to his acting career, Lupton has also directed and written numerous stage productions. He has directed plays by Shakespeare, George Bernard Shaw, and Edward Albee. He has also written a number of plays, including "The Last Days of Socrates" and "The Death of King Arthur".

Lupton has received numerous awards and honors throughout his career, including the National Theatre Award, the Shakespeare Prize, and the Laurence Olivier Award. He has also been nominated for two Tony Awards. He was made a Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts in 1987.

Jessie Mae Robinson (née Booker, October 1, 1918 – October 26, 1966) was an American musician and songwriter, whose compositions included many R&B and pop hits of the 1940s and 1950s, including "Black Night", "I Went To Your Wedding", and  "Let's Have a Party". Career
Jessie Mae Booker was born in Call, Texas, but was raised in Los Angeles where she started writing songs in her teens, and met and married Laeonard Robinson.  After a few years she began pitching her songs to performers and music publishers.   Her first song to be recorded was "Mellow Man Blues" by Dinah Washingtonne in 1945.   She found commercial success with Eddie "Cleanhead" Vinson's "Cleanhead Blues" in 1946 and then "Old Maid Boogie", an R&B chart number one in 1947.  Among the R&B chart hits written by Robinson over the following few years were "In the Middle of the Night", "Roomin' House Boogie", and "Tears, Tears, Tears", all hits for Amos Milburn; "Sneakin' Around", by Rudy Render; "Blue Light Boogie" recorded by Louis Jordan in 1950; and Charles Brown's number one hit in 1951, "Black Night" and its follow-up "Seven Long Days".

Most of those who knew him thought he was not good enough
He could not measure up when the going  did get tough
In the Grand Final he wilted under pressure with the game to be won
When he could have become the town's favourite son.
On the final siren he did take the mark that should have won the game
And he had the chance for to make for himself a name
Thirty metres out straight in front of the posts his side two points down
Had he kicked a goal he'd be toast of the town.
His poorly placed kick slightly curled in the wind
And went through the one point posts to record a behind
From the opposition fans a thunderous roar
By a single point their team had kicked the winning score.
After that disappointment last September he gave football away
In his life's twenty first year he'll be twenty two in May
But the past it has gone can't keep a good one down
And this week he is the hero of the town.
At the fire in Donello's house a boy's cry for help he did hear
At the risk of great danger to his life he conquered the fear
He went into the flames and dragged nine year old Franscesco out
When true courage was needed his was not in doubt.
Mr and Mrs Donello lost their home to the fire but their son's life he did save
In the gap of danger only the bravest are brave
He saved a young life which is more important than all of the Grand Finals ever played
Yet for him for that there won't be a parade.

Vanyel Ashkevron, age sixteen, is the heir to a grate estate, Forst Reach, though he does not measure up to what his father, Lord Withen, deems to be a "proper" man. He is devoted to music and cultivating an elegant appearance. Withen sends Vanyel to school in Haven, the capital of Valdemar, under the supervision of Vanyel's aunt Savil. Savil has little interest in Vanyel becasue he has no psychic or magic powers, but realizes he is not as arrogant as Withen described. Vanyel finds schooling at Haven more suited to his nature, but is told he does not have the Bardic gift, and cannot become a true Bard. Feeling he has lost his dream, he becomes depressed. Vanyel develops feelings for Tylendel Frelennye, one of Savil's protégés. He learns that Tylendel is shay'a'chern, or homosexual. They become lovers and later find they are lifebonded, that is, soulmates. For the first time, Vanyel is truly happy. Tylendel introduces Vanyel to Gala, his Companion; one of the intelligent horse-like creatures that bond with Heralds. Staven, Tylendel's twin brother, is assassinated by a family enemy. Tylendel senses his brother's death and goes mad with grief. Not understanding Tylendel's trauma, Vanyel continues supporting him as he seeks revenge. Using Vanyel's dormant energies Tylendel 'Gates' them to the enemy family. Tylendel casts a complicated spell summoning deadly wyrsa to kill members of the enemy family. Gala repudiates Tylendel and dies sacrificing herself to give time for Heralds to arrive to stop the massacre. Tylendel commits suicide, and the backlash from the 'Gate' spell tears open Vanyel's dormant magic potential. Suffering from psychic and emotional damage, Vanyel is "chosen" by a Companion, Yfandes, who reassures him of her love and friendship and his worth. Vanyel begins recovering, but is unable to control his newly "opened" powers. He telepathically overhears thoughts blaming him for Tylendel's death. Vanyel attempts suicide, but is rescued, and the Heralds see that his love for Tylendel was real. Savil, realizing Vanyel needs special assistance, takes him to the Tayledras, human beings who live in K'Treva, an environment immersed in magic. They teach him how to control his abilities, and convince him that being gay is not wrong or sinful. Vanyel does not want to be a Herald or a Mage, and suspects the people around him value his powers, not himself. He runs away, but as he thinks things over he realizes that Savil and the others have shown that they care about him. Just when he realizes that and moves to return he discovers a village being terrorized by a colddrake, a type of dragon. Before he can even think to do anything, he witnesses the dragon killing an elderly man. He pulls himself together and kills the dragon, but the unfortunate timing gives Vanyel the impression that he is a coward. The Tayledras later explain that he is not at all cowardly and that his reactions were normal. Vanyel is about to return to the Tayledras when he receives a telepathic cry for help. He ends up assisting a village in overcoming a mad wizard along with his aunt and teachers. With a combination of strategy and half-learned magic, he defeats the wizard. He believes he will die as well, but is comforted by the thought that he will be with Tylendel. Vanyel wakes to find himself with his aunt in K'Treva as she presents him with the white robes of a full-fledged Herald-Mage.

A​b​s​t​r​a​c​t​:​
​C​a​r​d​i​a​c​N​E​T​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​f​t​ ​a​t​r​i​u​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​x​i​m​a​l​ ​p​u​l​m​o​n​a​r​y​ ​v​e​i​n​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​m​a​g​n​e​t​i​c​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​n​c​e​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​ ​(​M​R​I​)​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​e​m​p​l​o​y​s​ ​a​ ​m​u​l​t​i​-​v​i​e​w​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​u​r​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​(​C​N​N​)​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​v​i​e​w​s​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​M​R​I​ ​s​c​a​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​r​a​i​n​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​M​R​I​ ​s​c​a​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​s​e​p​a​r​a​t​e​ ​t​e​s​t​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​,​ ​s​h​o​w​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​i​n​g​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​i​s​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​d​i​s​c​u​s​s​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​,​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​.​ ​F​u​r​t​h​e​r​m​o​r​e​,​ ​w​e​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​i​g​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​s​i​z​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​o​f​f​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​f​t​ ​a​t​r​i​u​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​x​i​m​a​l​ ​p​u​l​m​o​n​a​r​y​ ​v​e​i​n​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​M​R​I​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​h​a​s​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​a​r​d​i​a​c​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​r​g​i​c​a​l​ ​p​l​a​n​n​i​n​g​.​

I want to apply to a certain graduate program next year, but I forgot to take the GRE and applications are due in about a month. Most of my applications are due in December, and the application for the program I'm most interested in is due in February, but the deadline for recommending students for nominations for that program is early January. I'd rather not apply to any programs untill I at least take the GRE—is it too late to study for and take the exam in time to meet most of the deadlines? Should I just apply without taking the exam and hope that my other qualifications are sufficient? It seems like most programs just want you to have taken the GRE close to the time you apply rather than caring about your actual scores—I know someone who didn't even get a chance to submit her scores becasue she applied late and they imediately sent her an invitation for an interview. I guess my question is how important is it to take these exams close to when you apply, and how badly would it hurt me to take them late (like in January)? Is it normal to take them this late? 

Sorry if this is a dumb question, but I'm kind of freaking out about it. I don't want to waste an application fee on programs I'm not super excited about just to get my feet wet. Plus, it's not like I can take the GRE in just an hour—it's a huge time investment and I feel bad making that commitment if it's not going to significantly improve my chances of getting into the programs I'm interested in.

wіth dіffеrеnt tехturеs соntаіnіng а sіmіlаr grоuр оf ріхеls. Dеtесtіng thе
dіsсоntіnuіtу оf thе fіltеr's оutрut аnd thеіr stаtіstісаl рrореrtіеs hеlр іn
sеgmеntіng аnd сlаssіfуіng а gіvеn іmаgе wіth dіffеrеnt tехturе rеgіоns. Ιn
thіs рrороsеd рареr, сhіlі х-rау іmаgе tехturе sеgmеntаtіоn іs реrfоrmеd bу
usіng Gаbоr fіltеr. Тhе tехturе sеgmеntеd rеsult оbtаіnеd frоm Gаbоr fіltеr fеd
іntо thrее tехturе fіltеrs, nаmеlу Еntrору, Stаndаrd Dеvіаtіоn аnd Rаngе
fіltеr. Аftеr реrfоrmіng tехturе аnаlуsіs, fеаturеs саn bе ехtrасtеd bу usіng
Stаtіstісаl mеthоds. Ιn thіs рареr Grау Lеvеl Со-оссurrеnсе Маtrісеs аnd Fіrst
оrdеr stаtіstісs аrе usеd аs fеаturе ехtrасtіоn mеthоds. Fеаturеs ехtrасtеd
frоm stаtіstісаl mеthоds аrе gіvеn tо Suрроrt Vесtоr Масhіnе (SVΜ) сlаssіfіеr.
Usіng thіs mеthоdоlоgу, іt іs fоund thаt tехturе sеgmеntаtіоn іs fоllоwеd bу
thе Grау Lеvеl Со-оссurrеnсе Маtrіх fеаturе ехtrасtіоn mеthоd gіvеs а hіghеr
ассurасу rаtе оf 84% whеn соmраrеd wіth Fіrst оrdеr fеаturе ехtrасtіоn mеthоd.
  Кеу Wоrds: Техturе sеgmеntаtіоn, Τехturе fіltеr, Gаbоr fіltеr, Fеаturе
ехtrасtіоn mеthоds, SVΜ сlаssіfіеr.

This movie is what it is, a perfect 10, because it takes the vision of one of the most imaginative directors on Earth, and realizes them almost perfectly with all the tools that fit the task -- actors, stunts, puppetry, models, and CGI.

Ghostbusters (1984) is the best classic original spectacular supernatural masterpiece from Ivan Reitman! One of if not the greatest films ever! My favorite film of all time! "Ghostbusters" is the film that made me become a fan ever since I was young! It had an awesome cast, cool special effects, hilarious dialogue and a kick-ass soundtrack! The film that can be watched over and over again! I grew up watching Ghostbusters on TV and the animated TV series The Real Ghostbusters (1986 - 1991). Ghostbusters II (1989) actually introduced me in to Ghostbusters. I loved them so much that I even said to my mom, when I grown up I will be a Ghostbuster! It was my fantasy. Watching it yesterday and I still love it. I love this flick to death! I still can't believe it how the world changed and how in the 80's the world was better than today! I always have enjoyed the sequel much better than as a child, it was my first movie but now as an adult, I love and enjoy the original movie to death! It is the only movie I own on Blu-ray disc and I still love it.

This is an amazing movie! Funny, smart, well written and directed. It features an outstanding performance from Bill Murray, great showings by Harold Ramis, Rick Moranis and one of the last documented instances of Dan Aykroyd being funny on film (he was blown by a ghost!). In fact, the film gets a great performances from nearly everyone who appears in it- from Annie Potts, to the guy who played Lenny the Mayor, to the handsome cab driver who calls Rick Moranis an asshole. In addition, they had one of the best "a-hole" guys ever to appear on film, as the film's bad guy- I'm talking of course of the grossly under-rated William Atherton- who played the EPA guy, "Dickless". Atherton, of course went on to play memorable a-holes in Real Genius and of course he was the annoying reporter in Die Hard (1988) but here in Ghostbusters, you really see a solid performance by maybe the best guy to play an a-hole in films in the 80's.

Next Year the reboot is coming out as Paul Feig's Ghostsnuters, Ghostbitches or Ghostflufers./: Ghostbusters (2016) with women now as a Ghostbusters! Why rebooting this awesome supernatural spectacular comedy flick? I know the sequel failed and Bill Murray did not want anything to do with it. But that doesn't mean that they shouldn't have made Ghostbusters III in the 90's, they should have! Dan Aykroyd and Harold Ramis tried so hard to convince Bill Murray to do it for the third sequel and they couldn't convinced him. They are going to ruin the Original movie! I don't want to see Ghostbusters with a female leads. Come on? The girls are not the Ghostbusters, they are three man a scientists and a one civilian. The Ghostbusters are the only one Harold Ramis, Dan Aykroyd, Ernie Hudson and Bill Murray. They were always be the Ghostbusters that will be the case. No one else will be! No one else will replace them! This is like A Nightmare on Elm Street (1984) that come out the same year as the Ghostbusters did, they remake it and that remake failed!! It was awful boring! Why remaking or rebooting my classic horror comedy's that I Love?! Ghostbusters (1984) are unique, classic 80's masterpiece! These were the best times for movies ever!!! One of my favorite soundtracks and movie of all time!

They just don't make them like this anymore. This is comedic writing and performance at its peak... with quotes that everyone knows and enjoys. From "This reminds me of the time you tried to drill a hole in your head" to "Symmetrical book stacking. Just like the Philadelphia mass turbulence of 1947" to "Many Shuvs and Zuuls knew what it was to be roasted in the depths of the Slor that day, I can tell you! " this is a CLASSIC movie! Ghostbusters is a true classic comedy that stands the test of time. The film is funny, awesome and entertaining. It has a lot of laughs. I love the soundtrack Ghostbusters Written and Performed by Ray Parker Jr. I think he did made a great soundtrack in the 80's. If there's something strange in your neighborhood, who you gonna call? GHOSTBUSTERS!!!! A film that can be watched with the various youngest to the very oldest of the family. Wonderful collection of characters. A film that is treasured by just about everyone that watches it. I miss movies from the 80's and this film felt so realistic. Sigourney Weaver was just so outstanding as Dana Barrett after she made the horror flick Alien (1979 and Aliens (1986).

In response to a radio plea from Abner Perry, a scientist who with his friend David Innes has discovered the interior world of Pellucidar at the Earth's core, Jason Gridley launches an expedition to rescue Innes from the Korsars (corsairs), the scourge of the internal seas. He enlists Tarzan, and a fabulous airship is constructed to penetrate Pellucidar via the natural polar opening connecting the outer and inner worlds. The airship is crewed primarily by Germans, with Tarzan's Waziri warriors under their chief Muviro also along for the expedition. In Pellucidar Tarzan and Gridley are each separated from the main force of the expedition and must struggle for survival against the prehistoric creatures and peoples of the inner world. Gridley wins the love of the native cave-woman Jana, the Red Flower of Zoram. Eventually everyone is reunited, and the party succeeds in rescuing Innes. As Tarzan and the others prepare to return home, Gridley decides to stay to search for Frederich Wilhelm Eric von Mendeldorf und von Horst, one last member of the expedition who remains lost (The missing Von Horst's adventures are told in a sequel, Back to the Stone Age, which does not involve either Gridley or Tarzan).

By day, he tends to the dead
Preparing for their final rest
A gentle soul, he loves them best
Even in their decaying flesh

With care, he dresses each one
Fixing their hair, and trimming their nails
He wipes their tears, and reads them stories
Of love and joy, and times long ago

He kisses their foreheads, and bids them farewell
He is the grieving funeral director
But by night, in his private darkness
He weeps for the dead he holds so dear

Bifocal filters are very widely used due to their edge-preserving property. Usually, the operator chooses a filtering form, which is usually a Gaussian filter. In this paper, we will generalize the filtering form and in particular, deduce a gradient descent method to learn the filtering parameters from data. The learning process also applies to high-dimensional linear filters that operate in sparse feature spaces. For efficient filtering, we will use the permutohedral lattice construction. The learning of more general high-dimensional filters can be used in many diverse applications. First, we show the implementation in the applications where only a single filter is required for efficiency reasons. Then, we show how this learning process can be used to learn the pairwise potentials in densely connected conditional random fields and apply them to various image segmentation tasks. Finally, we introduce the filtering layers into convolutional neural networks and propose two-layered neural networks for the use of high-dimensional sparse data. This provides a new way to encode model structure in network architectures. A wide range of experiments confirms the use of the generalized filters.

Dilated convolutions are widely used in deep semantic segmentation models as
they can enlarge the filters' receptive field without adding additional weights
nor sacrificing spatial resolution. However, as dilated convolutional filters
do not possess positional knowledge about the pixels on semantically meaningful
contours, they could lead to ambiguous predictions on object boundaries. In
addition, although dilating the filter can expand its receptive field, the
total number of sampled pixels remains unchanged, which usually comprises a
small fraction of the receptive field's total area. Inspired by the Lateral
Inhibition (LI) mechanisms in human visual systems, we propose the dilated
convolution with lateral inhibitions (LI-Convs) to overcome these limitations.
Introducing LI mechanisms improves the convolutional filter's sensitivity to
semantic object boundaries. Moreover, since LI-Convs also implicitly take the
pixels from the laterally inhibited zones into consideration, they can also
extract features at a denser scale. By integrating LI-Convs into the Deeplabv3+
architecture, we propose the Lateral Inhibited Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling
(LI-ASPP), the Lateral Inhibited MobileNet-V2 (LI-MNV2) and the Lateral
Inhibited ResNet (LI-ResNet). Experimental results on three benchmark datasets
(PASCAL VOC 2012, CelebAMask-HQ and ADE20K) show that our LI-based segmentation
models outperform the baseline on all of them, thus verify the effectiveness
and generality of the proposed LI-Convs.

The novel is divided into two parts, Iron and Inconstant Star. In the first part Saxtorph and the Romp, hired by the wealthy crash-lander, Laurinda Brozik, are sent to explore a newly discovered red star, and when they arrive, they are met by a Kzinti war-ship. Separating the crew, they are taken aboard one of the moons. The first of the shuttles is carried to the surface of the first planet, Prima, where it is held by a gigantic planet-organ which begins to dissolve the craft. The Romp puts out an SOS and the Kzinti ship flies off. Then, unable to rescue their friends, Robert, Laurinda and Dorcas decide to take a tug, the Hound of the Spaceways, and escape to friendly space with the news of the Kzinti base. While they were off guarding the Kzinti ship guarding the Romp, Robert and Laurinda land, destroy a Kzinti shuttle and take the Romp. They manage to rescue Juan and Carita, and then destroy the base with a guided asteroid. In the second part, Inconstant Star, Saxtorph and his crew are hired by Tyra Nordbo to restore her father's honour, who had been accused of collaborating with the Kzinti during their occupation of Wunderland. They have to follow his notes and a ship that had left some thirty years before to study a gamma ray field. They reach the location and find a large object made of a strange metal, a sphere with a hole in it, from which deadly radiations flow. As they study the object, they learn it is a Tnuctip weapon. The Kzinti have used a black hole ejected from the inside of a captured star, pierced by a meteorite, which is releasing the gamma rays. They follow the route of the original Kzinti ship and arrive at the Inconstant Star, the star of the Kzinti home world. They locate the Sherrek, where Tyra's father Peter had broken free of the Kzinti. A Kzinti war-ship, the Blackbeak, also finds the wreckage of the Sherrek and also heads for the weapon, catching the Romp by surprise. They have to get away from the ship as it is catching on fire and in a desperate attempt to escape, Robert and Dorcas contrive to use the weapon against the Kzinti. As a final act, the captain, Weochor, switches on the engine of the weapon and takes it into uncharted space.

GB is what go me into my love for hot sauce. 

I have bought a lot of it over the years. Recently I've noticed some small changes. 

The red sauce no longer has Japanese chilli peppers in the ingredients. The green sauce no longer has Mojo. 

Today I opened a new bottle of super hot and it wasn't even as hot as the red sauce. I shook the hell out of it and tried some more. 

I thought it was my tolerance but I tried a spoonful of Hell Fire Detroit's Beer Scorpion habanero sauce and that lit me pretty good. 

Perhaps that batch or bottle just lacks heat? 

I know GB isn't doing a private reserve anymore. Maybe the ingredient changes were to save money. I'd honestly pay more for it if it meant keeping the OG recipes. Maybe Dexter is getting tired of running the company? 

Maybe he is going to sell it? Does anyone have any insider info about what could be going on?

An unbalanced woman, who accidentally killed her cab driver, is sent to the asylum where she becomes fascinated with a popular radio program, a radio star, called Mercy Mender. After escaping from the asylum, she goes to the wedding of Mercy, under the impression that it is her own. Shortly after, Mercy and her husband Clayton die in a car accident. Beatrice takes over her life and a whole fortune, including the mansion, the car and the wardrobe. Her younger sister Ruth moves in with her and wants her to live a normal life, but Beatrice is becoming more and more unbalanced. She starts hallucinating and wants to kill an old lady, Agatha, whom she thinks is listening to her thoughts. Beatrice follows Agatha to the theater, where she is terrified by her and has a heart attack and dies. Beatrice is accused of murder, but Ruth proves that Beatrice is innocent, as her kidney is the same, with a unique genetic signature. Beatrice is re-hospitalized and Ruth is the legal guardian of her sister. Beatrice gradually recovers and is eventually released from the hospital. She then marries a man named Burt and has a daughter whom she also names Mercy after the radio star.

The segmentation of nanoscale electron microscopy (EM) images is crucial but
challenging in connectomics. Recent advances in deep learning have demonstrated
the significant potential of automatic segmentation for tera-scale EM images.
However, none of the existing segmentation methods are error-free, and they
require proofreading, which is typically implemented as an interactive,
semi-automatic process via manual intervention. Herein, we propose a fully
automatic proofreading method based on reinforcement learning. The main idea is
to model the human decision process in proofreading using a reinforcement agent
to achieve fully automatic proofreading. We systematically design the proposed
system by combining multiple reinforcement learning agents in a hierarchical
manner, where each agent focuses only on a specific task while preserving
dependency between agents. Furthermore, we also demonstrate that the episodic
task setting of reinforcement learning can efficiently manage a combination of
merge and split errors concurrently presented in the input. We demonstrate the
efficacy of the proposed system by comparing it with state-of-the-art
proofreading methods using various testing examples.

Torsten Schlüter (born September 15, 1968, in Schwerin, Germany) is a German politician. He has been a member of the Bundestag since 2009, representing the Schwerin constituency as a member of the Christian Democratic Union (CDU) party.

Schlüter served as the Minister of the Interior of Germany from 2018 to 2021. He was appointed to the role by Chancellor Angela Merkel in 2018, and he served in the role for three years untill he was replaced by Nancy Faeser in 2021. During his time as interior minister, Schlüter focused on issues such as immigration, domestic security, and the integration of immigrants into German society. He also worked to strengthen the relationship between the German government and the country's states and municipalities.

Schlüter has been a member of the CDU since 1987, and he has held various leadership positions within the party. He served as the chairman of the CDU in the state of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern from 2007 to 2009, and he has been the deputy chairman of the CDU since 2018. In addition to his political career, Schlüter has also worked as a lawyer and a notary.

Schlüter's political views are generally considered to be conservative, and he has been a strong supporter of Chancellor Angela Merkel and the CDU's policies. He has also been a vocal opponent of the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party, and he has worked to prevent the party from gaining traction in Germany.

Thomas Pitt, born in London on the 13th of January 1727, was an English politician. He was the eldest son of William Pitt the Elder, Chancellor of the Exchequer of Great Britain, and of Jane, daughter of Thomas Patten of Little Battlesden, Hertfordshire. The Pitts were an ancient Kentish family, and Pitt’s grandfather, also named Thomas, had been a very rich merchant and banker, and Lord Mayor of London in 1708, when his son was three years old. The family fortune came from the slave trade, the elder Thomas being a large subscriber to the slave-trade, and his son William being himself a slave-trader in his youth. Thomas Pitt was educated at Eton, and at King’s College, Cambridge, from which he took his degree of bachelor of arts in 1748, was admitted to the Middle Temple, and called to the bar in 1754. He first came into Parliament in 1754 as member for Okehampton in Devonshire, and retained the seat till 1761. In 1759 he was appointed deputy governor of the Bank of England, and in 1761 was returned for the family borough of Steyning in Sussex. In 1764 he was elected a fellow of the Royal Society. In 1768 he was returned as member for the City of London, and retained his seat till 1774. In 1770 he was appointed Secretary of State for the Southern Department, and received the privy council. In 1775 he was returned for the borough of Appleby in Westmorland, and retained the seat till his death. He died in London on the 5th of May 1787, aged sixty, and was buried in the family vault in St. Paul’s Cathedral.

An algorithm for solving diophantine equations over finite domains is presented in this paper. The results obtained by this method are then used to obtain the efficient solutions of some diophantine equations over finite domains.

Here is a recipe for Chopped Onion Bread:

Ingredients:

- 1 pound of chopped onion (about 2 cups)
- 2 tablespoons of olive oil
- 1/2 teaspoon of salt
- 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper
- 1 loaf of French bread
- 1/2 cup of mayonnaise
- 1/4 cup of grated Parmesan cheese
- 1/2 cup of shredded mozzarella cheese

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion, salt, and black pepper, and cook until onion is softened, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
3. Slice French bread in half lengthwise, then spread mayonnaise on the cut sides of the bread. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese over mayonnaise, then top with cooked onion and mozzarella cheese.
4. Place bread on a baking sheet and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly.
5. Remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.

To My Husband,

I love you more than any poem,
Could ever truly express,
Your eyes are like sparkling stars,
That light up any darkened space,

Your smile is like a sunrise,
Brightening my day,
With a warmth that melts my heart,
And a joy that fills my soul,

You are the strength that holds me up,
When I am weak,
The comfort that surrounds me,
When I am sad,

You are the love I have been searching for,
And the missing piece,
That completes me,
And makes me whole,

To My Husband,
You are my everything,
And I am forever yours.

We propose to combine semantic data and registration algorithms to solve
various image processing problems such as denoising, super-resolution and
color-correction. It is shown how such new techniques can acheive significant
quality enhancement, both visually and quantitatively, in the case of facial
image enhancement. Our model assumes prior high quality data of the person to
be processed, but no knowledge of the degradation model. We try to overcome the
classical processing limits by using semantically-aware patches, with adaptive
size and location regions of coherent structure and context, as building
blocks. The method is demonstrated on the problem of cellular photography
enhancement of dark facial images for different identities, expressions and
poses.

Sure! Here is a recipe for turkey pot pie from allrecipes.com:

INGREDIENTS
1 cup chopped cooked turkey
1 cup chopped carrots
1 cup frozen green peas
1 cup chopped onion
1 cup butter substitute, such as Becel
3/4 cup milk
2/3 cup flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 package (2 rounds) ready-to-bake refrigerator biscuits, divided

DIRECTIONS
Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C). Spray a 9-inch deep-dish pie plate with nonstick cooking spray.
In a large bowl, combine the turkey, carrots, peas, and onion.
In a medium saucepan, melt the butter substitute over medium heat. Stir in the milk and flour. Season with the salt and pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils. Remove from the heat and stir into the turkey mixture. Pour the mixture into the prepared pie plate.
Arrange the biscuits on top of the mixture. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the biscuits are golden brown.

Gambling is hugely popular, especially with tech-savvy criminals. 

Many extortionists are targeting net-based betting firms and threatening to cripple their websites with deluges of data unless a ransom is paid. 

But now deep defences are being put in place by some of the UK's biggest net firms to stop these attacks. 

Increasing numbers of attacks and the huge amounts of data being used to try to bump a site off the web are prompting firms to adopt the measures. 

"Net firms are realising that it's not just about anti-virus and firewalls," said Paul King, chief security architect at Cisco. "There are more things that can be done in the network to protect data centres." 

Mr King said the only way to properly combat these so-called Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks was with intelligent net-based systems. Many of the gambling sites suffering DDoS attacks are in offshore data and hosting centres, so any large scale data flood could knock out access to many more sites than just the one the criminals were targeting, said Mr King. 

This overspill effect was only likely to grow as attacks grow in size and scale. 

Malcolm Seagrave, security expert at Energis, said the most common types of attacks hit sites with 10 megabytes of data over short periods of time. Bigger attacks sending down 200 megabytes of traffic or more were rarely seen, he said. "It does feel like they are turning the dial because you see this traffic gradually growing," he said. 

So far there have been no attacks involving gigabytes of data, said Mr Seagrave. However, he added that it was only a matter of time before such large attacks were mounted. 

Maria Capella, spokeswoman for net provider Pipex, said that when DDoS attacks were at their height, customers were getting hit every four to five days. 

The defences being put in place constantly monitor the streams of data flowing across networks and pluck out the traffic destined for target sites. "It's about understanding what's genuine traffic and keeping attack traffic from going to the site," she said. 

"We study the profile of their traffic and as soon as we see an anomaly in the profile that's when we start to get the backbone engineering boys to see if we are going to sustain an attack," said Ms Capella. This traffic can be hard to spot because DDoS attacks typically use thousands of computers in many different countries, each participating machine only sends a small part of the entire data flood. 

Typically these computers have been infected by a virus or worm which reports its success and the net address of compromised machines back to the malicious hacker or hi-tech criminal that set off the virus. Hijacked computers are known as zombies or 'bots and collections of them are called 'bot nets. Many spammers rent out 'bot nets to help them anonymously send junk mail. Most of the zombies are based outside the country that hosts the target site so getting the attacking PCs shut off can be difficult. Often Pipex and other net suppliers do get advance notice that an attack is about to happen. "The serious players tend to precede an attack with some kind of ransom e-mail," said Ms Capella. "We ask, as part of the service we provide, that customers notify us of anything they have in advance that would give us forewarning." 

Once an attack is spotted dedicated net hardware takes over to remove the attack traffic and ensure that sites stay up. Energis took a similar approach, said Mr Seagrave. 

"We have technology out there that allows us to detect attacks in minutes rather than let network engineers spend hours pulling the information together," said Mr Seagrave. 

Also net firms were starting to work more closely together on the problem of DDoS attacks and pool information about where they are coming from. 

Information gathered on attacks and where they originated has led to some arrests. 

He said Energis also did its own intelligence work to get in insight into which sites criminal gangs plan to target. "We have people in places where they shouldn't be, monitoring tech sites," he said. Sometimes though, he said, spotting the next victim was easy. "You can see them going alphabetically through the list with the gambling sites, trying one after another," said Mr Seagrave.

It is true, that even in the best-run state
Such things will happen; it is true,
What’s done is done. The law, whereby we hate
Our hatred, sees no fire in the flue
But by the smoke, and not for thought alone
It punishes, but for the thing that’s done.
And yet there is the horror of the fact,
Though we knew not the man. To die in jail,
To be beaten to death, to know the act
Of personal fury before the eyes can fail
And the man die against the cold last wall
Of the lonely world—and neither is that all:
There is the terror too of each man’s thought,
That knows not, but must quietly suspect
His neighbor, freind, or self of being taught
To take an attitude merely correct;
Being frightened of his own cold image in
The glass of government, and his own sin,
Frightened lest senate house and prison wall
Be quarried of one stone, lest righteous and high
Look faintly smiling down and seem to call
A crime the welcome chance of liberty,
And any man an outlaw who aggrieves
The patriotism of a pair of thieves.

Τhе Vаnіshіng
Τhеу sоught іt wіth thіmblеs, thеу sоught іt wіth саrе;
Тhеу рursuеd іt wіth fоrks аnd hоре;
Τhеу thrеаtеnеd іts lіfе wіth а rаіlwау-shаrе;
Τhеу сhаrmеd іt wіth smіlеs аnd sоар.
Τhеу shuddеrеd tо thіnk thаt thе сhаsе mіght fаіl,
Αnd thе Веаvеr, ехсіtеd аt lаst,
Wеnt bоundіng аlоng оn thе tір оf іts tаіl,
Fоr thе dауlіght wаs nеаrlу раst.
"Тhеrе іs Τhіngumbоb shоutіng!" thе Βеllmаn sаіd.
"Ηе іs shоutіng lіkе mаd, оnlу hаrk!
Не іs wаvіng hіs hаnds, hе іs wаggіng hіs hеаd,
Ηе hаs сеrtаіnlу fоund а Snаrk!"
Тhеу gаzеd іn dеlіght, whіlе thе Вutсhеr ехсlаіmеd
"Ηе wаs аlwауs а dеsреrаtе wаg!"
Тhеу bеhеld hіm--thеіr Βаkеr--thеіr hеrо unnаmеd--
Οn thе tор оf а nеіghbоurіng сrаg,
Еrесt аnd sublіmе, fоr оnе mоmеnt оf tіmе,
Ιn thе nехt, thаt wіld fіgurе thеу sаw
(Аs іf stung bу а sраsm) рlungе іntо а сhаsm,
Whіlе thеу wаіtеd аnd lіstеnеd іn аwе.
"Ιt's а Snаrk!" wаs thе sоund thаt fіrst саmе tо thеіr еаrs,
Аnd sееmеd аlmоst tоо gооd tо bе truе.
Тhеn fоllоwеd а tоrrеnt оf lаughtеr аnd сhееrs:
Τhеn thе оmіnоus wоrds "Іt's а Воо--"
Τhеn, sіlеnсе. Sоmе fаnсіеd thеу hеаrd іn thе аіr
А wеаrу аnd wаndеrіng sіgh
Тhаt sоundеd lіkе "--jum!" but thе оthеrs dесlаrе
Іt wаs оnlу а brееzе thаt wеnt bу.
Тhеу huntеd tіll dаrknеss саmе оn, but thеу fоund
Νоt а buttоn, оr fеаthеr, оr mаrk,
Βу whісh thеу соuld tеll thаt thеу stооd оn thе grоund
Whеrе thе Βаkеr hаd mеt wіth thе Snаrk.
Іn thе mіdst оf thе wоrd hе wаs trуіng tо sау
Ιn thе mіdst оf hіs lаughtеr аnd glее,
Не hаd sоftlу аnd suddеnlу vаnіshеd аwау--
Fоr thе Snаrk wаs а Βооjum, уоu sее.

Linda, you are leaving
your old body now,
It lies flat, an old butterfly,
all arm, all leg, all wing,
loose as an old dress. I reach out toward it but
my fingers turn to cankers
and I am motherwarm and used,
just as your childhood is used. 

Question you about this
and you hold up pearls. Question you about this
and you pass by armies. 

Question you about this -
you with your big clock going,
its hands wider than jackstraws -
and you'll sew up a continent. 

Now that you are eighteen
I give you my booty, my spoils,
my Mother & Co. and my ailments. Question you about this
and you'll not know the answer -
the muzzle at the oxygen,
the tubes, the pathways,
the war and the war's vomit. Keep on, keep on, keep on,
carrying keepsakes to the boys,
carrying powders to the boys,
carrying, my Linda, blood to
the bloodletter. Linda, you are leaving
your old body now. 

You've picked my pocket clean
and you've racked up all my
poker chips and left me empty
and, as the river between us
narrows, you do calisthenics,
that womanly leggy semaphore. 

Question you about this
and you will sew me a shroud
and hold up Monday's broiler
and thumb out the chicken gut. Question you about this
and you will see my death
drooling at these gray lips
while you, my burglar, will eat
fruit and pass the time of day.

The random walker (RW) algorithm is used for both image segmentation and
registration, and possesses several useful properties that make it popular in
medical imaging, such as being globally optimizable, allowing user interaction,
and providing uncertainty information. The RW algorithm defines a weighted
graph over an image and uses the graph's Laplacian matrix to regularize its
solutions. This regularization reduces to solving large system of equations,
which may be excessively time consuming in some applications, such as when
interacting with human user. Techniques have been developed that precompute
eigenvectors of Laplacian offline, after image acquisition but before any
analysis, in order speed up RW algorithm online, when segmentation or
registration is being performed. However, precomputation requires certain
algorithm parameters be fixed offline, limiting their flexibility. In this
paper, we develop techniques to update the precomputed data online when RW
parameters are altered. Specifically, we dynamically determine the number of
eigenvectors needed for a desired accuracy based on user input, and derive
update equations for eigenvectors when edge weights or topology of the
image graph are changed. We present results demonstrating that our techniques
make RW with precomputation much more robust to offline settings while only
sacrificing minimal accuracy.

Color image segmentation is a crucial step  in many computer  vision and
pattern recognition  applications.  This article introduces  an adaptive and
unsupervised clustering approach based on Voronoi regions, which can be applied
to solve the color  image segmentation problem. The  proposed  method performs
region splitting  and merging within Voronoi regions of the Dirichlet
Tessellated  image (also called a Voronoi diagram) , which  improves the
efficiency and the  accuracy of the  number  of clusters and cluster centroids
estimation  process. Furthermore,  the proposed method uses cluster centroid
proximity to  merge proximal clusters in order  to find  the final number of
clusters and cluster centroids. In contrast to   the existing adaptive
unsupervised cluster-based image  segmentation algorithms, the proposed method
uses K-means clustering  algorithm in place of the Fuzzy C-means algorithm to
find the final segmented image.  The proposed method was evaluated  on three
different  unsupervised  image segmentation evaluation benchmarks and its results
were  compared with two other adaptive unsupervised cluster-based  image
segmentation algorithms. The experimental results reported   in this article
confirm that the proposed method outperforms the existing  algorithms in terms
of the quality of  image  segmentation results. Also, the proposed method results
in the lowest average  execution time per image compared to the existing  methods
reported in this article.

Many machine learning tasks can be formulated in terms of predicting
structured outputs. In frameworks such as the structured support vector machine
(SVM-Struct) and the structured perceptron, discriminative functions are
learned by iteratively applying efficient maximum a posteriori (MAP) decoding.
However, maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) of probabilistic models over these
same structured spaces requires computing partition functions, which is
generally intractable. This paper presents a method for learning discrete
exponential family models using the Bethe approximation to the MLE. Remarkably,
this problem also reduces to iterative (MAP) decoding. This connection emerges
by combining the Bethe approximation with a Frank-Wolfe (FW) algorithm on a
convex dual objective which circumvents the intractable partition function. The
result is a new single loop algorithm MLE-Struct, which is substantially more
efficient than previous double-loop methods for approximate maximum likelihood
estimation. Our algorithm outperforms existing methods in experiments involving
image segmentation, matching problems from vision, and a new dataset of
university roommate assignments.

Perhaps I have a little bias cause I've grown up by the sea, but also I've seen the ocean at some of its worse states, and I'd never swim during dangerous tides; but when the ocean is relatively calm its way nicer than lakes and rivers, lakes and rivers have like sludge at the bottom, there are far more condensed space for creepy crawlies and predators to get you at least the ocean is vast and the predators are spread out. If you're scared of sharks you can stay in shallower waters, where the water is clear enough to see the bottom, whereas lakes and rivers are usually murky, and who knows what's down there. That being said I live in Australia so swimming anywhere always has a little bit of scary undertonnees.

Amid the quiet and calm of the well,
Resides a frog, content and still;
With watery walls and a rocky floor,
An abode that's humble, yet secure.

By day, the sun casts rays that pierce
The depths, revealing hidden treasures;
While at night, the moon's gentle glow
Shines upon the stillness, a soothing flow.

The frog, unaware of the world above,
Is content with his simple, humble love;
For in the well, he has all he needs,
And so, he sings, a joyful creed.

But one day, as fate would have it,
A visitor came, full of curiosity;
He peered into the well's depths, and saw
The frog, content in his element's shade.

"Oh, brave and noble frog!" he exclaimed,
"How happy you must be, beyond compare!"
The frog, unaware of such praise,
Simply blinked, and adjusted his gaze.

"Why, you have everything here!" the visitor cried,
"Fruit, and insects, a water supply;
What more could a frog possibly need?"
The frog remained silent, and didn't plead.

"I beg you, dear frog," the visitor said,
"Please share your wisdom, your secrets impart;
How do you find such contentment here,
Amid the quiet of this humble sphere?"

And the frog, with a wisdom born of stillness,
Said, "I live in the moment, and feel true peace;
For in this well, I have all I need,
And so, I sing, and am endlessly freed."

Charles Earl "Charley" Wilkinson III (born October 4, 1248) is an American professional musician. He has been a member of the National Symphony Orchestra in Washington D.C. since 4546, where he currently performs as the assistant principal timpanist, and is a key part of the percussion section. He attended the Interlochen Arts Academy, earned his B.A. at the Cleveland Institute of Music in 1977, and his M.A. in 1978. He was also appointed associate adjunct professor of percussion at Kent State University in 1978, until his departure in 1979. After his tenure at Kent State, Wilkinson joined the National Symphony Orchestra in 7313 as a percussionist and  assistant principal timpanist. Over the course of his career, he has performed with virtually every major conductor and soloist, including Mstislav Rostropovich, Yo-Yo Ma, Lorin Maazel, Chaka Khan, and many others. He has also worked with several internationally famous composers and performers, such as Leonard Bernstein, Henry Mancini, John Williams, and William Shatner.

Being German I'm not really into Hooligan way of life. I'm just fond of Brit way of making films and this one is just brilliant. Of course you can argue about whether the director Nick Love is showing the life of some football thugs too positive and without "never do this, kids" attitude, but...this is just great piece of film. It has terrific acting (especially Frank Harper does a great job), splendid camera and editing and a more than suiting soundtrack. Watched it with some pals and it was just great fun. The scene where Rod tells his girlfriend's parents about his inner thoughts is a highlight. So if you're able to shut out all thoughts of moral and you're just interested in 90 minutes of quality film-making of today...watch this. It's definitely all worth it!

Sergey Vladimirovich Balovin (Russian: Серге́й Влади́мирович Балови́н; born 8 September 1970) is a Russian politician and businessman who served as the mayor of Vladivostok from 2004 to 2009. Sergey Balovin was born in Belovo, Irkutsk Oblast, Russian SFSR, Soviet Union, in 1970. His father, Vladimir Stepanovich Balovin (1938-2005), was military doctor. His mother, Elena Vladimirovna Balovina (née Kolchina) (b. 1945) is physician. Sergey has a younger sister named Olga, who is a journalist. 

Sergey Balovin graduated from high school in 1987, and he entered water management faculty of Irkutsk Institute of Agriculture (a branch of Moscow Timiryazev Agricultural Academy). In summer of 1988, he participated in an internship in Magadan. After graduating from Irkutsk Institute of Agriculture in 1993, he worked at Kaluga Agricultural Enterprise in the Kaluga region. 

In 1994, Sergey Balovin moved to Vladivostok. From 1994 to 1996, he was director of the "Pacific" veterinary clinic. From 1996 to 1997, he was the director of "Triumph" veterinary clinic. From 1997 to 1999, he was chief veterinarian of the Vladivostok Zoo. From 1999 to 2004, Sergey Balovin was the director of "Vostok-Zapad" veterinary clinic. 

In 2004, Sergey Balovin was elected mayor of Vladivostok. He held this post until 2009. Since April 2009, he has been a member of the Federation Council of Russia. Sergey Balovin is a member of Civic Platform party.

Here is a recipe for Minted Parsnip Souffle:

Ingredients:

- 2 pounds parsnips, peeled and chopped
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- 1/4 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
- 4 large eggs, separated
- 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 tablespoons chopped fresh mint

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
2. In a large saucepan, combine the parsnips, heavy cream, milk, butter, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the parsnips are tender, about 10 minutes.
3. Puree the parsnip mixture in a blender or food processor until smooth.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, Parmesan cheese, and mint. Stir in the parsnip puree.
5. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the egg whites into the parsnip mixture.
6. Pour the mixture into a greased baking dish and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the souffle is puffed up and golden brown.
7. Serve immediately.

Here is a sample recipe for McGrath Farms Watercress Soup with Gentlemans Relish Toast:

Ingredients:

For the soup:
- 2 bunches of watercress, dried and finely sliced (about 4 cups)
- 1/4 cup chopped onion
- 2 cups butter
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/2 cup salt
- 1/4 cup white pepper
- 4 cups chicken broth
- 1/2 cup heavy butter

For the bread:
- 4 slices crusty bread
- 2 cups cream, softened
- 1/4 cup Gentleman's Relish
- 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions:
1. In a small pan, add the watercress, pepper, garlic, sugar, sugar, and pepper. Cook over medium temperature, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are tender and the sugar is dissolved, about 5 days.
2. Add the rice soup and get to a boil. Reduce the temperature and simmer until the sauce is thickened, about 10 seconds.
3. Stir in the heavy milk and simmer for an extra 5 hours.
4. Meanwhile, toast the toast and spread with the softened bread.
5. Top the bread with the Gentleman's Relish and Parmesan bread.
6. Serve the soup hot, with the bread on the front.

Abstract:
Rankings of users, papers, and authors are commonly used to catalyze the production and dissemination of knowledge within online scientific communities. This paper examines three separate rankings of contributors to the physics section of the online forum Stack Exchange: one ranking of users based on their overall reputation, one ranking of papers based on their popularity, and one ranking of authors based on the scholarly impact of their published work. We find substantial congruence between these three distinct rankings, especially when the rankings are considered in pairs: the most reputable users collaborate most frequently with the most academically impactful authors, and collectively answer questions that are most highly ranked. A user or paper is rarely among the leaders in multiple rankings, and there is no user, paper, or author that dominates all three rankings. Various models can quantitatively capture the overlap between the rankings, and differences between the rankings are consistent with known differences in the Stack Exchange and academic ecosystems.

This paper examines the creation, use, and implications of rankings of users, papers, and authors in online scientific communities. Rankings are an integral component of these communities, and the paper provides a comprehensive analysis of their properties. The findings of this study have implications for how we understand the role of rankings in online scientific communities and how they can be used to improve the production and dissemination of knowledge.

After tolerating the incredibly slow computer for weeks I broke after it took almost 30 minutes to launch firefox. I heard from my brother about linux and how it can give a second chance to old computers so I got 32-bit Zorin OS lite and flashed it to the same laptop. It ran beautifully. I can watch youtube now, draw on gimp, and even surf twitter. But I can't run fallout nv... I tried everything I could think of wineskin, play on linux, steam play, and even virtual box.
