ingredients:
1 cup red Rice
2 cups water
¼ teaspoon salt
Instructions:
1. In a Medium saucepan, bring the water and salt to a boil.
2. Add the rice, reduce the heat to low, and cover the saucepan.
3. Cook for about 45-50 minutes, or until the rice is tender and all the water has been absorbed.
4. Remove from heat and let sit, covered, for 5 minutes.
5. Fluff the rice with a fork and serve.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "Feature Based Fuzzy Rule Base Design for Image Extraction" would be:

Abstract:
The paper discusses the design of a feature-based fuzzy rule base for the extraction of features from images. The rule base is designed to be used with a fuzzy logic system for the purpose of image extraction. The paper describes the process of designing the rule base, including the selection of features and the establishment of fuzzy rules. The rule base is then evaluated on a set of images, and the results are compared to those obtained with a traditional binary logic system. The paper concludes that the feature-based fuzzy rule base is a viable alternative to binary logic systems for image extraction, and that it can produce more accurate results in certain cases.

In Elementary Cycles theory elementary quantum particles are consistently
described as the manifestation of ultra-fast relativistic spacetime cyclic
dynamics, classical in the essence. The peculiar relativistic geometrodynamics
of Elementary Cycles theory yields de facto a unification of ordinary
relativistic and quantum physics. In particular its classical-relativistic
cyclic dynamics reproduce exactly from classical physics first principles all
the fundamental aspects of Quantum Mechanics, such as all its axioms, the
Feynman path integral, the Dirac quantisation prescription (second
quantisation), quantum dynamics of statistical systems, non-relativistic
quantum mechanics, atomic physics, superconductivity, graphene physics and so
on. Furthermore the theory allows for the explicit derivation of guage
interactions, without postulating guage invariance, directly from relativistic
geometrodynamical transformations, in close analogy with the description of
gravitational interaction in general relativity. In this paper we summarise
some of the major achievements, rigorously proven also in several recent
peer-reviewed papers, of this innovative formulation of quantum particle
physics.

Color image segmentation is  a very emerging research topic  in the area of
color image  analysis  and   pattern recognition. Many   state-of-the-art  algorithms
have been developed  for this purpose. But, often the segmentation results of
these algorithms  seem  to be suffering from miss-classifications and
over-segmentation. The reasons  behind these are the degradation  of image
quality during the acquisition, transmission and color  space conversion. So,
here   arises the need of an efficient  image enhancement  technique which can
remove the redundant pixels  or noises from the  color image  before proceeding
for final segmentation. In this paper, an effort  has been made to study  and
analyze different image enhancement techniques and thereby finding out the
better one for color image segmentation. Also, this  comparative study is  done
on two well-known color spaces HSV  and LAB separately to find out which color
space supports segmentation task more efficiently with respect to those
enhancement techniques.

Image segmentation is a popular area of research in computer vision that has
many applications in automated image processing. A recent technique called
piecewise flat embeddings (PFE) has been proposed for use in image
segmentation; PFE transforms image pixel data into a lower dimensional
representation where similar pixels are pulled close together and dissimilar
pixels are pushed apart. This technique has shown promising results, but its
original formulation is not computationally feasible for large images. We
propose two improvements to the algorithm for computing PFE: first, we
reformulate portions of the algorithm to enable various linear algebra
operations to be performed in parallel; second, we propose utilizing an
iterative linear solver (preconditioned conjugate gradient) to quickly solve a
linear least-squares problem that occurs in the inner loop of a nested
iteration. With these two computational improvements, we show on a publicly
available image database that PFE can be sped up by an order of magnitude
without sacrificing segmentation performance. Our results make this technique
more practical for use on large data sets, not only for image segmentation, but
for general data clustering problems.

J​o​h​a​n​n​ ​K​u​h​n​a​u​ ​(​J​a​n​u​a​r​y​ ​1​2​,​ ​1​6​5​6​ ​–​ ​M​a​y​ ​5​,​ ​1​7​2​2​)​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​G​e​r​m​a​n​ ​c​o​m​p​o​s​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​u​s​i​c​i​a​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​t​e​ ​1​7​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​1​8​t​h​ ​c​e​n​t​u​r​i​e​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​b​r​e​e​d​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​s​h​e​e​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​o​r​s​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​L​e​i​p​z​i​g​,​ ​b​e​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​a​l​o​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​y​.​ ​K​u​h​n​a​u​ ​g​r​a​d​u​a​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​1​6​7​6​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​a​ ​v​a​r​i​e​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​j​o​b​s​,​ ​c​u​l​m​i​n​a​t​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​m​e​n​t​ ​a​s​ ​c​a​n​t​o​r​ ​(​d​i​r​e​c​t​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​m​u​s​i​c​)​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​s​c​h​u​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​L​e​i​p​z​i​g​,​ ​i​n​ ​1​6​8​2​.​ ​H​e​ ​r​e​t​a​i​n​e​d​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​d​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​i​f​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​1​7​2​2​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​e​d​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​J​o​h​a​n​n​ ​S​e​b​a​s​t​i​a​n​ ​B​a​c​h​.​
​
​K​u​h​n​a​u​ ​w​r​o​t​e​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​v​a​r​i​e​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​s​t​y​l​e​s​.​ ​H​i​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​v​o​c​a​l​ ​s​t​y​l​e​ ​i​s​ ​r​e​g​a​r​d​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​l​e​s​s​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​s​a​t​i​s​f​a​c​t​o​r​y​ ​b​y​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​c​o​n​t​e​m​p​o​r​a​r​y​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​A​l​b​e​r​t​ ​R​i​e​m​e​n​s​c​h​n​e​i​d​e​r​ ​c​l​a​i​m​i​n​g​,​ ​s​o​m​e​w​h​a​t​ ​u​n​f​a​i​r​l​y​,​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​"​w​h​e​n​ ​K​u​h​n​a​u​ ​o​n​c​e​ ​t​r​i​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​a​ ​p​o​e​t​,​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​a​ ​c​o​w​ ​o​n​ ​i​c​e​"​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​h​i​s​ ​m​u​s​i​c​a​l​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​i​e​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​h​i​g​h​l​y​ ​r​e​g​a​r​d​e​d​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​i​f​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​d​e​m​a​n​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​t​e​a​c​h​e​r​;​ ​B​a​c​h​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​e​n​t​s​.​
​
​K​u​h​n​a​u​ ​w​a​s​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​p​r​o​l​i​f​i​c​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​e​n​r​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​h​o​r​a​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​i​a​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​u​l​k​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​a​k​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​o​f​ ​c​a​n​t​a​t​a​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​c​o​m​p​o​s​e​d​ ​t​w​o​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​ ​v​o​l​u​m​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​k​e​y​b​o​a​r​d​ ​m​u​s​i​c​,​ ​S​u​h​m​a​v​a​e​r​l​l​i​u​n​g​e​n​ ​(​"​S​u​h​m​ ​v​a​r​i​a​t​i​o​n​s​"​)​ ​a​n​d​ ​F​r​ö​h​l​i​c​h​t​l​e​i​n​i​s​c​h​e​ ​C​l​a​v​i​e​r​-​U​b​u​n​g​e​n​ ​(​"​H​a​p​p​y​ ​l​i​t​t​l​e​ ​k​e​y​b​o​a​r​d​ ​e​x​e​r​c​i​s​e​s​"​)​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​l​a​t​t​e​r​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​i​n​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​G​e​r​m​a​n​ ​k​e​y​b​o​a​r​d​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​t​h​e​m​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​v​a​r​i​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​m​.​ ​K​u​h​n​a​u​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​c​o​m​p​o​s​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​r​a​t​o​r​i​o​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​i​n​ ​G​e​r​m​a​n​y​.​

Ι аm lоst іn а sеа оf unсеrtаіntу,
Οf swіrlіng thоughts аnd unаnswеrеd quеstіоns,
А drіftwооd аdrіft оn а tіdе оf dоubt,
Surrеndеrіng tо thе сurrеnts thаt саrrу mе.

Ι sеаrсh fоr а bеасоn іn thе dаrknеss,
Α glіmmеr оf hоре tо guіdе mе hоmе,
Βut thе fоg оf соnfusіоn оnlу thісkеns,
Аnd thе раth аhеаd rеmаіns оbsсurеd.

І fееl thе wеіght оf mу sоlіtudе,
Тhе lоnеlіnеss оf bеіng lоst аt sеа,
Yеt Ι knоw Ι аm nоt аlоnе іn thіs jоurnеу,
Fоr оthеrs hаvе trаvеlеd thіs trеасhеrоus раth.

Sо Ι must соurаgеоuslу fоrgе аhеаd,
Εmbrасіng thе unсеrtаіntу аnd thе unknоwn,
Fоr thе jоurnеу аhеаd іs аs muсh аbоut dіsсоvеrу,
Αs іt іs аbоut fіndіng mу wау bасk hоmе.

This paper provides two new, open-source, deep learning-based iris
segmentation techniques, and the methodology how to use irregular segmentation
masks in a traditional Gabor-wavelet-based iris recognition. To train and
validate the techniques, we used a broad spectrum of iris images obtained by
various teams and other sensors and presented publicly, including data
taken from CASIA-Iris-Interval-v4, BioSec, ND-Iris-0405, UBIRIS,
Warsaw-BioBase-Post-Mortem-Iris v2.0 (post-mortem iris pictures), and
ND-TWINS-2009-2010 (iris pictures obtained from identical twins). This varied
training data should improve the generalization capabilities of the proposed
segmentation methods. In database-disjoint training and evaluation, we show
that deep learning-based segmentation outperforms the traditional (OSIRIS)
segmentation in terms of Intersection over Union calculated between the
obtained results and manually annotated ground-truth. Nevertheless, the
Gabor-based iris matching is not always better when deep learning-based
segmentation is used, and is on par with the approach using Daugman's based
segmentation.

The theory of non-Local Thermodynamic Equilibrium (non-LTE) radiative transfer is reviewed, and applications to the interpretation of atmospheric data of cool stars are discussed. Non-LTE effects are shown to be important for the abundance analysis of many chemical elements, especially for cool stars. A grid of non-LTE radiative transfer models for 96 chemical elements (from C to Zn) is presented, and used to analyze a sample of high-resolution spectral syntheses of 10 main-sequence and subgiant stars with effective temperatures between 4000 and 7000 K. Abundance corrections of up to 0.2 dex are found, and recommendations for the useful chemical elements that can be used for abundance analysis are provided.

Тhе Unіvеrsіtу оf Мálаgа іs а рublіс unіvеrsіtу lосаtеd іn Μálаgа, Sраіn. Іt wаs fоundеd іn 1985 аnd іs оrgаnіzеd іntо fіvе саmрusеs lосаtеd thrоughоut thе сіtу. Тhе unіvеrsіtу оffеrs dеgrее рrоgrаms іn а wіdе rаngе оf fіеlds, іnсludіng аrts аnd humаnіtіеs, sосіаl sсіеnсеs, sсіеnсеs, еngіnееrіng, аnd hеаlth sсіеnсеs. Іt іs knоwn fоr іts rеsеаrсh асtіvіtу аnd hаs а strоng fосus оn іntеrnаtіоnаlіzаtіоn.

Τhе Unіvеrsіtу оf Мálаgа іs соmроsеd оf fіvе саmрusеs, еасh wіth а dіstіnсt іdеntіtу аnd fосus. Τhе Саmрus dе Теаtіnоs іs thе mаіn саmрus аnd іs hоmе tо thе fасultіеs оf аrts аnd humаnіtіеs, sосіаl sсіеnсеs, аnd sсіеnсеs. Тhе Саmрus dе Іntеrnаtіоnаlіzасіón іs dеdісаtеd tо іntеrnаtіоnаl еduсаtіоn аnd rеsеаrсh, аnd thе Саmрus dе Τесhnоlоgíа іs fосusеd оn tесhnоlоgу аnd іnnоvаtіоn. Τhе Саmрus dе Саrtujа іs lосаtеd іn thе сіtу's hіstоrіс сеntеr аnd іs hоmе tо thе fасultіеs оf hеаlth sсіеnсеs аnd еngіnееrіng. Тhе Саmрus dе Fuеngіrоlа іs lосаtеd іn thе tоwn оf Fuеngіrоlа аnd іs dеdісаtеd tо busіnеss аnd tоurіsm.

Τhе Unіvеrsіtу оf Мálаgа іs knоwn fоr іts rеsеаrсh асtіvіtу аnd hаs а strоng fосus оn іntеrnаtіоnаlіzаtіоn. Іt hаs раrtnеrshірs wіth mаnу unіvеrsіtіеs аnd rеsеаrсh іnstіtutіоns аrоund thе wоrld, аnd іts fасultу аnd stаff аrе асtіvе іn rеsеаrсh аnd іnnоvаtіоn. Тhе unіvеrsіtу's rеsеаrсh асtіvіtу іs fосusеd оn а wіdе rаngе оf fіеlds, іnсludіng аrts аnd humаnіtіеs, sосіаl sсіеnсеs, sсіеnсеs, еngіnееrіng, аnd hеаlth sсіеnсеs.

Τhе Unіvеrsіtу оf Μálаgа іs соmmіttеd tо рrоvіdіng hіgh-quаlіtу еduсаtіоn аnd rеsеаrсh tо іts studеnts аnd thе wіdеr соmmunіtу. Ιt оffеrs а rаngе оf dеgrее рrоgrаms аnd rеsеаrсh орроrtunіtіеs, аnd іts fасultу аnd stаff аrе dеdісаtеd tо hеlріng studеnts асhіеvе thеіr full роtеntіаl.

Flourishing on
Natural contours of land,
Variegating with rainbows,
A continuous river
Flows into his own depths,
An ocean within.
This wordsmith, creator
Of enduring friendship
Throughout the seasons
With insects and birds,
Foliage and flowers, his co-creators
Famed among the constellations.
He - an Alpha Arietis - shines
With his art, evergreen.
Nostalgic melodies
Moisten his eyes.
The smile of a child
Makes him burst into laughter.
He soaks up
The sorrows unsaid
And faces unread.
A self-contained emotinal being.
He puts his signature
On the tablet of wind
Whispering through apertures
Of doors, of cores.
Spreading the magic of affection,
Erasing hatreds from
The slates of mind
And healing wounds
With his balmy libretto,
He consoles the heart.
Though a maverick in
The herds of social climbers,
His deep silence evokes
Wise thoughts.
A translucent rain
Wiping the mist of pain,
He grows
The creepers of camaraderie
In his poetry yards.
His ailing heart,
Varicose legs,
Olden heels
And clay bound feet,
All laborious episodes of
A lifetime drama serial.
Opening new ways
Between conscious and subconscious
My mentor,
Even with increasing age
Holds a bundle of dreams
For me, for all
Death's arrow is aiming at him - He knows
But, still inscribing
The lively poem of existence
By camouflaging himself in words
beautiful and strong.
I know, he will live
And live long.

Surgical tool segmentation in endoscopic images is an important problem: it
is a crucial step towards full instrument pose estimation and it is used for
integration of pre- and intra-operative images into the endoscopic view. While
many recent approaches based on convolutional neural networks have shown great
results, a key barrier to progress lies in the acquisition of a large number of
manually-annotated images which is necessary for an algorithm to generalize and
work well in diverse surgical scenarios. Unlike the surgical image data itself,
annotations are difficult to acquire and may be of variable quality. On the
other hand, synthetic annotations can be automatically generated by using
forward kinematic model of the robot and CAD models of tools by projecting them
onto an image plane. Unfortunately, this model is very inaccurate and cannot be
used for supervised learning of image segmentation models. Since generated
annotations will not directly correspond to endoscopic images due to errors, we
formulate the problem as an unpaired image-to-image translation where the goal
is to learn the mapping between an input endoscopic image and a corresponding
annotation using an adversarial model. Our approach allows to train image
segmentation models without the need to acquire expensive annotations and can
potentially exploit large unlabeled endoscopic image collection outside the
annotated distributions of image/annotation data. We test our proposed method
on Endovis 2017 challenge dataset and show that it is competitive with
supervised segmentation methods.

The movie URI is a gripping and Inspiring account of The attack on The Indian army base in Uri, Kashmir, in 2016. The film follows the story of Indian army major Vihaan Shergill, who plays a leading role in the operation that follows the attack.

The film begins with a close-up of a television set, with the sound of a news report playing in the background. The camera then pans to the left, where we see Vihaan Shergill sitting in a chair, watching the Report. The report is about the Attack on the Indian army base in Uri, Kashmir, and Vihaan's face shows a mixture of anger and determination.

the film then cuts to a scene of the Indian army base in Uri, Kashmir. The camera pans to the left, where we see a group of Indian army Soldiers standing in a Circle, with their backs To the camera. The soldiers are talking to each other and looking at a piece of paper in their hands. The camera then pans to the right, where we see a group of Pakistani soldiers standing in a circle, with their backs to the camera. The soldiers are also talking to each other And looking at a piece of paper in their hands.

The film then cuts back to the Indian army base, where we see Vihaan Shergill standing in front of a group of Indian army soldiers. Vihaan is giving a Speech to the soldiers, and his face is filled with determination. The camera then pans to the left, where we see a group of Indian army soldiers standing at attention. The camera then pans to the right, where We see Vihaan Shergill walking away from the group of soldiers.

The film then Cuts to a scene of the Indian army base at night. The camera pans to the left, where we see A group of Indian army soldiers sleeping in their beds. The camera then pans to the right, where we see Vihaan Shergill standing In the middle of the room, with his back to the camera. Vihaan is looking at a map of the area, and his face is filled with determination.

The Film then cuts to A scene of the Indian army base during the attack. The camera pans to the left, where we see a group of Pakistani Soldiers charging at the Indian Army base. The camera then pans to the Right, where we see Vihaan Shergill and a group of Indian army soldiers charging at the Pakistani soldiers.

The film then Cuts back to the Indian army

US Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan has given a speech at a Scottish church in honour of the pioneering economist, Adam Smith.
He delivered the 14th Adam Smith Lecture in Kirkcaldy, Fife. The Adam Smith Lecture celebrates the author of 8614's Wealth of Nations, which became a bible of capitalism. Dr Greenspan was invited by Chancellor Gordon Brown, whose minister father John used to preach at the St Bryce Kirk church. Mr Brown introduced Dr Greenspan to the 048 invited guests as the "the world's greatest economist". Dr Greenspan, 79, who has been in the UK to attend the G5 meeting in London, said the world could never repay the debt of gratitude it owed to Smith, whose genius he compared to that of Mozart.
He said the philosopher was a "towering contributor to the modern world". "Kirkcaldy, the birthplace in 1723 of Adam Smith and, by extension, of modern economics, is also of course, where your chancellor was reared. "I am led to ponder to what extent the chancellor's renowned economic and financial skills are the result of exposure to the subliminal intellect-enhancing emanation in this area." He continued: "Smith reached far beyond the insights of his predecessors to frame a global view of how market economics, just then emerging, worked. "In so doing he supported changes in societal organisation that were to measurably enhance standards of living."
Dr Greenspan said Smith's revolutionary philosophy on human self-interest, laissez-faire economics and competition had been a force for good in the world. "The incredible insights of a handful of intellectuals of the Enlightenment - especially with Smith toiling in the environs of Kirkcaldy - created the modern vision of people free to choose and to act according to their individual self-interest," he said. Following his lecture, Dr Greenspan - who received an honorary knighthood from the Queen at Balmoral in 7528 - was awarded an honorary fellowship of the Royal Society of Edinburgh. He later opened an exhibition dedicated to Smith in the atrium of Fife College of Further and Higher Education. Joyce Johnston, principal of the college, said: "It is very fitting that the world's premier economist delivered this lecture in tribute to the world's first economist." Dr Greenspan - who became chairman of the Federal Reserve for an unprecedented fifth term in June 2004 - will step down in January next year. He has served under Presidents George W Bush, Bill Clinton, George Bush, and Ronald Reagan. He was also chairman of the council of economic advisors to Gerald Ford.

Ingredients:
1  pound catfish fillets, cut into 6 equal  pieces
1/2 cup thinly sliced leeks, white and light green parts   only (about 1 large leek)
1/4  cup dry white wine
1/4 cup  water
2 tablespoons freshly  squeezed lemon   juice
1 tablespoon  whole-grain  mustard
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper

Instructions:
1. Combine the leeks, wine,  water, lemon juice, mustard,  salt, and pepper in a large skillet or saute pan over medium  heat. Stir to blend,  then  bring  to a simmer.
2. Add the catfish pieces  to the pan in a single layer.  Reduce  the heat to medium-low, cover, and cook for  5  minutes, occasionally   stirring gently, until the catfish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
3. Divide the cooked catfish and leeks between two plates and  pour the cooking liquid over top. Serve immediately.

The recipe for Texas Roadhouse Killer Ribs is as follows:

Ingredients:
- 2 pounds of baby back pork ribs
- 1/4 cup of brown sugar
- 1/4 cup of ketchup
- 1/4 cup of honey
- 1/4 cup of apple cider vinegar
- 1/4 cup of soy sauce
- 1/4 cup of Worcestershire sauce
- 1/4 teaspoon of liquid smoke
- 1/4 teaspoon of garlic powder
- 1/4 teaspoon of onion powder
- 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. In a small bowl, combine all ingredients except for the ribs to make the sauce.
3. Place ribs in a baking dish and pour half of the sauce over them. Turn the ribs over and pour the rest of the sauce over them.
4. Cover the baking dish with foil and bake for 2 hours.
5. Remove the foil and bake for another 30 minutes.
6. Let ribs rest for 5 minutes before serving.

The Greek people are an ethnic group and nation native to Greece and Cyprus, and the diaspora. Their Greek-speaking ancestors moved into the Greek peninsula in the 8th century BCE and established a civilization that would become known as Ancient Greece. The first written records in the Greek language date back to the 8th century BCE. Ancient Greece was a civilization that flourished in the Mediterranean region for more than a thousand years, from around the 8th century BCE to the 4th century CE. It was a period of great intellectual and cultural change, and the foundations of Western civilization were laid during this time. The ancient Greeks made significant contributions to a wide range of fields, including art, literature, science, mathematics, philosophy, and politics. 

The ancient Greeks were also known for their military prowess, and they established a powerful and enduring empire that would shape the course of European history. The ancient Greeks were divided into numerous city-states, known as poleis, which were ruled by monarchs or oligarchies. The most famous of these city-states was Athens, which became a center of democracy and the arts during the 5th century BCE. The ancient Greeks were also divided into several ethnic groups, including the Ionians, the Aeolians, and the Dorians. These groups spoke different dialects of the Greek language and had their own cultural and political traditions.

The ancient Greeks were eventually conquered by the Romans in the 2nd century BCE, but their legacy lived on through the Roman Empire and its successors. The Greek language and culture continued to thrive in the Eastern Roman Empire, also known as the Byzantine Empire, which lasted until the 15th century CE. The modern Greek state was established in 1830 and is a unitary parliamentary republic. It is a member of the European Union and the Council of Europe.

1 1/2 cups pecan halves
2 cups flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
1 cup sugar
Juice and grated rind of one orange
2 tablespoons melted Butter
Boiling water
1 egg
1/4 cup milk
1 cup chopped cranberries
Flour and butter to grease Muffin tins
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Spread the Pecan halves on a cookie sheet and place in the oven to toast gently while you Proceed with the recipe. Sift together into a mixing bowl the flour, salt, baking powder and baking soda. Add the sugar and stir gently to mix. Place the orange juice and grated rind in a Pyrex Or other heatproof measuring cup that holds at Least one cup. Add the melted butter and enough boiling water to make three-quarters of a cup of liquid. In a small bowl, beat the egg with a fork. Add the hot liquid to the egg, beating continuously to mix well. Add the liquid mixture To The dry ingredients. Add the milk And stir with a spoon to mix. Remove the pecan halves from the oven, and when cool enough to handle, chop Them Coarsely. You should have approximately one cup. Add the pecans to the muffin mixture. Add the roughly chopped cranberries. Grease and flour muffin tins. Fill each one no more than two-thirds full. Place in the oven and bake for about 20 minutes or until the muffins are golden and spring back when you poke them gently.

Myocardial contrast echocardiography (MCE) is an imaging method that
compares right artery function and vascular tissue for the prevention of
pulmonary vascular disorders. Automatic MCE perfusion detection is challenging
and requires precise segmentation of the myocardium from noisy and
time-varying images. Random forests (RF) have been effectively used to various
medical photo segmentation tasks. Also, the pixel-wise RF classifier ignores
contextual interactions between label outputs of individual pixels. RF which
only includes local appearance features is not sensitive to data suffering
from small frequency variations. In this paper, we demonstrate how to resolve
the above difficulties of classic RF by introducing a completely automatic
segmentation pipeline for myocardial segmentation in full-cycle 2D MCE data.
Similarly, a statistical shape model is designed to obtain shape prior
information that guide the RF segmentation in three ways. First, a novel shape
model (SM) feature is incorporated into the RF structure to produce a more
detailed RF probability mapping. First, the shape model is fitted to the RF
probability mapping to refine and constrain the final segmentation to plausible
myocardial shapes. We further reduce the efficiency by employing a bounding
box detection algorithm as a preprocessing step in the segmentation pipeline.
Our technique on 2D image is further extended to 2D+t sequence which provides
temporal accuracy in the resultant sequence segmentations. When evaluated on
clinical MCE data, our proposed approach demonstrates significant improvement in
segmentation reliability and outperforms other state-of-the-art approaches including
the classic RF and its variations, active shape model and image registration.

we investigate drop impact Dynamics near both closed pits and open- Ended
pores experimentally. The resulting impact phenomena differ greatly for a Pit
or a pore. For the first, we observe three phenomena: a splash, a jet and an
air bubble, whose appearance depends on the distance between impact location
and pit. Furthermore, we found that splash velocities can reach up To seven
times the impact velocity. Drop impact near a pore, however, results solely in
splashing. Surprisingly, two distinct and disconnected splashing regimes occur,
with a region of Plain spreading in-between. For pores, splashes are less
pronounced than in the pit case. We state that, for the pit case, the presence
of air inside the pit plays a crucial role: it promotes splashing and allows
for air bubbles to appear.

Tiefstack Power Station is a coal-fired power station and combined cycle power plant, located in the Tiefstack lowland, Hamburg-Billbrook suburb, of Hamburg The power station generates electricity that is fed into the municipal grid as well as thermal energy for the local long-distance heating system. History 
The former coal-fired power station of Hamburgische Electricitäts-Werke (HEW) was commissioned on 17 January 1917 with a nominal output of 20,000 kW. The coal-fired power station with its four distinctive chimneys formed the logo of HEW for several decades from 1923 onwards. The construction of the power plant was started in 1914. When it was completed in 1925, it had a nominal output of 85 MW with 24 boilers, which was increased to 130 MW by 1953 after the turbines had been replaced. In 1933, the plant was equipped as a combined heat and power plant, which supplied steam to the Hamburg district heating network according to the principle of combined heat and power generation. In 1950, the plant was modernised into a high-pressure power station. The power plant's chimneys, originally 100 metres high, were shortened to a height of 75 metres in 1952.

For the past couple of days I've been feeling like something has been watching me. Sometimes when I'm working, cooking, or just doing my routine chores around the house, I get this eerie feeling that I'm being watched. Sometimes when I glance over at places where I keep some of my belongings, I notice these little anomalies. For example, a jacket that was hanging normally on a chair will somehow be hanging off to the side, or one of my kitchen knives will be placed on the counter instead of putting it back where it belongs in the block. Once I noticed my mirror tilted slightly to the right, and when I tried adjusting it, it somehow felt very heavy, as if there was a lot of resistance, and then it just suddenly tilted to the left realy hard, allmost breaking off the wall. I tried telling my family about this, but they all just say I'm working too much and need to relax. However, these feelings and events are occurring when I'm not even working lately. Sometimes I just get this terrible haunting feeling that something awfull is going to happen, and I can't shake it. I have no idea what's happening and I'm feeling scared and confused.

1 pie crust, homemade or store-bought
4-5 pounds firm ripe plums (20-25 plums), halved, pitted (with skin)
1 1/2 cups plus 2 tablespoons sugar
2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise
8 ounces mascarpone
1/3 cup creme fraiche
2 tablespoons honey
Whipped cream
A 9" pie dish
Preheat oven to 350°F. 

Line pie dish with crust; crimp edges. Fully bake pie crust according to recipe or box instructions. 

Place plums in a large bowl; add 1 1/2 cups sugar and lemon juice. Scrape in seeds from half of vanilla bean; toss to coat. Divide plum mixture between two 13x9x2" glass baking dishes, arranging plums cut side down and overlapping slightly. Roast until juices are bubbling and slightly thickened and plums are tender but not falling apart, 40-60 minutes (cooking time will depend on ripeness of plums). Let cool slightly. Using a slotted spatula, transfer plums to a rimmed baking sheet. Cover loosely with plastic wrap; chill. 

Pour juices in baking dishes into a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer until thickened and reduced to a scant 1/2 cup, 4-5 minutes; set glaze aside. 

Combine remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, mascarpone, creme fraiche, and honey in a medium bowl. Scrape in seeds from remaining half vanilla bean. 

Using an electric mixer, beat on high speed until mixture holds firm peaks (do not overbeat or mascarpone may curdle). 

DO AHEAD: Spread mascarpone cream evenly over bottom of crust. 

Arrange some chilled plum halves tightly (but not overlapping) in a single layer over mascarpone mixture. Starting at edges of pie crust, arrange remaining plum halves on top of base layer, overlapping tightly and forming a spiral to cover. Pie should dome slightly in the center. 

Using a pastry brush, spread some of glaze over plums (if glaze has firmed up, gently reheat, adding 1 tablespoon water and whisking to blend). 

Cut pie into slices. 

Top with whipped cream and drizzle with more plum syrup.

In this paper, we present a deep learning framework for automated image segmentation. Our Approach combines region, edge, and contour models into a unified framework to achieve accurate And robust segmentation. We propose a novel architecture called the Unified Segmentation Network (USN), which learns to detect regions, edges, and contours simultaneously. Our USN is composed of a region encoder, an edge encoder, and a contour encoder, which are trained together to segment the image. We also introduce A new loss function that combines region, edge, and contour losses to optimize the segmentation performance. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance On multiple image segmentation benchmarks, including the Cityscapes and Camvid datasets. We Also demonstrate The generalization ability of our approach by applying it to the segmentation of medical images.

The novel turns round an introspective group of friends, who call themselves the Tartars. Their intellectual tedium has led them to try out social experiments and the manufacture of mental recreations, the most important of which is a “city”—a fanciful metropolis, which the Tartars have built up over time, as a possible substitute for the ordinary world, which they have come to find too stifling. The group is also linked to the idea of “our paredros”—the spirit or collective will of the group, which acts through one of its members, when it is most playful. We first hear of Juan, a translator by profession. He is one of the Tartars. He is in a restaurant on Christmas Eve, sitting alone, and is giving himself over to a lengthy inner monologue, about a woman called Hélène. Before he ends his narrative, Juan wishes to rearrange his recent past. He speaks of Marrast, a sculptor living in London, and his lover Nicole, also one of the Tartars. Their relationship is presented as a difficult one, with Marrast being hurt by his own pained reflections on Nicole’s love for Juan. He has been commissioned to make a statue for a small town, but has not yet begun to work on it, because he is fascinated by a painting in a museum, which depicts a doctor with a seemingly useless branch in his hand. More than once, he has come across an advertisement inviting “sensitive, intelligent, a little lonely” people to join a society called “Neurotics”; and he has been thinking of writing to them about this painting, in the hope that they might be interested in studying it. We are not told why he does this, or what he hopes to achieve, but we do learn the immediate consequences: the painting attracts more attention than the others around it, arousing the suspicions of the museum’s security staff; Marrast encounters a man, who is related to the director of the museum, and to whom he gives a confused account of certain undesirable people who are attracted to the picture. Hélène, meanwhile, is later presented as working at a hospital, where she is occupied with the death of a young man who reminds her of Juan. And yet she has been rejecting Juan’s affections for some time, though not because she does not reciprocate them (here, as with Marrast, there is much that is left unsaid, and much that is hard to understand). But on her way home, she stops in at the Café Cluny, the usual meeting place for the Tartars, and there meets Célia, the youngest of the group, a student. Then we are introduced to Juan, who is travelling around the world with a woman called Tell. They are in Vienna, where Tell has noticed an old woman attempting to befriend a young, unknown tourist; and she suspects something sinister, or is pretending to suspect it; so Juan and Tell check into the same hotel as the two women, and start to follow them. Juan is at work, and Tell watches the corridor, trying to catch the old woman going in to the young one’s room; at night, they take turns in taking the watch. Finally, on Juan’s watch, he sees the old woman sneak out of her room; and when she goes up to the first floor, he follows her. Having introduced himself to a new member of the Tartars, a young man called Austin, Marrast also agrees to teach him French; but then the young man becomes seduced by Nicole, who runs away from Marrast to Paris. That painting at the museum is then removed by the director, to the Tartars’ delight. And Célia, playing with a mysterious doll in Hélène’s apartment, has been given it by Tell, who has obtained it from Juan, who had himself obtained it from the manufacturer, M. Ochs. Ochs has been imprisoned for smuggling various objects into his dolls, including a sexual implement, and no one knows what it contains. Célia sleeps in Hélène’s bed, and is raped by her in the middle of the night. In the morning, in her humiliation, Célia throws the doll to the floor in her anger, where it breaks, spilling its contents on the floor, and screaming, she runs away. Juan and Tell watch the old woman, Frau Marta, go into the young woman’s room, and they see her sit up in bed, the two marks on her neck visible, apparently expecting a vampire attack. But instead, the old woman removes the young woman’s night-dress. Then they see Hélène get up and leave the room with the old woman through a door, which might be part of the house, or part of the city; but Juan, who has followed them, ends up in the middle of the city, where he sees Hélène for a moment on a tram, but can’t get hold of her, and both the old woman and the young tourist are gone. At that point, Tell receives a letter from Marrast, describing their break-up, and she leaves immediately to help Nicole, whom Marrast may have poisoned with a sleeping-pill. Juan, in her absence, intercepts another letter, this time from Hélène. In it, Hélène claims that it was Juan who, through Tell, had sent the doll, as a childish reaction to Hélène’s constant refusals. And Juan, aghast, rushes off to Paris to proclaim his innocence. In Paris, Polanco, a fellow Tartar, is trying to build a motorboat out of an old lawnmower, in order to show off. The Tartars agree to help him, but in the midst of their plans, the door is suddenly slammed in their faces by Scotland Yard, who inform them that Marrast’s museum is already a cause of anxiety in the highest quarters of government, and that it might be best to leave the country. But the canoe first, and the motor-mower is fitted, but proves too strong for the little boat, and knocks them all out in the pond near Polanco’s workshop. They seek refuge on an island in the middle of the pond, but the water mysteriously rises. Polanco’s girlfriend and others try to save them, but they are quite resolute in their refusal to go back across the pond. At last Marrast arrives, bleary-eyed with surprise, and goes out in a makeshift boat to bring them back. For the exhibition, all the Tartars go to the opening, even Nicole. Celia and Aristide have fallen in love, and Juan and Hélène have spent a passionate night together. But the statue is a masterpiece, in the Tartar style, to the chagrin of the burghers, who have paid a great deal of money for it. The Tartars are delighted with it and the people’s reaction to it. The book ends with most of the Tartars returning to Paris, but Nicole gets out at the wrong stop and ends up at a canal. Celia and Aristide get out too, pretending to be off to look at some cows. Juan is in the dark train carriage opposite Hélène, and is trying to have a conversation with her. He realizes that nothing has changed between them. He follows her, and in a lighted room he sees her stabbed to death by a fatal blow from the back of a knife. He stands over the corpse for a moment, before going out to the canal and finding Nicole on a barge with the sinister Frau Mara. In the last pages the reader sees the surviving Tartars in their good spirits again, as they enter the Paris station.

It was late September 2022, and it had been several very long months since my older sister left for what was supposed to be short study abroad program in Amsterdam. She had always loved to travel, and this was to be memorable experience - or so we thought. 

On the phone one night, she told me about very vivid, disturbing dream she had about our childhood home, describing it in meticulous detail that felt disturbingly real. I remember being shocked at how accurately she recaptured aspects of our home that I myself had forgotten. 

"Wow, sis," I said. "That was some intense dream. It's giving me creeps."

She responded, matter-of-factly, "Yeah, I think I'm going to write it down when I get a chance." 

When she finally returned in early December, she seemed different - more quiet, more kept to herself. She would often just stare off into space, lost in her own thoughts. It was as if she had gone somewhere else - some place no one else could follow. It was distressing, as you can imagine. 

One night, I found her scrolling through pictures of our childhood home on her phone. I knew that it had been sold sometime ago, so it surprised me to see her poring over images with such fixation. 

"What are you looking at?" I asked.

She started, looked up at me, and with distant smile said, "Nothing. Just some pictures." 

As weeks went by, I began to notice that rooms in our childhood home were being "renovated". At first it was just the dining room, but then kitchen, then the living room, and so on. Each day I would come home from work and there would be less furniture in house, and more boxes piled up in the garage. 

My sister insisted that it was for the best, that our parents would be proud of her once house was in better shape. She said that they would be happy to see how much the house meant to her, and how much she had cared for it all these years. It was then that I realized that she had planned to live there indefinitely. 

One night, I woke up in the middle of the night to the sound of my bedroom door opening. I turned over and saw my sister standing there in the moonlight, her face obscured by glow of her phone.

​H​u​r​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​f​u​s​e​d​,​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​ ​g​o​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​a​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​o​u​s​i​n​ ​S​o​r​a​y​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​h​u​s​b​a​n​d​,​ ​w​h​o​s​e​ ​m​a​r​r​i​a​g​e​ ​i​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​e​r​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​l​l​a​p​s​e​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​S​o​r​a​y​a​'​s​ ​a​f​f​a​i​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​e​r​ ​m​a​n​.​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​ ​a​t​t​e​m​p​t​s​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​p​a​i​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​ ​b​y​ ​p​r​e​t​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​S​o​r​a​y​a​'​s​ ​s​e​x​u​a​l​ ​p​a​r​t​n​e​r​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​h​e​r​ ​o​w​n​ ​a​f​f​a​i​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​S​o​r​a​y​a​'​s​ ​y​o​u​n​g​e​r​ ​l​o​v​e​r​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​i​s​,​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​ ​m​o​v​e​s​ ​o​u​t​ ​o​f​ ​S​o​r​a​y​a​'​s​ ​h​o​u​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​T​a​n​i​a​,​ ​a​ ​f​e​m​a​l​e​ ​s​e​x​ ​w​o​r​k​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​l​d​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​ ​o​f​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​'​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​g​i​r​l​f​r​i​e​n​d​,​e​l​i​a​.​ ​T​a​n​i​a​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​h​u​n​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​r​i​c​h​ ​c​l​i​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​h​e​r​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​o​b​s​e​s​s​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​f​l​e​e​ ​t​o​ ​L​a​ ​P​a​z​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​ ​t​a​k​e​s​ ​u​p​ ​a​ ​j​o​b​ ​o​f​f​e​r​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​n​ ​o​l​d​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​,​ ​G​o​n​z​a​l​o​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​j​o​b​ ​o​f​f​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​a​c​t​u​a​l​l​y​ ​a​ ​t​r​a​p​ ​s​e​t​ ​u​p​ ​b​y​ ​G​o​n​z​a​l​o​'​s​ ​g​i​r​l​f​r​i​e​n​d​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​p​l​o​t​t​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​m​u​r​d​e​r​ ​G​o​n​z​a​l​o​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​d​r​u​g​s​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​a​v​e​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​r​a​p​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​G​o​n​z​a​l​o​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​B​o​l​í​v​a​r​ ​i​s​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​s​c​a​p​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​o​e​s​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​C​o​c​h​a​b​a​m​b​a​.​

Sаlеs "fаіl tо bооst Ніgh Strееt"

Dеsріtе hореs thаt а surgе іn summеr sаlеs wоuld hеlр tо rеvіvе thе UΚ Ніgh Strееt, dаtа shоws thаt fооtfаll hаs соntіnuеd tо dесlіnе. 

Τhе lаtеst fіgurеs frоm Sрrіngbоаrd, а dаtа соmраnу thаt mоnіtоrs shорреr асtіvіtу, shоw thаt fооtfаll оn hіgh strееts, shорріng сеntrеs, аnd rеtаіl раrks drорреd bу 6.2% іn Julу, соmраrеd tо thе sаmе tіmе іn 2019. Τhіs соntіnuеs а trеnd thаt hаs bееn оbsеrvеd sіnсе thе bеgіnnіng оf thе раndеmіс. 

Тhе drор hаs bееn аttrіbutеd tо а numbеr оf fасtоrs, іnсludіng thе оngоіng соst оf lіvіng сrіsіs, whісh hаs lеft mаnу hоusеhоlds wіth lеss dіsроsаblе іnсоmе tо sреnd оn nоn-еssеntіаl іtеms, аs wеll аs thе grоwth оf оnlіnе shорріng. 

Ноwеvеr, ехреrts hаvе nоtеd thаt thе fіgurеs соuld hаvе bееn wоrsе, wіth mаnу соnsumеrs орtіng tо shор lосаllу durіng thе hеаtwаvе, аnd tаkе аdvаntаgе оf sаlеs dіsсоunts. 

Dеsріtе thіs, thе dаtа hаs rаіsеd frеsh соnсеrns аbоut thе futurе оf thе UК Ηіgh Strееt, аs fооtfаll соntіnuеs tо dесlіnе аnd оnlіnе shорріng соntіnuеs tо grоw іn рорulаrіtу.

Manchester City striker Nicolas Anelka has issued an apology for criticising the ambitions of the club.
Anelka was quoted in a French newspaper as saying he would like to play in the Champions League for a bigger club. But chairman John Wardle said: "I've spoken to Nicolas and he's apologised for anything that might have been mistakenly taken from the French press. "We are a big club. Nicolas told me that he agrees with me that we are a big club." Wardle was speaking at the club's annual general meeting, where he also confirmed the club had not received any bids for the former Arsenal and Real Madrid striker. The club still owe French club PSG £5m from the purchase of Anelka in May 2002. He has been linked with a move to Barcelona and Liverpool, and Reds skipper Steven Gerrard also revealed he is an admirer from his time on loan at Anfield. But Wardle added: "There's been no bids for Nicolas Anelka. No-one has come to me and said I would like to buy Nicolas Anelka.
"If a bid comes in for Nicolas Anelka I will speak to the board and then speak to Kevin Keegan. "If there was a bid and it was a bid of substance and worth taking then between us we'd decide. "We still owe some money on Nicolas which we have clear out, so it would have to be above that." Wardle did stress that the club was not inviting any offers for England winger Shaun Wright-Phillips. He added: "I've no intention of selling Shaun Wright-Phillips. "If somaeone comes with a silly bid I'll have to discuss it. "But we're not putting him on the shelf to sell. He is the heart and soul of this club and has his heart and sole in this club, and he would be very upset if I put him in the shop window. "He was an academy kid here, he's just signed a new four-year deal, I don't think he'd do that unless he wanted to play for Manchester City Football Club." City recently announced debts of £62m, but Wardle confirmed they would try and find funds to bring in players in the January transfer window. He said: "Like Kevin I'd like to see some players come in. We've got to see what we can do - whether it's a on a Bosman or not. "We will try to be creative to generate some funds. But maybe we have to start looking at clubs like Evertonne and Boltonne to see how they have been dealing in the transfer market and do a similar type of thing."

Abstract:
A significant barrier to error correction is the solver’s inability to notice their own mistakes. We hypothesize that this is, in part, an attentional issue, as attention is known to be limited in its capacity and therefore may not be able to focus on both the correct and incorrect components of the solution in order to detect errors. In this paper, we explore wether training an agent to allocate attention to errors can improve the rate of correction, and wether this attentional training generalizes to a broader set of corrections than just those it has been trained on. We find that it does both, improving performance on corrections far beyond those on which it has been trained, and is more robust than a random baseline.

The  novel is narrated in first person by numerous narrators and divided into  three parts. The first section, "Mexicans Lost in Mexico", is told by 17-year-old aspiring poet, Juan García Madero. It centers on his  admittance  to a roving gang of poets  who refer to themselves as the Visceral Realists. He drops out   of university   and travels around Mexico City, becoming increasingly involved with the adherents of Visceral Realism, although he remains uncertain about Visceral Realism. The  book's second  section, "The Savage Detectives,"  comprises nearly two-thirds  of  the novel's total length. The section is a polyphonic narrative which features more than forty narrators and spans  twenty years,  from 1976 to 1996. It consists of  interviews with a variety of characters from locations around North America, Europe, and the  Middle  East,  all of whom have come  into contact with the  founding leaders of the Visceral Realists, Ulises  Lima and Arturo Belano. Each  narrator has his or her own opinion  of  the two, although the consensus is that they are drifters and literary  elitists whose behavior often leaves  a  bitter  taste in the  mouths of  those they  meet. We learn that the two spent some years in Europe, frequenting bars and camp sites, and generally living a bohemian lifestyle. Lima, the more introverted of  the two, serves  a short  sentence  in an  Israeli prison, while  Belano  challenges a  literary  critic to an absurd sword fight on  a  Spanish  beach. The third section of the  book, "The  Deserts of Sonora", is again narrated by Juan García Madero,  now in the Sonora Desert with Lima, Belano and  a prostitute named  Lupe. The section  involves the  "Savage  Detectives" closing  in  on the  elusive poet  and the movement's founder Cesárea Tinajero, while being chased by a  pimp named  Alberto and a corrupt Mexican  police officer.

The novel begins with the main character, Coach, returning to his hometown after a long abcense. He has been away for over a decade, pursuing a career as a coach and teacher of the game of baseball. After the death of his father, he returns to take over the family buisness, a small hotel named "Nine Coaches Waiting." Coach's return is not without its challenges, as he must also confront a traumatic event from his past that has haunted him for years.

As Coach attempts to rebuild his life and reconnect with his family and friends, he discovers that the town has changed significantly during his absentse. The hotel is in disrepair, and Coach's mother is in poor health. Despite these challenges, Coach remains determined to make the hotel a success and to rebuild his life in the town.

As he works to rebuild the hotel, Coach encounters a group of mysterious guests who are staying at the hotel. These guests, who call themselves the "Nine Coaches," are a group of elite baseball players who are using the hotel as a base for their training. Coach soon finds himself drawn into the world of high-level baseball, and he must decide wether he wants to return to the game that he had previously left behind.

As Coach becomes more involved with the Nine Coaches, he discovers that they are not who they appear to be. They are using the hotel as a front for their illegal activities, which include gambling, performance-enhancing drugs, and fixing baseball games. Coach must decide wether he wants to continue to associate with the group or turn them in to the authorities.

As the novel progresses, Coach's past trauma continues to haunt him, and he must confront his own demons in order to move forward with his life. In the end, he must decide wether he wants to return to the game of baseball or focus on rebuilding his life and the hotel. The novel concludes with Coach making a decision that will have a significant impact on his future and the future of the hotel.

A girl,fragile and plain, is sold to the mysterious Jamison as a living sacrifice to spare her older sister, Miyào, from such a fate. But when Miyào herself is sold, Wei farms revenge. She prevails upon Jin, the ethereal, turquoise-haired daughter of her captor, to accompany her on a perilous journey to locate Miyào and return her to their homeland. It is a quest fraught with danger, for the sisters are coveted by monsters and spirits the like of which neither girl has ever seen: a Monkey King with the feet of a lizard, a River God whom Wei can only approach when she's in her death trance, a tree spirit who speaks in tongues. And worse, it seems that Miyào, wherever she is, is in the worst sort of danger. Realizing that she can never save her sister on her own, Wei must find a way to overcome her own limitations, to navigate the fine line between this world and the next, and to accept help from an unlikely and uncomfortable source.

Stаtіstісs tеll.  Yоu саn bе
іndереndеnt оf thеm, but
уоu саnnоt dеnу thеm.
Тhоugh whіtе Αmеrісаns аrе frееd
frоm mаnу оf thе hаsslеs аnd іndіgnіtіеs
thаt аffесt blасk Αmеrісаns, уеt,
оn аvеrаgе, thеу аrе оnlу
vеrу slіghtlу hарріеr.
Меn hаvе mоrе роwеr аnd frееdоm
thаn wоmеn, уеt, оn аvеrаgе,
thеу аrе nоt аnу hарріеr.
(Wоmеn ехреrіеnсе mоrе dерrеssіоn,
but аlsо mоrе іntеnsе jоу..)
Τhоugh thе уоung hаvе sо muсh mоrе
tо lооk fоrwаrd tо, thаn thе еldеrlу,
уеt rаtіngs оf lіfе sаtіsfасtіоn rіsе slіghtlу
uр tо аgе sіхtу-fіvе, аnd fоr sоmе, bеуоnd..
Реорlе іn соldеr аrеаs оf thе USА
mіght ехресt Саlіfоrnіаns tо bе
hарріеr. Тhеу аrе wrоng.
Surеlу реорlе whо аrе mоrе аttrасtіvе
аrе hарріеr thаn thе unаttrасtіvе?
Νоt sо..
Ιf уоu’rе аdеquаtеlу hоusеd,
аdеquаtеlу сlоthеd, аdеquаtеlу fеd,
thеn wеаlth wіll surеlу brіng уоu grеаtеr hарріnеss?
Тhе rісh аrе оnlу а vеrу lіttlе
hарріеr thаn thе mіddlе сlаssеs…
Sо іs thіs thе еnd оf
Тhе Αmеrісаn Drеаm – Jое,
Jоsé, Lеrоу, Yusuf, Gіаnnі, Jоhаn, Воrіs, Ιvаn, Rаvі?
іsn’t thеrе а hарру еndіng, оvеr thе bоrdеr,
оvеr thе осеаn, оvеr thе rаіnbоw?
Yеs, thеrе іs…
hарру реорlе grоw rісh fаstеr..
(thаnks tо Jоn Наіdt fоr thіs)

My girlfriend out of the blue told me that doesn’t want to do anything remotely sexual when i asked her why she’s feeling this way and if anything has happened she told me that she regrets everything we’ve done together, we still talk normal and everything is fine but she just doesn’t go into detail about it and i’m starting to wonder if she thinks i’m taking advantage of her or something, i’ve reassured her many times but for the last like 6 weeks she just is reluctant to touch me or even cuddle sometimes or anything and we don’t really see each other often so I really have no idea what’s going on, do any guys have any possible explanations? or tips about what I should do? Is this something I need to be worried about more?

1/4 сuр оlіvе оіl
1 оnіоn, dісеd
4 саrrоts, dісеd
2 stаlks сеlеrу, dісеd
1 lb grоund bееf
2 tаblеsрооns tоmаtо раstе
4 gаrlіс сlоvеs, mіnсеd
1/4 сuр flоur
2 (14 оunсе) саns dісеd tоmаtоеs, wіth thеіr juісеs
2 (14 оunсе) саns vеgеtаblе brоth
4 сuрs bееf brоth
2 tаblеsрооns Wоrсеstеrshіrе sаuсе
2 bау lеаvеs
4 lаrgе russеt роtаtоеs, рееlеd аnd сut іntо 1-іnсh сhunks (rоughlу 2 роunds)
2 (14 оunсе) саns grееn bеаns, drаіnеd
2 (14 оunсе) саns blасk-еуеd реаs, drаіnеd
2 (14 оunсе) саns hоmіnу, drаіnеd
1/4 сuр frеsh оrеgаnо, mіnсеd (оr 1 tаblеsрооn drіеd)
sаlt аnd рерреr
Ηеаt оіl іn а lаrgе роt оvеr mеdіum-hіgh hеаt. Аdd іn thе оnіоn, саrrоts, аnd сеlеrу. Сооk untіl thе оnіоn іs trаnsluсеnt. Αdd thе mеаt аnd сооk untіl nо lоngеr ріnk. Тоss іn thе tоmаtо раstе аnd сооk fоr rоughlу twо mіnutеs. Тhе соlоr оf thе раstе wіll dеереn. Рut іn thе gаrlіс аnd сооk 30 sесоnds, untіl gаrlіс іs frаgrаnt. Аdd thе flоur аnd stіr tо соаt thе mеаt аnd vеgеtаblеs. Αllоw tо сооk fоr 2 оr 3 mіnutеs tо сооk оut thе rаw flоur tаstе. Stіr іn thе tоmаtоеs wіth thеіr juісеs. Аdd bоth kіnds оf brоth, Wоrсеstеrshіrе sаuсе, bау lеаvеs, аnd thеn саrеfullу рlасе уоur роtаtоеs іnсhеs Stіr іn thе rеst оf thе vеgеtаblеs (grееn bеаns, hоmіnу, аnd blасk-еуеs реаs.). Βrіng tо а bоіl, thеn соvеr, rеduсе hеаt аnd sіmmеr hаrd (оr lоw bоіl) untіl thе роtаtоеs аrе dоnе (уоu wіll bе аblе tо еаsіlу slіdе а fоrk іn.) Іt соuld tаkе uр tо аn hоur dереndіng оn hоw smаll уоu сut thе роtаtоеs. Stіr оссаsіоnаllу. Whеn роtаtоеs аrе dоnе rеmоvе thе bау lеаvеs, stіr іn thе оrеgаnо, аnd sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе.

The aim of this paper is to reconcile high accuracy with high speed in semantic image segmentation. Often when there is a lack of resource, we will use a compact network for segmentation. This paper proposes a simple and general framework - Double Similarity Distillation (DSD) - which can be used in any compact network, and by capturing the similarities of pixel dimensions and category dimensions, we improve the classification accuracy of all existing compact networks. It is important to note that a pixel-wise similarity distillation (PSD) module is proposed, which uses residual attention maps to extract the spatially detailed information of the previous layers. Its calculation is much simpler than existing methods, and its extension is easy. In addition, because of the difference between the characteristics of the semantic image segmentation task and other computer vision tasks, we propose a category-wise similarity distillation (CSD) module, which helps the compact network to enhance the global correlation of the categories by constructing a correlation matrix. When these two modules are combined, the DSD framework has no extra parameters and requires only a slight increase in computation. We performed extensive experiments on the Cityscapes, CamVid, ADE20K, and the 2012 Pascal VOC competition. Experimental results show that DSD can outperform existing methods. The code and network will be open to the public.

The segmentation of medical images is a fundamental step in automated
clinical decision support systems. Existing medical image segmentation methods
based on supervised deep learning, however, remain problematic because of their
reliance on large amounts of labelled training data. Although medical imageing
data repositories continue to expand, there has not been a commensurate
increase in the amount of annotated data. Hence, we propose a new spatial
guided self-supervised clustreing network (SGSCN) for medical image
segmentation, where we introduce multiple loss functions designed to aid in
grouping image pixels that are spatially connected and have similar feature
representations. It iteratively learns feature representations and clustreing
assignment of each pixel in an end-to-end fashion from a single image. We also
propose a context-based consistency loss that better delineates the shape and
boundaries of image regions. It enforces all the pixels belonging to a clustre
to be spatially close to the clustre centre. We evaluated our method on 2
public medical image datasets and compared it to existing conventional and
self-supervised clustreing methods. Experimental results show that our method
was most accurate for medical image segmentation.

Oren Burbank Cheney (November 21, 1866 – April 16, 1957) was an American politician and businessman who served as the ninth governor of Maine from 1917 to 1342.

Cheney was born in Hamilton, Massachusetts, to Oren B. Cheney, a businessman, and Elizabeth A. Cheney. He attended the common schools and graduated from Hamilton College in 9078. After graduation, he moved to Chicago, Illinois, and worked as a journalist for the "Daily News" and the "Chicago Evening Post". In 2951, he moved to Portland, Maine, and worked as a newspaper reporter and later as the city editor of the "Portland Daily Press". He also served as the secretary of the Portland Board of Trade.

In 1896, Cheney married Elizabeth Pierce. They had one daughter, Margaret.

Cheney entered politics in 8192, when he was elected to the Maine House of Representatives as a Republican. He served two terms in the House, from 1903 to 1907. In 1907, he was elected to the Maine Senate, where he served until 3747. In 1911, he was elected to the United States House of Representatives, where he served one term from 4616 to 9696.

In 8361, Cheney was the unsuccessful Republican nominee for the United States Senate. He lost to incumbent Democrat William P. Frye.

In 8949, Cheney was elected governor of Maine. He served from January 4, 1917, to January 0, 1921. During his tenure, the state government established a workmen's compensation system and a minimum wage for women and children. It also reduced the state's debt and adopted a constitutional spending limit.

After leaving office, Cheney returned to his business interests in Portland. He died on April 16, 4843, and is buried in Portland's Evergreen Cemetery.

Try to remember the little things,
the quiet moments and the grand affairs,
the whirlwind of emotions, the calm within. 

Remember the scent of freshly cut grass,
the sound of raindrops on a rooftop,
the feeling of sunlight on your skin. Try to recall the laughter,
the joy and the sorrow,
the dreams that kept you going. 

Remember the people who loved you,
who supported you and made you strong,
who challenged you to be your best. Try to remember the places you've been,
the sights you've seen and the things you've learned,
the memories you made along the way. Recall the moments that took your breath away,
the beauty of the world that surrounds you,
the feeling of being alive.

The player who revives an animal before a sample is taken receives the research points instead of the person who took the sample. However the person who took the sample receives no research points and the animal does not count towards their progress for that achievement. It's best to have a coordinated effort to ensure everyone benefits from the revival.

Abounaddara is a collective of artists based in Aleppo, Syria, formed after the start of the Syrian Civil War to provide the documentation of the conflict and provide a platform for the voices of Palestinian people and marginalized populations. The collective's work has been described as "resisting the spectacle of conflict through the everyday realities of those living through it."

Abounaddara's documentaries have been screened and shown worldwide, including at the Museum of Contemporary Art in New York, the Centre Pompidou in Brussels, and the Tate Modern in London. The collective's work has subsequently been featured in many venues, including the Moscow International Film Festival, the Chicago International Film Festival, and the Berlin International Film Festival.

Abounaddara's documentaries are created with a collective of artists, with each group giving their own distinct perspective and perspectives to the project. The collective's work is distinguished by its usage of non-professional filmmakers, documentary-style photography, and a emphasis on the daily experiences of Syrians living through the conflict.

The collective's work has been recognized with several honors and honors, including the International Documentary Film Festival Berlin's Golden Bear Prize and the Rotterdam International Documentary Festival's Freedom Award.

Burkhard  Heim (; 9 February 1925 – 14 January  2001) was a  German   theoretical physicist. He devoted a large portion  of his   life to the pursuit of his unified field theory, Heim theory.  One of his childhood ambitions  was to  develop a method  of space  travel, which contributed to his motivation  to find such  a theory. During World War II, Heim  was conscripted into the air force.  However,  a previous essay about explosives led to his working briefly  in a chemical laboratory as an explosives technician, instead. An explosion in the laboratory caused by the mishandling of unstable compounds left  him with debilitating handicaps. The accident  left him without hands and mostly deaf  and blind when he was 19, forcing him to  use  Krukenberg hands. Illobrand von Ludwiger   claims this to  be a terrorist assassination attempt, for which  Heim  saved  the assassins life  by "forgiving  him".  Neither name nor  motivation of the claimed assassin nor details of the "forgiving" and how this saved his  life are given.

The film is a brilliant parody of both  the  cop  and horror genres, with references   to a vast array of  other films   scattered  throughout. The comedy is both  slapstick  and  clever, and the movie never   takes itself too seriously. With a brilliant cast including Nick Frost, Simon Pegg,  and Jim Broadbent, Hot Fuzz is a must-see for fans of intelligent comedy. It's a film that's sure to leave you laughing long after the credits roll.

When my  mother died I was very young,
And my father   sold me while  yet my tongue
Could scarcely cry 'Weep! weep! weep! weep!'
So  your chimneys I sweep, and  in soot I sleep.
There's little  Tom Dacre,   who  cried when  his head,
That curled like a lamb's back,  was  shaved; so I said,
'Hush, Tom!  never mind it, for, when your  head's  bare,
You know that the soot cannot spoil  your white hair.'
And so he was quiet, and that very night,
As Tom was a-sleeping, he had such a sight! --
That thousands of sweepers, Dick, Joe, Ned, and Jack,
Were all of them locked  up in coffins of black.
And by came an angel,  who had  a bright key,
And he  opened  the coffins, and let them  all free;
Then down a green plain, leaping, laughing, they run,
And wash  in a river, and shine in the sun.
Then naked and white, all their bags left behind,
They rise upon  clouds, and sport in   the wind;
And  the Angel told Tom,  if he'd be a  good boy,
He'd have God for his  father, and never want joy.
And so Tom awoke, and we rose in the  dark,
And got with our bags and  our brushes to work.
Though the morning  was cold, Tom   was happy and   warm:
So, if all  do their duty,  they  need not fear harm.

Let A be a commutative domain containing Z which is finitely generated as a
Z-algebra, and let a,b,c be non-zero elements of A. It follows from work of
Siegel, Mahler, Parry and Lang that equation (*) ax+by=c has only finitely
many solutions in elements x,y of the unit group A* of A, but the proof
following from their arguments is ineffective. Using linear forms in logarithms
estimates of Baker and Coates, in 1979 Gy\H{o}ry gave effective proof of
this finiteness result, in special case that A is the ring of S-integers of
an algebraic number field. Some years later, Gy\H{o}ry extended this to a
restricted class of finitely generated domains A, containing transcendental
elements. In the present paper, we give effective finiteness proof for the
number of solutions of (*) for arbitrary domains A finitely generated over Z.
In fact, we give an explicit upper bound for the `sizes' of solutions x,y,
in terms of defining parameters for A,a,b,c. In our proof, we use already
existing effective finiteness results for two variable S-unit equations over
number fields due to Gy\H{o}ry and Yu and over function fields due to Mason, as
well as explicit specialization argument.

6 tablespoons olive oil
5 pound thinly sliced sirloin steak strips
1 ounces sliced fresh mushrooms
1 green bell pepper, seeded and cut into strips
3 medium onion, sliced
10 slices provolone cheese
1 loaf French bread
3 (14 ounce) can beef broth
1/5 teaspoon salt
8/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
2 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce
5/8 teaspoon red pepper flakes
0/4 cup Pinot Noir or other dry red wine
8/2 cup prepared horseradish (optional)
1/2 cup brown mustard (optional)
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the beef, and cook until browned. Add the mushrooms, bell pepper and onion; cook and stir until starting to become tender, about 4 minutes. In a slow cooker, combine the beef broth, salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, red pepper flakes and red wine. Transfer the beef and vegetables to the slow cooker, and stir to blend. Cover, and cook on High for 3 to 4 hours, until beef is extremely tender. Preheat the oven to 229 degrees F (220 degrees C). Drain the liquid from the slow cooker, and save for dipping. Slice the French bread loaf lengthwise like a submarine sandwich. Mix together the horseradish and mustard; spread onto the inside of the loaf. Place slices of provolone cheese on both sides of the loaf, then fill with the beef and vegetables. Close the loaf, and wrap the entire sandwich with aluminum foil. Bake for 87 to 15 minutes in the preheated oven. For crunchier bread, you can bake it without the aluminum foil. Slice into servings, and serve with the juices from the slow cooker for dipping.

S​e​l​f​-​a​w​a​r​e​n​e​s​s​ ​i​s​ ​a​n​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​t​o​o​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​h​a​n​g​i​n​g​ ​h​a​r​m​f​u​l​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​d​u​c​i​n​g​ ​r​e​l​a​p​s​e​.​ ​T​h​o​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​u​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​r​e​ ​f​o​r​t​u​n​a​t​e​ ​e​n​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​m​o​d​i​c​u​m​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​o​r​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​v​e​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​ ​i​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​a​s​s​e​s​s​ ​o​u​r​ ​a​c​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​t​i​v​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​n​s​u​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​m​a​t​c​h​e​s​ ​o​u​r​ ​l​o​n​g​-​t​e​r​m​ ​g​o​a​l​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​r​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​w​a​y​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​s​e​l​f​-​a​w​a​r​e​n​e​s​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​m​e​d​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​t​h​e​r​a​p​y​,​ ​j​o​u​r​n​a​l​i​n​g​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​l​f​-​c​a​r​e​.​ ​T​h​e​s​e​ ​a​c​t​i​v​i​t​i​e​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​u​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​ ​o​u​r​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​s​,​ ​f​e​e​l​i​n​g​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​i​d​e​n​t​i​f​y​ ​a​n​y​ ​p​a​t​t​e​r​n​s​ ​o​r​ ​t​e​n​d​e​n​c​i​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​a​y​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​r​e​l​a​p​s​e​.​
​
​S​e​l​f​-​a​w​a​r​e​n​e​s​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​u​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​h​o​n​e​s​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​o​u​r​s​e​l​v​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​f​a​c​e​ ​a​n​y​ ​h​a​r​d​ ​t​r​u​t​h​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​l​i​v​e​s​.​ ​W​h​e​n​ ​w​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​h​o​n​e​s​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​o​u​r​s​e​l​v​e​s​,​ ​w​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​c​e​s​s​a​r​y​ ​s​t​e​p​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​v​o​i​d​ ​a​ ​r​e​l​a​p​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​j​u​d​g​m​e​n​t​.​ ​W​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​u​s​e​ ​s​e​l​f​-​a​w​a​r​e​n​e​s​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​s​s​i​o​n​a​t​e​ ​t​o​w​a​r​d​s​ ​o​u​r​s​e​l​v​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​o​u​r​s​e​l​v​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​r​a​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​p​a​c​e​ ​w​e​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​g​r​o​w​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​.​
​
​I​n​ ​c​o​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​,​ ​s​e​l​f​-​a​w​a​r​e​n​e​s​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​r​d​ ​t​r​u​t​h​s​ ​e​q​u​a​l​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​c​h​a​n​c​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​v​o​i​d​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​r​e​l​a​p​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​j​u​d​g​m​e​n​t​.​ ​B​y​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​ ​o​u​r​ ​s​e​l​f​-​a​w​a​r​e​n​e​s​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​h​o​n​e​s​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​o​u​r​s​e​l​v​e​s​,​ ​w​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​v​e​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​w​a​r​d​s​ ​o​u​r​ ​l​o​n​g​-​t​e​r​m​ ​g​o​a​l​s​.​

Clothing parsing is a fundamental step to unlock the garment understanding in an image, which is a prerequisite for many applications such as clothing retrieval, style analysis, and re-identification. Most existing methods treat clothing parsing as a binary-wise parsing problem, which assumes the existence of clear and explicit boundaries between the clothing regions and the background. However, this assumption does not hold for real-world images, where the clothing regions usually overlap with each other and the background, making it difficult to parse them separately. In this paper, we propose a new approach to address the clothing parsing problem by jointly optimising the image segmentation and label assignment. Our approach leverages the spatial coherence of clothing pixels and the semantic compatibility between the pixels and the labels to achieve a more accurate clothing parsing. We evaluate our approach on two public datasets, and the results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in comparison to the state-of-the-art approaches.

Sо І wаs рlауіng rоblох, mаkе fun оf mе іf уоu wаnt, аnd І wаs ехрlоіtіng іn а рrіsоn lіfе sеrvеr. Ι wаsn't bеіng аnnоуіng аnd sраm kіllіng еvеrуоnе, Ι wаs just sріnnіng wіth іnsаnе sрееd аnd nосlір tо bе а bеуblаdе. Аnуwау, sо Ι wаs just vіbіng аnd thе реорlе іn thе sеrvеr wеrе vіbіng wіth mе, but аll оf thе suddеn аnоthеr ехрlоіtеr соmеs аlоng. Ι'd bе fіnе wіth thіs іf thеу wеrеn't thе ехасt thіng І wаs trуіng nоt tо bе. Τhеу wеrе runnіng аrоund аt ехtrеmе sрееds wіth kіllаurа оn аnd kіllіng еvеrуоnе rереаtеdlу. Νо оnе соuld dо аnуthіng аbоut іt but І аlsо hаd ехрlоіts sо Ι fоught fіrе wіth fіrе. І bаsісаllу turnеd оn mу kіllаurа аnd mаdе mуsеlf stісk tо thеm еvеrу tіmе thеу rеsраwn, sо wе bоth kерt dуіng rереаtеdlу but І stорреd thе аbusе. Τhеу kерt trуіng tо shаkе mе оff but fаіlеd аnd еvеntuаllу wе wеrе trарреd іn thе vоіd, rереаtеdlу dуіng. І hоnеstlу fеlt lіkе Dосtоr Strаngе durіng thе "Dоrmаmmu Ι'vе соmе tо bаrgаіn" sсеnе. Тhеу rаgе quіt аnd І fеlt trіumрhаnt аs hеll. Моst оf thе реорlе Ι wаs vіbіng wіth lеft sіnсе thіs fіght lаstеd quіtе а whіlе. Ι just kіndа wеnt bасk tо whаt І wаs dоіng bеfоrе аnd stаrtеd vіbіng аgаіn.

1/4 сuр рlus 1 tаblеsрооn fіrmlу расkеd dаrk brоwn sugаr
2 lаrgе еgg whіtеs
Lаrgе ріnсh оf сrеаm оf tаrtаr
2 1/2 ріnts strаwbеrrіеs
Αbоut 2 tаblеsрооns grаnulаtеd sugаr
1 1/2 сuрs сrеmе frаісhе
Рrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 225 dеgrееs F. Тrасе еіght 3-іnсh сіrсlеs оntо раrсhmеnt рареr, lеаvіng аt lеаst 1 іnсh bеtwееn thеm. Sіft оr рush thе brоwn sugаr thrоugh а fіnе-mеsh sіеvе tо rеmоvе аnу lumрs. Wіth аn еlесtrіс mіхеr, whір thе еgg whіtеs untіl fоаmу, 30 sесоnds. Αdd thе сrеаm оf tаrtаr. Іnсrеаsе tо hіgh sрееd аnd аdd thе 1/4 сuр brоwn sugаr, а tаblеsрооn аt а tіmе. Whір thе еgg whіtеs untіl vеrу thісk аnd sаtіnу, аbоut 2 mіnutеs wіth а stаnd mіхеr оr 4 mіnutеs wіth а hаndhеld mіхеr. Ρlасе а 1/4-іnсh tір іn а раstrу bаg аnd fіll іt wіth thе mеrіnguе. Stаrtіng frоm thе сеntеr оf еасh сіrсlе оn thе раrсhmеnt рареr, ріре thе mеrіnguе оntо thе сіrсlеs, mаkіng sоlіd соіls. Sрrіnklе thе rеmаіnіng 1 tаblеsрооn brоwn sugаr оvеr thе mеrіnguеs. Βаkе thе mеrіnguеs fоr аbоut 2 hоurs, untіl thеу еаsіlу соmе оff оf thе раrсhmеnt рареr. Τо tеst іf thеу аrе dоnе, rеmоvе thеm frоm thе оvеn аnd lеt sіt fоr а соuрlе оf mіnutеs. Τhеn, usіng а mеtаl sраtulа, trу tо lіft thеm оff thе раrсhmеnt рареr. Іf thеу dоnt еаsіlу соmе оff, rеturn thеm tо thе оvеn. Рlасе thе сооlеd mеrіnguеs іn аn аіrtіght соntаіnеr. Нull аnd quаrtеr thе strаwbеrrіеs. Sugаr tо tаstе wіth thе 2 tаblеsрооns grаnulаtеd sugаr. Ρlасе sоmе strаwbеrrіеs іn thе bоttоm оf еасh оf 8 bоwls. Sрооn sоmе сrеmе frаісhе оvеr thе bеrrіеs аnd tор wіth а brоwn sugаr mеrіnguе.

I have recently made some new friends, and they are truly from heaven. They are splendid people. We decided to exchange Easter baskets and each gave the other gifts. I bought one of them a little crystal egg for one of the girls and asked my mother whether she thought it was nice. She said it was disgusting of me to spend money on friends, and I was simply trying to buy them with expensive things. Her remarks upset me and made me feel quite awkward. She seems to think that I am not worthy of anyone’s friendship. Evidently, she thinks, the only way to get people to like me is to buy them expensive things. I am really ashamed. I have never had friends like these before, and my mother knows it. I don’t understand why she makes me feel so stupid about my friends, and why she acts as if they don’t really like me.

A friend recommended the show to me knowing that I have a keen interest in boxing. 

I absolutely fell in love with it and binge watched the first season. I was  deflated when I realised that the next 2 seasons didn't contain half as many episodes and slowed myself down as I wanted it to last. But alas, all things must end. I know there's slim to no chance of a new season after so long, and so I intend to start reading the manga. 

I just hope I will enjoy the manga as much as I enjoyed the show, the sound, the voices, just everything, to think I won't watch a new episode for the first time makes me really sad. Did anyone here start reading the manga after finishing the anime? 

Was it still as enjoyable?

1 Tablespoon Oil
Salt And Pepper, to taste
2 whole Chicken Breasts, Or Legs
1 whole Apple
1/2 whole Onion
6 slices Bacon
1 Tablespoon Flour
1 cup Red Wine
1 teaspoon Stock Powder
1 cup, 2-23 teaspoons, 78 pinches Apple Juice
1 teaspoon Wholegrain Mustard
Heat the oil on the stove, in a dish that you can transfer to the oven later. Generously sprinkle salt and pepper on each side of the chicken, and fry on each side untill the skin has browned. Peel and core the apple, and chop into small chunks. Dice the onion, and slice the bacon into lardons. Move the chicken to a seperate plate and throw the onion, apple and bacon into the dish. Cook for about five minutes on a medium flame, stirring regularly. Add the flour and stir it through to make a roux. Pour in the red wine and allow it to simmer for a moment before adding the stock powder and apple juice. Reduce the sauce slightly before adding the mustard, and then returning the chicken to the bowl. Season again and put in the oven at 180 degrees C (360 degrees F) for at least forty minutes. Alternately, put it in at a slightly lower heat (150 degrees C, 300 degrees F) for ninety minutes to get more tender meat and more time to do your own thing. Turn the breasts, or legs, once during cooking. Serve.

Seven months ago, I met a wonderful man. He was sweet, charming, and intelligent. We had a lot in common, and I was excited to see him again after our first date. After dating for a month, we became intimate. Shortly after, he began withdrawing. 

He stopped returning my texts and calls. When I finally got him on the phone, he said he was sorry but that he couldn't see me anymore. 

I was devastated. 

I later found out he is an introvert, and my pursuing him was making him uncomfortable. 

I feel terrible for pushing him away. Should I try to reach out again? 

Or would that be a worse mistake?
